title: Engineer - Process (Design)
description: Responsibilities: Preparation of process scheme/process flow diagram/FEED and process description Familiar with material and heat balance calculations and thermal rating of heat exchangers Development of process data sheets and able to perform NPSH/hydraulic calculation and equipment and line sizing Participate in Hazop Study and develop the P&ID Prepare material selection diagram and establish requirements for any chemicals, solvents and catalyst Perform relief valve sizing, control valve sizing and popping philosophy Prepare the equipment and line list and review the various equipment and instrument data sheets Interface/coordinate with other discipline for design development Requirements: Degree in Chemical Engineering Preferred 3 years of design experience for process system in the oil and gas, chemicals storage and process environment. Candidate without relevant experience but with right aptitude and interest are welcome to apply. Familiar with respective code of practices for process design and fire protection such as API, NFPA, SS532, SS634 etc.

title: Senior Production Engineer
description: Job Descriptions: Process Optimisation: To ensure efficient operation of all plant and equipment with Production team. To participate and coordinate within Production, with discussions with other Departments involved, ensure process and system adherence is complied according to the required specifications. Production Coordination: Responsible for the coordination works for the day-to-day operations of all Production lines and other departments. This includes daily operational aspects of job planning, manpower arrangements, chemical processing, plant equipment and chemical bath maintenance. Production Line Upkeep: Be the main point of contact for production supervisors and operators to ensure chemical baths, chemical tanks and other critical equipment are properly maintained within specification and functioning efficiently. Collaborate with technical team to troubleshoot and resolve issues. Quality Control: Ensure documentation and procedures are updated and maintained within the Production lines. Assist in troubleshooting works in relation to production adherence to the related documentation and procedures. Continuous Improvement: To actively pursue and improve the efficiency of existing processes and procedures, meet technical specifications, seek continuous improvements, and to sustain the company’s internal capacity in order to meet output targets. Safety Compliance: To ensure safety regulations and company policies are adhered to within the Production lines. This includes participation in safety meetings and upholding safety standards to maintain a safe working environment in accordance to industry standards. Housekeeping: To coordinate with supervisors to ensure good housekeeping (5S), safe working condition, quality of work and proper handling of chemicals and waste disposal of production lines. Others: Senior Production Engineer may be delegated with additional duties by the reporting heads. However, such delegation does not relieve his or her overall work responsibilities as stated above. Requirements: Diploma or Degree in Engineering or related disciplines. Preferably in relation to Chemistry or Chemical Engineering. Preferred Experience: 3 - 6 years in Aerospace industry in supervisory positions relating to operations. 3 - 6 years of chemical processing experience including but not limited to anodizing, electroplating, chemical cleaning, etc.

title: Process Safety Engineer (Operations I Process Engineering)
description: Well Established Company Basic $4000 - $4800 + AWS + Variable Bonus Excellent Welfare and Benefits + Career progression Working location : Tuas (Transport Provided) Working days : Monday to Friday Working hours : 8am to 5pm Job Responsibilities: Develop technical studies towards the implementation of processes in the plant, evaluating plant capacity, process conditions, and other variables which can affect the process. Champion a culture of Process Safety excellence at the company based on the PSM 14 elements. Develops the Process Engineering Package (P&ID, PFD specification of process equipment, specification of electrical  process equipment, material balances, energy balances, and DCS control logics) for the plant processes. Reviewing Risk Assessment Screenings and Involve in Management of change procedure and be a team player. Involve in design safety review procedure for all processes and be a team player. Manage and conduct the PHA - HAZOP program, as well as track, assist and follow up with the resulting recommendations. Assist with data collection for Quantitative Assessment (QRA) tools. Organizing and delivering effective and efficient Process Safety studies (PSI, MOC, Mechanical integrity, Operating procedures & etc. for all plant processes as needed for stewardship of processes. Develop Pre Start-up Safety Review (PSSR) for plant processes and follow up with the design review. Perform risk analysis and coach others on the use of risk analysis techniques. Collaborate with the teams to discover and implement long-term process improvement efforts. Perform audit of process safety management systems and execute risk mitigation corrective actions. Assisting Incident Investigation teams in investigating and classifying process safety incidents, as well as      providing support in the development of process tools and action plans to close identified Process Safety gaps. Utilizes process simulation software to test and find  the most appropriate PSM strategies. Other ad-hoc duties as assigned Requirements: Minimum of Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering and/or Equivalent. Minimum of 2 – 5 years of engineering experience supporting Operations, Engineering and/or Projects, such as plant or process engineering, project safety design, execution, engineering and/or technical service roles. Candidates are encouraged to apply this position via Apply Now button with the following information in the resume Work experiences and job responsibilities Current and Expected salary Reason for leaving Date of availability Education background We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. title: Chemical Process Engineer (West/ Japan MNC)
description: Job Summary This role responsible for improving production efficiency, quality, and productivity. This includes analyzing current processes, identifying improvement opportunities, and implementing changes. The role involves working closely with different teams to solve problems, ensure smooth operations, and support safety and regulatory compliance. The engineer also plays a key role in new product development and scale-up, helping to ensure a smooth transition from development to full-scale production. Responsibilities: Review and identify gaps in current processes, recommend improvements to HQ, and lead the implementation of approved changes. Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure smooth adoption of new or updated processes. Proactively identify and address potential issues before they lead to incidents. Ensure plant operations meet design specifications. Lead risk assessments and FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis) activities. Follow the Plan-Do-Check-Act cycle to drive continuous improvement. Conduct regular inspections and team briefings. Act as a trainer to engineers and senior engineers, sharing knowledge on new or updated processes. Train technicians on process changes and support the engineering team in achieving high proficiency. Requirements: Degree in Chemical Engineering or a related field. Minimum 3 years of relevant work experience. Strong planning, organization, and prioritization skills. Proficient in Microsoft Office (intermediate level). Skilled in data analysis, quality tools, and problem-solving. Effective written and verbal communication skills; able to engage with all levels of the organization. Able to read and understand P&IDs (Piping & Instrumentation Diagrams). Basic understanding of chemical process equipment and operations. Familiar with ISO standards: ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, and 50001. Experience in internal auditing. Knowledge of risk assessment frameworks and ability to conduct assessments. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Well-versed in chemical handling procedures. Capable of writing clear and structured reports. Leadership qualities with the ability to lead and motivate others. Systematic thinker with a structured approach to tasks. Makes decisions based on sound technical and engineering judgment. Results-oriented and self-motivated. Follows management systems with diligence. Strong awareness of safety practices. Honest, dependable, and acts with integrity. Independent and proactive; takes initiative and ownership without needing supervision. Persistent in achieving goals and completing tasks. **Working Hours: 8am-4.45pm (Mon-Fri) **Company Transport provided. **AWS, Bonuses, Allowances Wecruit Pte Ltd | 20C0270 Tew Jie Wei | R22106822

title: Chemical Plant Operator (East)
description: Role Overview: Responsible for operating and maintaining chemical processing and support equipment, ensuring smooth production workflows, safety compliance, and quality control in a pilot-scale manufacturing environment. Core Duties: Conduct material testing using instruments such as TGA analyzers and tensile strength testers. Perform preventive maintenance on mechanical components like pumps, valves, mixers, and tanks. Manage chemical receiving, unloading, and storage in line with standard operating procedures. Support fibre processing and wetlaid operations, including batch documentation and traceability. Qualifications & Skills: Diploma/Higher Nitec/Nitec in Chemical Process Technology, or equivalent certifications (e.g., WSQ in Chemical Operations and WSH). Hands-on experience with lab and plant equipment including mixers, pumps, and valves.

title: Process R&D Engineer
description: Roles and Responsibilities: Build and iterate bench‑top demonstration units that integrate multiple unit operations. You’ll prototype mechanical designs, implement controls & in‑line measurements, close mass/energy balances , and generate P&IDs and vendor packages. Key Responsibilities Lead experimental process design; integrate unit operations into a bench‑top demo. Prototype unit operations and implement design iterations (strong hands‑on mechanical skills). Optimise operations & controls; implement practical reliability improvements. Establish/test measurement strategies (in‑line sensors, data historians); close M&E balances . Perform equipment sizing (tanks, mixers, reactors, separators/columns, pumps, valves). Develop equipment/instrument lists, specs, and supplier options; coordinate with SMEs on materials   selection. Prepare P&IDs , detailed specs, draft cost estimates, and project schedules. Write operating procedures (start‑up, shutdown, normal & emergency), and sampling plans. Qualifications Degree in Chemical/Mechanical Engineering (or related). 3–7+ years in process development, prototyping, and pilot/demonstrator build. Proficient with CAD, instrumentation basics, and technical documentation.

title: Senior E&A Engineer
description: Position Snapshot Location: Tuas, Singapore Company: Wyeth Nutritionals, Singapore Type of role: Permanent Joining Nestlé means you are joining the largest Food and Beverage Company in the world. At our very core, we are a human environment – passionate people driven by the purpose of enhancing the quality of life and contributing to a healthier future. A Nestle career empowers you to make an impact locally and globally, as you are provided with the opportunity to make a mark and stand out, as long as you seek it. With Nestle, you are enabled and encouraged to grow not only as professionals, but also as people.' As a Senior E&A Engineer, you will be responsible to provide support for Manufacturing Execution Systems (MES), Electricity and Automation (E&A) professional and technical operational activities and participation in CAPEX projects; doing so in line with corporate guidelines, enforcing that all MES-E&A activities enhance business objectives, product quality and plant operation/performance while ensuring that people health and safety, product safety, and environment are never compromised. A day in the life of a Senior E&A Engineer... Excel in Industrialisation: Participates in Capital Investments projects in the factory ensuring that the technical solution follows corporate and market standards and is delivered with the expected quality, being on time and in budget. Responsibilities include: the preparation of the user and technical specifications, coordinating the requirements of the main users (production, quality, etc.), the follow up of the solution development, the pre-test, implementation and training of operators and maintenance crews. Must fully understand the technical details of the MES-E&A solutions in all projects. Product and Process Mastership: MES-E&A engineers play a key role in the continuous improvement of our factories through mastering the process and product characteristics. The MES-E&A engineer must have a deep insight of the processes, and continuously strive for the improvement of quality, safety or efficiency while protecting the technological know how and competitive advantage. Manage existing assets: Ensure Process and Equipment Safety for people, products and environment and that one exhibits an attitude of absolute intolerance for unsafe situations. Participates in the implementation of engineering tools at site level (i.e. NSAT, NEST, AMM, etc.), and in the M&I activities in the factory ensuring realibility of the assets. Functional Guidance: Execute the strategy of the market in terms of MES, Electricity and Automation in alignment with corporate standards best engineering practices. What will make you successful: Minimum 5 years Automation and/or MES engineer with Electrical experience Personnel Supervision (of technical teams, contractors or other 3rd parties) Participation in the development of engineering projects as part of a multidisciplinary team (preferably including different products and technologies in Electrical and Automation areas) Participation in the start-up of technical equipment(s) and/or production line(s) Self Management following defined KPIs/Objectives, and proactively tracking them Exposure to TPM, preventive maintenance planning and/or maintenance execution desired but not required

title: Manager/ Senior Manager, Process and Quality Control (Fab Operations) 3 Years Renewable Contract
description:
Job Description: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. This role is critical for ensuring world-class product yield, reducing defectivity, and maintaining strict compliance with customer specifications, internal quality standards, and relevant industry certifications. Key Responsibilities: Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical
     process modules. Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Fault
     Detection and Classification (FDC) , and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps. Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in
     integration run sheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability. Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield. Serve as the technical expert and primary quality interface for key customers, leading customer audits, addressing quality-related concerns, and driving customer-specific quality improvement plans. Problem Solving: Direct and oversee high-level, cross-functional root-cause analysis (RCA) and corrective and preventive action (CAPA) teams for major process excursions, non-conformances, and field returns, utilizing methodologies like 8D or Six Sigma Metrology & Inspection: Oversee the selection, qualification, and maintenance of all in-line and off-line metrology and defect inspection tools, ensuring measurement system accuracy (Gage R&R) and high data quality. Process Window Definition: Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met. Data Analytics: Leverage large-scale manufacturing data (Big Data) to identify subtle process drifts, predict
     failures, and proactively implement controls. Lead, mentor, and manage a team of Quality Engineers, Process Control Engineers, and Technicians, setting
     performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making. Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime. Required Qualifications: Degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Chemical Engineering, or a related technical field. Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment. Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules. Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Design of Experiments (DOE) , Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) , and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred). Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. title: Senior Chemical Technician – 2 years contract
description:
Senior Chemical Technician (WDA) – 2 years contract Based in Singapore, the regional hub for Asia Pacific (AP) and top-rankedbiopharmaceutical company on The Straits Times and Statista’s list of Best Employers in Singapore for two consecutive years (2020, 2021). Join the premier biopharmaceutical company that has been in Singapore for more than 25 years and in AP for over 60 years. Our Manufacturing Operations teams are the people that make our products. We work in the manufacturing plants with a “Safety First, Quality Always” mindset striving for continuous improvement. We work in the local plant connected to our global manufacturing network to ensure the highest quality of raw materials, intermediates and finished products. WHAT YOU WILL DO The Chemical Technicians will operate and monitor the chemical production activities in the plant, ensure that processes are run according to process description, complete batch documentation accurately and thoroughly, while adhering to the required quality and safety standards, and in a productive, cost-effective manner.  They are also required to meet production targets/schedules while maintaining satisfactory standards of Good Manufacturing Practice, safety, and housekeeping. Operate and monitor the chemical processes assigned: Each of these processes may comprise the following activities: Participate in an appropriate hand-over of the process(es) on which he / she is working. Obtain, follow and complete process related documentation. Check prior shifts' documentation for accuracy and thoroughness. Clean vessels and lines prior to and following batches as required. Conduct all required pre-batch checks. Charge vessels with solids or liquids as directed in procedures. Conduct post-charge verification checks and take any necessary corrective action. Adjust and monitor (using all information available, including automation input/outputs and personal inspection) process conditions as required. Carry out routine sampling, testing some samples as necessary. Transfer process streams to other units in coordination with other staff. Monitor process; both with Distributed Control System (DCS) and manually observing temperature, pressure, flow, PH, volume, colour, salt content, moisture content and adjusting the parameters accordingly. Perform Batch / Offloading operations. Operate general facility service equipment such as vacuum pumps, water systems, scrubbers. Record all operational and analytical data on the appropriate documents and inform the Shift Production Lead of any process abnormalities. Put on appropriate clothing and personal protective equipment for the process and the processing area. Maintain material segregation and label appropriately and accurately all containers, bags and vessels. Carry out routine checks and scheduled cleaning procedures, for both process and facilities as required. Obtain and verify materials necessary for processing, monitor materials stock and notify Shift Production Lead as necessary in the event of a material or supply abnormality. Notify Shift Production Lead of requirement for maintenance. Prepare equipment before maintenance work e.g. depressurizing, cleaning of lines and equipment, de-energizing etc. Conduct on-the-job training of other Chemical Technicians. Perform material handling duties including forklift/reach-truck operation driving. Carry out assignments for alternate or simultaneous processes as directed. Meet eligibility requirements for the Emergency Response Team. Attend meetings and training events as required. Maintain all qualifications and development milestones as necessary for duties. Lead/participate in troubleshooting and resolving problems that arise during the processing. Lead/Participate in pre-commissioning and chemical commissioning activities as required under the direction of the Senior Manager, Operations and Shift Production Lead. Lead/Participate in SHE, Business Compliance, cGMP and all other compliance-related matters, where applicable. Lead a section of Chemical Technicians to meet production targets/schedules while maintaining satisfactory standards of Good Manufacturing Practice, safety, and housekeeping. Adhere to and comply with the company and departmental procedures, health & safety rules and regulations, and comply with current Good Manufacturing Practice and Good Housekeeping at all times. Supervise and oversee Third Party Contractors, where applicable. Any other duties as and when assigned by the Production Lead. WHAT YOU MUST HAVE To be successful in this role, you will have: Higher NITEC or Diploma in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Electrical) or Science, or equivalent operations experience. Good stewardship awareness (including cGMP, regulatory inspection preparation, SHE)· Good knowledge in applicable regulations and divisional policies/guidelines (especially cleaning/housekeeping/maintenance, environmental control, process/cleaning/equipment validation, change control and manufacturing practices). Demonstrate Ethics & Integrity & Result Driven Focus on Customers & Patients & Make Rapid Disciplined Decision Act with Courage & Candor, Build Talent & Foster Collaboration Demonstrate Our Company's Inclusive Behaviors & Effective communication skills Willing to work 12 hours rotating shift (day/night) This is a career conversion program under Workforce Singapore (WSG) and candidates must meet the following criteria and are open to
the following conditions: Singaporeans/Singapore PRs only PMETs/Mid-Careerist with no prior work experience in Biopharma industry are welcomed to apply Open to two years contract WHAT YOU CAN EXPECT Limitless opportunities across various areas in Manufacturing; well-structured career path A state-of-the-art facility that delivers solution to its customers world-wide Highly engaging team that aims to innovate the future Our Manufacturing & Supply Division is committed to be the most trusted supplier of biopharmaceuticals worldwide. Our facilities, along with our external contractors, suppliers, and partners, create an interdependent global manufacturing network that’s committed to delivering a high quality, reliable supply to customers and patients on time, every time. We are proud to be a company that embraces the value of bringing diverse, talented, and committed people together. The fastest way to breakthrough innovation is when diverse ideas come together in an inclusive environment. We encourage our colleagues to respectfully challenge one another’s thinking and approach problems collectively. We are an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse
workplace.

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/

description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. title: Control Room Operator | FPSO | 2 Years
description: Job Description: Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Overall Job Purpose Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Core Responsibilities Control and carry out process plant operation and production-related equipment via FCS and other control panels, under the direction of Production Supervisor and with the support from Operators' team, including operational routines, monitoring, controlling and operational optimization of the process plant, sampling and chemical analyses, start/stop and isolations of equipment, commissioning/decommissioning and operational alignments/maneuvers, in order to ensure that the production systems of the platform are operated according to the project and its operating philosophy, thus optimizing production performance and chemical consumption. Contribute to the unit's annual budget planning (expenses and investments), by participating of the corporate planning, providing information about resources, replacements, services and materials needs and other expenses used in its activities, liaising with Supervisors and Technicians to ensure accurate information. Contribute to an effective response to emergencies on board by being part of the Emergency Response Team, participating in all trainings, simulations and acting properly in a real situation, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, guidelines, as well as national and international regulations and best practices. Support the offshore and onshore leaderships in the interface and contact with the client, authorities and internal stakeholders., by providing faithful information regarding operation's and control room routine and indicators, when requested by Production Supervisor. Contribute to the MODEC’s Permit to Work management systems' implementation, by acting as Permit user of PTW for the task performed, in accordance with MODEC's procedures. Contribute to the continuous improvement of fleet performance by participating on, when requested, RCAs, investigations of NOIs/NODs, HAZID/HAZOPs, following up or acting upon CAPAs, sharing lessons learned and promoting a culture of continuous seek for performance and integrity enhancement opportunities. Act proactively to safeguarding MODEC from noncompliance to legal/regulatory requirements by following MODEC's procedures, management systems, client guidelines, internal and external regulations, national and international standards, participating in audits and inspections in the unit, developing and ensuring the implementation of action plans when required. Guarantee the proper use of Technical Support departments, by ensuring that technical queries are properly identified, recorded in the system, and always directed to Onshore departments for review/study, promoting a culture adherent to the company's systems. Contribute to enhance MODEC's procedures, by constantly evaluating the department activities and seeking improvement opportunities, sharing lessons learned with Technical Support departments and Operations leadership, participating in procedures' review, following MODEC's Philosophy, manufacturer recommendation, and personnel experience. Ensure the proper communication regarding the department activities developed onboard, by assisting in the preparation of the OIM Daily Report, as well as periodic and on demand reports for the client, authorities, or leadership. Communicate to the Production Supt all production or safety systems' emergency, by providing accurate and relevant information immediately, request technical advice as necessary and prepare, follow up and close out any necessary vendor assistance on board for the Production department, in liaison with relevant leaderships. Contribute to the operational performance by inhibiting and overriding controller, when required, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, and properly registering all occurrences in the production and safety / emergency systems. Assume the responsibilities of the Production Supervisor whenever designated by the OIM or Production Superintendent, complying with all training, procedures and responsibilities relevant to the function. Contribute with the quality of Production works performed by core crew or vendors, by providing them with adequate and suitable information, as directed by the Production Supervisor. Contribute to the proper response to emergencies and shutdowns, understanding and applying PSD, ESD and C&E (cause & effects diagram) diagrams. HSSE Requirements and Code of Conduct HSSE: Act in compliance with the corporate HSSE Management System Policy, the MODEC Life Saving Rules, and the policies, guides, process standards and work instructions available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Act to promote the preservation of the environment, the health and occupational safety (personnel and their team), the safety of processes, and the preservation of assets in their scale of responsibility. Ethics and Compliance: In compliance with the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics, as well as with MODEC Group Ethics and Compliance Policies and Local Procedures, their respective forms, online and live mandatory trainings is committed to comply with all applicable laws, regulations and obligations and act in accordance with the highest ethical standards and with MODEC values. Emergency Plan: As per indication on the Training Matrix, Emergency Management Guide and Offshore Emergency Management, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Financial and Operational Dimensions / Impact and Decision-making Authority Complies with Authorization Matrix Procedure, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Work Relationships As per indication on the Organizational Chart, provided by the company. Job Challenges and Problem Solving Monitor all production indicators while guiding the Operators' team in the field maneuvers, due to high level of simultaneous attention that both activities require. Work in shifts due to continuous operation and constant personnel presence required, being necessary to invert the shifts of work during the offshore period. Qualifications & Experience Minimum 5 years' experience is expected; Previous experience in Offshore/Onshore Oil & Gas area is expected. Previous experience as Production Operator in F(P)SO units is required. Previous experience in Production Control Rooms is required. Previous experience and knowledge of hydrocarbon processing and utility systems are expected. Key Behaviors: Complies with the Job Competence Model, provided by the T&D&P team. Languages: Fluent English is mandatory. Software: Microsoft Office suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word) Working Conditions Complies as a highest importance and priority with safety, security, protection of environment and occupational health conditions for the job position.

title: Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning
description: School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences (SPMS) Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning Young and research-intensive, Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. The College of Science seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit on the basis of merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status, family responsibilities, or disability. The School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences at NTU offers multidisciplinary academic programs that provide students with a wide-ranging and up-to-date education. They offer undergraduate training leading to BSc (Hons} and graduate training leading to MSc and PhD degrees. We invite applications for an Assistant Professor / Associate Professor position specialising in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning . We are looking for world-class candidates with expertise in dynamical systems, numerical analysis, stochastic analysis, and with experience in applying these domains to recurrent neural networks, state-space systems, reservoir computing, learning of dynamic processes, and mathematical learning theory in general. The successful candidate will be expected to teach both undergraduate and postgraduate courses in pure and applied mathematics, conduct high-quality research in the areas of dynamical systems, random dynamical systems, state-space systems, and their applications to machine learning theory and time series forecasting, and promote interdisciplinary collaboration among the disciplines of mathematics, physics, computer science, and engineering that push the development of reliable dynamical machine learning methods for applications. Candidates with strong academic portfolios are encouraged to apply. The successful candidate should actively seek collaborations with research groups beyond his/her research field and leverage on the multidisciplinary ecosystem of NTU. The candidates are expected to: · Establish a world-class research programme. · Enhance the College’s visibility as an international leader in education and research. · Secure research funding from competitive grants and industry partnerships. · Mentor and supervise graduate students and postdoctoral researchers. · Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams within the university and with external partners. · Deliver engaging and effective teaching to both undergraduate and graduate students. · Undertake essential administrative responsibilities within the Schools. Requirements: · PhD in mathematics or a related subject. · Strong research record and relevant publications in reputable peer-reviewed international journals and conferences. · A good track record of supervising research staff, postdocs, students, and interns in relevant fields · Experienced in cross-disciplinary research initiatives and collaboration. · Demonstrated excellence in research, evidenced by a strong publication record in reputable journals · Recognition in the research community, such as invited talks, awards, or memberships in professional societies. · Proven ability to secure research funding. Application Procedure Required application documents: · Cover letter. · Curriculum Vitae, including a full publication list. · Statement of current and future research interest. · Teaching statement. · Names of at least three referees. Please click here to apply.

title: Safety Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on supporting and reporting to the Singapore Refinery Operational Safety Manager in the area of operational safety, workplace safety, Emergency and Crisis Management to deliver full compliance with Corporate Safety, local and regulatory safety requirements. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, refinery engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore and the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your operational safety knowledge to our Refinery on matters of operational safety, such as Safety Leadership cultural shift, critical field controls & verifications, Contractor Safety, Workplace Safety & Health Committee, Fire Safety Management, Emergency Response and Crisis management. In our Safety team, you will be required to support the Safety Manager and safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your safety knowledge, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Safety Engineer Drive and execute operational safety, workplace safety, emergency response and crisis management Ensure refinery legal right to operate safely is delivered Support refinery safety manager in strategic and tactical safety planning, implementation and execution of safety programs Operational Safety Support and participate in refinery operational risk assessments, safety critical job and deviations reviews Support corporate and refinery implementation of creating safety together to strengthen refinery safety cultural shift Ensure the executions of critical safety risk controls, monitoring, trending, verifying and communicated for learnings & improvement Workplace Safety Together with refinery key stakeholders, implement contractors and suppliers safety management Support the Turnaround Safety Lead and team in turnaround planning, preparations executions and lesson learnt Support Workplace Safety & Health Committee initiatives and programs Support and execute site fire safety management Incident Management Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Prepare, maintain emergency response plan and execute training of company emergency response team to mitigate incidents, emergencies and compliance to National CERT Audits and Standard Support preparation of refinery crisis leader and crisis management team training and dive into the role and responsibility of crisis management team support during crisis Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 6 years of safety working experience in the oil, gas or petrochemical industry Experienced to support and operationalise workplace safety, fire safety, emergency response and crisis management Good understanding of local WSH regulations and have experience dealing with local regulators such as MHD, MOM, SPF, SCDF, etc. Good communication, writing and analytical skill Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in Safety highly desired Registered  WSHO and FSM, Noise Monitoring Officer added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application. We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. title: Senior Scientist, Biotransformation and Engineering (SIFBI)
description: We are seeking a Biotechnology Scientist for the Biotransformation and Engineering Department at A*STAR's Singapore Institute of Food and Biotechnology Innovation (SIFBI). The Agency for Science, Technology and Research (A*STAR) is Singapore's lead public sector R&D agency. Through open innovation, we collaborate with our partners in both the public and private sectors to benefit the economy and society. A*STAR SIFBI's vision is to be an innovation engine for health and well-being, focusing on the Asian phenotype. Our two strategic pillars are: 1) Developing sustainable, value-added ingredients for food and consumer care; and 2) Optimizing health and wellbeing within the Asian phenotype. At SIFBI's Biotransformation and Engineering Division, we drive sustainable biomanufacturing through the integration of synthetic biology and innovative bioprocess development. Harnessing our expertise in bioprocess design, biocatalysis, precision fermentation and scale-up research, we create engineering solutions to translate the scientific proof of concepts into economic value. The current position will be further strengthening our biocatalysis, fermentation and downstream processing team with the following primary responsibilities expected from this role: Develop techniques, conditions, and strategies to understand, identify and test innovative and scalable submerged fermentation processes Deliver high quality science holistically: clear & justified hypotheses, efficient experimentation, rigorous data analysis, factual & concise documentation and an openness to engage in scientific debate. Lead and manage multidisciplinary research projects Effectively collaborate internally and externally, and with stakeholders or team members that vary geographically, culturally, and stylistically. Communicate work by preparing and presenting proposals and progress updates to various technical, leadership and business development team. Write journal papers, technology disclosures and project reports. Develop and test novel approaches to fermentation, automation and digitalisation (i.e. continuous fermentation, digital twin) Initiate risk assessments and ensure compliance with all HSEP (Health, Safety and Environmental Protection) rules and regulations, as well as SIFBI internal rules. Requirements PhD in Bioprocess Engineering/Biotechnology/Biochemical Engineering/Molecular Biology/Microbiology or related field Fermentation experience working with difference microbial systems at various scales as well as Biocatalysis and Downstream Processing expertise Process-centric approach to fermentation process development and holistic process understanding ("begin with the end in mind") Able to interpret data and derive next steps Demonstrated track record in project management and applied multidisciplinary research/development in the field of biocatalysis/bioprocess development/biochemical engineering Good interpersonal and written skills Very good organization and time management in planning daily work activities. Commitment to strict compliance with established standards for safe work practices. Self-motivation and perseverance. Excellent communication and teamwork skills. A constant striving for further development of one's own abilities. The above eligibility criteria are not exhaustive. A*STAR may include additional selection criteria based on its prevailing recruitment policies. These policies may be amended from time to time without notice. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Industrial Hygienist
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Industrial Hygienist to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on preventing occupational illness and disease by anticipating, recognizing, evaluating, and controlling environmental factors arising in the workplace which may cause sickness, impaired health and well-being, or significant discomfort to the workforce. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, stakeholders from other functions and departments including the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your expertise to our Refinery on matters of industrial hygiene (IH) and occupational health & safety (OH&S). In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager. As the technical authority and expert in IH and OH&S, you manage and lead the refinery in IH and OH&S function to reduce operational risk exposure to the workforce through governance of OEMS principles, regulatory compliance and requirements. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Risk Identification, Evaluation and Control Proactively anticipate and recognize chemical hazards, physical hazards, biological hazards, and ergonomic hazards across refining areas Design, execute, and manage comprehensive Industrial Hygiene (IH) monitoring programs . Conduct qualitative and quantitative exposure assessments for airborne contaminants, chemical exposure, noise & physical agents and ergonomic risk Interpret monitoring results against local (MOM/WSH) and internationally recognized (ACGIH/OSHA) standards Conduct routine Occupational Hygiene site inspections and audits, focusing on exposure risks, ventilation systems (LEVs), and chemical storage/handling procedures Control Measures and Management Systems Recommend, implement effective industrial hygiene engineering as key control strategies and evaluate its effectiveness Oversight chemical inventory, Safety Data Sheet (SDS) management, and risk assessment process for hazardous substances Develop and enforce stringent chemical handling procedures Administer and manage IH Management system and programs such as Ventilation Systems, Hazard Communications, PPE standards, Ergonomic standards, heat stress program, medical examination & surveillance program, Hearing Conservation Program (HCP), including noise mapping, audiometric testing, noise control implementation, process and office ergonomic program Conduct industrial hygiene risk assessments for process work area, offices, laboratory, recommending necessary adjustments and controls measures Strategizing Regulatory Compliance (PEL, STEL, TWA, C) and Translating to Operable Compliance Translate PEL, STEL, OEL requirement into practical, executable operational compliance Deep working knowledge of Singapore and international acts, regulations, requirements related to industrial hygiene topics Ensure strict adherence to permissible/occupational exposure limit and relevant regulations and Codes of Practice relating to occupational health and safety Prepare detailed industrial hygiene reports, regulatory submissions and internal performance metrics. Maintain accurate records of all monitoring data, calibration records, and exposure assessments Provide technical support and expertise during health-related emergency response situations (e.g chemical spills) Training and Collaboration Develop and deliver targeted training programs on industrial hygiene topics, including chemical management and hazard communication, PPE use, respiratory protection, noise conservations, heat stress, ergonomics Collaborate closely with cross functional teams to integrate industrial hygiene into daily operations, projects and Process Safety Management (PSM). Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Technical expert in industrial hygiene, occupational health role and operationalized industrial hygiene into day to day operations Minimum 10 years experience as a practicing industrial hygienist in a refinery, petrochemical or chemical manufacturing industry; track record in industrial hygiene monitoring (chemical, physical and biological to personal and area exposure)  IH engineering control, IH Management System Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in industrial hygiene highly desired Professionally certified industrial hygienist (CIH) or registered industrial hygienist (RIH) or equivalent. Candidates without IH certification but demonstrate mastery and proven track record in IH will be considered Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request) We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Staff Engineer, Gas, Global Facilities
description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Gas team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Bulk Gas and Specialty Gas Systems. As a Senior Engineer, Gas for the Global Facilities Engineering department at Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible to provide high quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of your discipline specific Facilities systems in support of the Semiconductor Manufacturing sites across the Micron Global Manufacturing Network – for Bulk Gas systems and Specialty Gas systems. In this role, you will support the Facilities Teams and Manufacturing sites across the network as the Technical Subject Matter Expert through all phases of design, construction, installation, start-up, operations and maintenance with focus and emphasis on Global Standardization and Alignment, Innovation, Reliability and Performance, and Sustainability. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Gas Systems including Bulk Gas Systems, Specialty Gas Systems, Gas Purification, Gas Cabinets and Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Gases and Piping, Compressed Air, and Gas Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities

title: Process Safety Engineer
description: We are looking for a Process Safety Engineer (m/f/d) to join our Process Safety department at Advario in Singapore, starting as soon as possible. Job Profile: The Process Safety Engineer leads the implementation and continuous improvement of Process Safety Management (PSM) systems to identify, assess, control and monitor major hazard risks in the terminals. The PSE leads the delivery of Process Safety initiatives by collaborating with Engineering, Operations, HSSE teams and regulatory authorities to ensure compliance with Singapore’s regulations and industry best practices. He/She tracks PSM performance data and contributes to the development and maintenance of the relevant sections of the organisation's Major Hazard Installation (MHI) Safety Case. He/She conducts process safety reviews and hazard/risk studies for existing and new facilities. The PSE also participates in process safety incident investigations and follows up on preventive and corrective actions. Your Tasks: 1) Administer Process Safety Management Systems Support Process Safety framework adoption and implementation for the local organization in accordance to relevant local regulations, internal and industry standards/best practices. Ensure governance of Process Safety Management (PSM) system is in place to achieve organization's operational effectiveness and efficiency, reliable reporting, compliance to laws, regulations and policies through: - Develop operational process safety solutions by defining, studying, estimating, and screening alternative solutions. - Improve operational process safety results by studying, evaluating, and recommending process re-design; implementing changes; contributing information and opinion to engineering design and modification teams. - Monitor and report key process safety performance indicators, track action closure, support audits and compliance reviews, promote continuous improvement in risk control. Investigate Process Safety incidents with the local team and propose suitable improvements / recommendations to avoid reoccurrence. Identifies operational process safety problems by observing and studying system and performance results. 2) Support Process Engineering Projects Review and/or provide support in e.g. Process Hazard Analyses (PHA), MOCs, risk assessments, safety studies, Safety Case and project designs, to ensure process safety related considerations are identified and addressed. Develop recommendations and addressing Process Safety deficiencies identified in these reviews, where applicable and ensure relevant documentation is complete. To systematically seek out process safety improvement opportunities and assess risk to ALARP 3) Administer Staff and Organizational Development Build Process Safety competency in the organization through training sessions on process safety topics. Your Profile: Bachelor’s degree in in Chemical Engineering, Process Engineering, Safety Engineering (or equivalent with relevant experience) Minimum 5 years working experience in oil and gas industry, specializing in process safety and hazard management Strong knowledge of process hazard identification and risk assessment methods and tools (e.g. HAZOP, LOPA, QRA, Bowtie). Knowledge of international and local safety standards and regulatory requirements (e.g., API, NFPA, SS, functional safety). Strong terminal operations knowledge, its drivers from a technical and risk point of view. Good communication, analytical, problem-solving and stakeholder-management skills; able to work across engineering, operations, maintenance and HSSE teams. Proficient in consequence modeling software (preferred). Please note: This position is based in Jurong Island Official Working hours: Mon - Fri, 0830 - 1700 hours We offer you among other things : Special benefits and company events organized for employees Employees are covered under the group insurance plan, with dependent coverage for hospitalization Lunch is provided while in the terminal and company shuttle bus transport to and from Jurong East MRT

title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. As a result of this unique SUTD treatment, AI and humans brainstorm, spar and prototype together, resulting in solutions that are elevated several-fold. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “AI-Native Token Communication for Physical AI". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Physical AI. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of token communication and Physical AI over wireless networks. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.
