title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

title: Piping Stress Engineer
description: Performing stress analysis on piping systems using software like CAESAR II, ensuring compliance with codes and standards, selecting appropriate support components like springs and anchors, and collaborating with other engineering disciplines. Key responsibilities involve creating critical line lists, generating design reports, and ensuring the structural integrity of the piping system under various loads (thermal, vibration, deadweight). Key responsibilities Stress analysis: Conduct pipe stress analyses using software like CAESAR II to ensure the piping system can withstand operational and environmental loads. Compliance: Ensure all designs comply with project specifications, client requirements, and relevant industry codes and standards (e.g., ASME, API). Support design: Design and select all piping support components, including springs, constant hangers, guides, and anchors, and prepare corresponding datasheets. Documentation: Prepare and review technical documentation such as stress analysis reports, critical line lists, specifications, and datasheets. Collaboration: Work with other disciplines, including layout, process, and structural, to integrate piping stress requirements into the overall plant design. Review and verification: Review vendor documents, isometric drawings, and other engineering deliverables to ensure technical accuracy and compliance with stress requirements. Technical support: Provide technical support for project-related activities, such as HAZOPs, technical bid evaluations, and design reviews. Problem-solving: Analyze and provide solutions for technically challenging projects, addressing issues like vibration, fatigue, and nozzle loads. Required skills and qualifications Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering or a related field. Experience with pipe stress analysis software, particularly CAESAR II. Strong understanding of piping codes and standards. Proficiency in performing various types of stress calculations, including thermal, vibration, and fatigue analysis. Familiarity with related software like WRC 107/297 and FEA tools is a plus. Good communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with multi-disciplinary teams. Attention to detail and ability to produce high-quality technical documentation.

title: Field Ops Engineer
description: The successful candidate will be a part of the Business Development Team, reporting to the Business Development Manager. The Field Operations Engineer supports the expansion of VISCOY’s market space in Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance (AIMM) through field operations (proof-of-concepts (POC), product demonstrations and field deployments) and applications development for products in our portfolio. The Field Operations Engineer is the technical expert of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies. He/she is empowered to experiment and push the technical boundaries of the technologies that we represent in the field. Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance presents challenges that require the cross deployment of multiple technologies and customisation to the local context to overcome. The Field Operations Engineer leads the field deployment of our portfolio of technologies with our customers with the objective of overcoming these challenges through the development of more effective, cost efficient and safer solutions for the customer’s AIMM regime. The Field Operations Engineer works closely with our Technology Partners and Customers in the scoping, testing and deployment of our products and solutions. He/she will be the bridge between technology and real-world applications in the markets that we serve. To be successful in the role, the Field Operations Engineer is also required to have a good understanding of the limitations of our products. He/she works closely with the Repair and Maintenance Engineer in trouble-shooting, repair and developing the optimal operating parameters for the products in our portfolio. With deep understanding of technology gaps in AIMM, the Applications Development Engineer will be actively involved in scanning the market for relevant new technologies and developments. Technology is not bounded by geography or culture. The successful Field Operations Engineer enjoys the opportunity to travel and work in international teams with Technology Partners and Customers. The role will require the successful candidate to be proactive, possessing initiative, and a strong sense of ownership to take on and see-through projects assigned. He/she will need to collaborate closely both with internal and external stakeholders (technology partners and customers). Responsibilities Support the Business Development Team in: Development of use cases & applications for our Robotics and Digital Technology solutions in Asset Inspection, Monitoring & Maintenance Field Testing and Deployment of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies Solving AIMM challenges presented by our customers Be an expert of the Robotics and Digital Technologies in our portfolio To establish strong network, build close rapport and work effectively with both internal and external stakeholders. Manage and execute assigned projects to achieve agreed objectives Expand our technologies portfolio by keeping actively abreast of latest/emerging technologies that is relevant for the company’s current or adjacent market space. Develop market intelligence relevant for the development of Business Development strategies. Requirements An enthusiastic team player who can work independently Strong sense of ownership, positive mind-set and “can-do” attitude Highly motivated and results driven in the execution of assigned projects Enjoys travelling and working in international, cross-cultural teams Tertiary qualifications in Engineering/Technical disciplines. Fresh graduates are welcomed to apply. Candidates with experience will be preferred. Good spoken and written English Excellent communication and interpersonal skills Remuneration Attractive Basic Monthly Salary commensurate with relevant experience.

title: Senior Engineer - Production & Material Control (PMC)
description: Job Purpose Senior Engineer - Production and Material Control Engineer (PMC) is responsible for ensuring that the materials required for manufacturing processes and projects are available when needed, in the correct quantities, and at the right cost. Participate in the planning and implementation of team-building activities, training sessions, and workshops to enhance productivity, morale, and teamwork. Provide feedback and suggestions to improve team performance and address challenges. Perform additional duties as assigned by manager. Requirements Bachelor’s degree in supply chain management, business, engineering, or related field. Prior experience as a Material Planner, Inventory Coordinator, or similar role in an equipment manufacturing environment. Prior experience as a Production Planner, Scheduler, or similar role in a manufacturing environment, preferably in equipment or heavy machinery manufacturing. Strong understanding of supply chain and inventory management systems. Excellent organizational and planning skills, with the ability to multitask and manage priorities. Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities. Good negotiation and communication skills. Proficiency in relevant software, including ERP systems and Microsoft Office. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Comfortable working on-site (Monday - Friday).

title: QA Engineer
description: Job Descriptions 1. Quality Control & Inspection: Perform detailed QC buy-off inspections of precision-engineered parts, assemblies, or systems at various stages of production, utilizing industry-standard inspection techniques (e.g., CMM, visual inspection, functional testing). Track non-conformance reports filed during the buy-off process and develop and implement preventive action plans to mitigate recurring defects. 2. Process Development Design and implement quality assurance protocols, checklists, and testing procedures specific to precision engineering workflows. Establish, update, and ensure adherence to quality documentation, including inspection reports and non-conformance records. Coordinate and oversee the First Article Inspection (FAI) process for new manufacturing processes or parts, ensuring compliance with industry and customer standards. 3. Collaboration & Problem Solving Work closely with design engineers to review technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances, providing feedback to enhance manufacturability and quality. Drive cross-functional initiatives to enhance long-term quality and reliability, performing root cause analysis and implementing corrective actions. Lead the tracking and investigation of quality-related returns and customer complaints using the 8D methodology, coordinating problem-solving efforts and implementing long-term preventive measures. 4. Compliance & Standards Ensure all products meet industry standards (e.g., ISO 9001, ASME, or other relevant certifications) and customer requirements. Participate in internal and external audits, ensuring adherence to regulatory and supplier quality standards. 5. Continuous Improvement Analyze quality data and trends from issue tracking systems (Project Issue Log) to identify opportunities for process optimization and defect reduction. Classify issues into relevant disciplines such as quality, operational, or design-related concerns. Host post-mortem discussions after project closures, documenting lessons learned and integrating preventive action plans into future workflows. 6. Documentation & Reporting Maintain accurate records of inspections, test results, and quality metrics using digital Quality Management Systems (QMS). Prepare detailed reports for management and clients on quality performance, compliance status, and improvement initiatives, providing actionable insights to drive quality excellence." 7. Any other Task Assigned by Direct reporting manager or Stage BU head. Requirements Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Precision Engineering, Quality Management, or a related field from a recognized institution. Preferably 3-5 years of experience in quality control or quality assurance within precision engineering or manufacturing, with hands-on expertise in inspection techniques (e.g. CMM, visual inspection, functional testing). Skill Proficiency in interpreting technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances. Experience with Quality Management Systems (QMS) and issue tracking tools (e.g., Project Issue Log). Familiarity with First Article Inspection (FAI), root cause analysis (e.g., 8D methodology), and non-conformance reporting Personal Attributes Detail-Oriented: Exceptional attention to detail to ensure precision in inspections, documentation, and compliance with stringent quality standards. Analytical Thinker: Strong problem-solving skills with a methodical approach to conducting root cause analysis and implementing effective corrective actions. Collaborative Team Player: Ability to work seamlessly with cross-functional teams, including design engineers and production staff, fostering a cooperative and solution-driven environment. Proactive and Initiative-Driven: A self-starter who anticipates quality issues, develops preventive measures, and drives continuous improvement without needing constant supervision. Adaptable: Flexible and resilient, able to handle evolving priorities and additional tasks assigned by management in a fast-paced manufacturing setting. Communicative: Clear and concise in conveying quality findings, preparing reports, and presenting insights to stakeholders at all levels. Results-Focused: Committed to achieving high-quality outcomes, meeting deadlines, and ensuring customer satisfaction through reliable processes. Ethical and Integrity-Driven: Upholds the highest standards of professionalism and accountability, ensuring compliance with industry regulations and organizational policies. Calm Under Pressure: Maintains composure and sound judgment during audits, customer complaints, or production challenges, ensuring effective resolution.

title: Research Engineer (in Multimodal AI ) - MRP
description: Job Purpose: The primary responsibility of this role is to deliver on an industry innovation research project where you will be part of the research team to develop a Multimodal AI for Fire Detection & Safety Systems. title: Lab Analyst / Technician
description: About NTI Membranes: NTI is a Singapore-based leader in advanced membrane technology, dedicated to providing sustainable energy and water recovery solutions. We specialize in designing, manufacturing, and integrating high-performance hollow fiber membrane systems like for municipal, industrial, and commercial applications. Our mission is to help our clients "close the energy and water loop" by turning wastewater and waste heat into a valuable resource, ensuring a sustainable future for Singapore and the region. The Opportunity : Join our R&D team and work on applied research that directly improves our water and energy recovery solutions. As a lab analyst, you will support process development and optimization within wastewater treatment and energy recovery operations. The role involves conducting laboratory-based experiments,  performing water quality analyses, characterizing membranes, and providing technical insights to enhance system performance and resource recovery efficiency. Key Responsibilities: ·      Conduct laboratory tests related to waste water treatment, membrane filtration, and energy recovery processes. ·      Monitor and record key performance indicators such as flux, trans-membrane pressure, chemical oxygen demand (COD), biological oxygen demand (BOD), total suspended solids (TSS), conductivity, and otherrelevant water quality parameters. ·      Carry out membrane autopsies to determine fouling mechanisms and identify inorganic, organic, or biological foulants. ·      Perform fouling analysis to support the development and evaluation of cleaning protocols and assess membrane lifecycle. ·      Analyze experimental data to determine optimal operating conditions and evaluate system performance for various applications. ·      Conduct membrane characterization using analytical instruments such as FTIR, SEM, and other relevant methods to study surface morphology and chemical composition. ·      Prepare technical reports, maintain accurate laboratory records, and ensure data integrity for ongoing research or plant optimization work. ·      Support pilot-scale or bench-scale studies aimed at improving treatment efficiency, resource recovery, and environmental impact reduction. ·      Adhere to laboratory safety standards, ensure proper handling of chemicals, and maintain cleanliness and organization of the lab environment. Who You Are Hold a Degree in Environmental Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Chemistry, or a related discipline. Possess hands-on laboratory experience, especially in water quality testing and sample preparation. Familiar with membrane separation principles, fouling mechanisms, and analytical characterization     techniques. Meticulous, detail-oriented, and committed to producing reliable, high-quality experimental results. Motivated by solving practical environmental challenges through data-driven approaches. A collaborative team player who can also work independently on defined research tasks and timelines.

title: Quality Control Inspector
description: HAID BIOTECHNOLOGY INDUSTRY (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD. is seeking a meticulous and experienced Quality Control Inspector to join our new startup team in Singapore. The successful candidate will play a vital role in ensuring our products meet established standards of quality, reliability, and performance by monitoring and auditing all phases of the production process. 品管Quality Control Inspector 一、来料检验职责Incoming Material Inspection 按照检验标准对供应商来料进行外观、细度、性能等检查。 Perform inspections on supplier materials according to established standards, including appearance, fineness, and performance. 对不合格物料进行标识、隔离、记录并及时反馈采购与供应商。 Identify, isolate, and record non-conforming materials, providing timely feedback to Procurement and suppliers. 统计来料不良率，参与供应商质量评估与改善。 Calculate and track the incoming defect rate, and participate in supplier quality evaluation and improvement initiatives. 对检验工具（卡尺、振筛等）进行日常保养与校验状态确认。 Conduct daily maintenance and verify the calibration status of inspection tools (e.g., calipers, vibrating sieves). 二、制程检验职责 In-Process Inspection 按检验计划对生产过程进行首件、巡检、抽检。 Execute first-article, patrol, and sampling inspections based on the inspection plan for the production process. 监督生产按作业指导书、工艺流程执行，发现异常及时制止并上报。 Monitor production to ensure adherence to Work Instructions and process workflows; halt and report any irregularities immediately. 跟踪制程问题的改善过程，确认改善措施的有效性。 Follow up on the correction of process issues and verify the effectiveness of improvement measures. 记录并统计制程不良数据，为工艺优化提供依据。 Record and compile data on in-process defects to provide basis for process optimization. 对关键工序的关键参数进行监控，确保过程稳定。 Monitor key parameters in critical processes to ensure operational stability. 三、成品检验职责Final Product Inspection 按出货标准对成品进行外观、性能等检查。 Inspect finished products for appearance and performance against shipping standards. 对不合格品进行判定、隔离及处理（返工/报废）。 Judge, isolate, and disposition non-conforming products (for rework or scrap). 确保所有出货批次具备完整的检验记录。 Ensure complete inspection records are available for all outgoing batches. 与生产、仓库沟通，避免不良品流入客户。 Liaise with Production and Warehouse to prevent defective products from reaching the customer. 四、质量异常处理 Quality Exception Handling 发现质量异常及时报告并推动异常分析。 Report quality anomalies promptly and drive root cause analysis. 跟进纠正预防措施的落实与验证。 Follow up on the implementation and verification of corrective and preventive actions. 协助组织质量会议，推动跨部门改善。 Assist in organizing quality meetings and driving      cross-departmental improvements. 五、质量文件与记录维护 Quality Documentation & Record Maintenance 负责检验记录、检验报告的填写、整理与归档。 Complete, organize, and archive all inspection records and reports. 维护检验标准、作业指导书等质量相关文件。 Maintain quality-related documents such as inspection standards and work instructions. 协助编制和更新检验规范与SOP。 Assist in the compilation and updating of inspection specifications and SOPs. 六、设备与环境管理 Equipment & Environment Management 负责使用的检验设备的日常点检与维护。 Perform daily spot checks and maintenance on assigned inspection equipment. 确保检验区域的 6S 管理符合要求。 Ensure the 6S management standards are met in the inspection area. 协助设备校准与外部计量机构联系。 Assist with equipment calibration and liaise with external metrology agencies. 七、培训与沟通Training & Communication 对新员工进行质量意识、检验标准的培训。 Conduct training on quality awareness and inspection standards for new employees. 与生产、购销等部门沟通质量问题与改善计划。 Communicate quality issues and improvement plans with Production, Purchasing, and Sales departments. 参与客户投诉问题的调查与回复资料准备。 Participate in the investigation of customer complaints and assist in preparing response reports. Requirements: · Diploma or higher in Food Science, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Pharmaceutical, or a related field. · At least 3 years of working experience in a similar role with proven experience. · Good command of both verbal and written English and Mandarin to liaise with Mandarin-speaking business associates. · Able to start immediately or with a short notice period preferred. · Only Singaporeans/PRs may apply.

Inclusive Culture: A diverse workplace where innovative ideas are valued and teamwork is encouraged. Position Overview: Hysses Singapore is looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Laboratory Technician to support daily laboratory operations, product testing, and formulation work. In this role, you will help carry out QC checks, prepare samples, assist in product development, and ensure that laboratory processes follow safety, GMP, and SOP standards. You will work closely with the R&D and Production teams to support testing, documentation, and small-scale manufacturing activities. If you enjoy hands-on lab work and want to contribute to creating high-quality products, we welcome you to join our team. Job Descriptions: Conduct formulation development tasks, including creation of scent, skincare/cosmetic prototypes, mix development, colour matching and verification. Assist in sensory evaluation sessions, including fragrance testing or product benchmarking. Carry out laboratory tests, including viscosity measurement, pH checks, appearance evaluation, stability testing and compatibility testing. Support QC checks on incoming raw materials, in-process mixtures, and finished goods. Record, compile, and update test results, product specifications, batch records, and formulation sheets in a standardized format. Assist in technology transfer from pilot batches to full-scale manufacturing, ensuring smooth handover to production team. Provide operational support during small production runs, filling trials, or machine setup. Accurately weigh, measure, and prepare raw materials according to formulation requirements. Ensure the laboratory is kept organized, clean, and audit-ready following internal SOPs and GMP guidelines. Operate, clean, and maintain laboratory and production equipment, ensuring compliance with safety and hygiene standards. Any other jobs assigned. Professional Qualifications & Experience: Minimum Higher Nitec in Chemical, Chemical Process Technology, Biotechnology, or a related discipline. Working knowledge of quality assurance methods, laboratory practices, and testing procedures. Solid understanding of relevant regulatory standards (e.g., GMP, ISO). Good communication skills, both verbal and written. Strong attention to detail; meticulous and well-organized. Excellent data collection, documentation, and analytical abilities. Ability to work independently with minimal supervision and collaborate effectively in a team. Willingness to learn; on-the-job training will be provided. Technical Skills: Proficient in using laboratory instruments such as viscometers, pH meters, analytical balances, mixers, and stability chambers. Familiar with basic physical/chemical testing, sample preparation, and formulation procedures. Able to perform QC checks on raw materials, in-process samples, and finished goods. Comfortable operating and maintaining laboratory and small-scale production equipment. Skilled in data entry and documentation using tools such as MS Excel, Google Sheets, or LIMS (if applicable). Basic understanding of formulation development, color matching, and prototype preparation.

title: Chief Technology Officer (CIM)
description:
About the Role: As our Chief Technology Officer, you will define and lead the technological
vision and strategy for our next-generation Computer-Integrated Manufacturing
(CIM) platform. You will architect AI-driven solutions that power digital
transformation and Industry 4.0 for global clients, with a strategic focus on
the semiconductor industry. This role is critical in transitioning our product
suite into intelligent, scalable, and market-leading manufacturing systems. Key Responsibilities: Establish and execute the overall technology roadmap, ensuring alignment with business goals and market evolution in industrial AI and smart manufacturing. Lead and scale global engineering teams across the full software development lifecycle (SDLC) — from concept and design to deployment, iteration, and support. Drive architectural decisions and hands-on technical direction using modern stacks including C#, Java, Vue.js, SQL, and emerging AI/ML frameworks. Foster innovation by integrating AI/ML capabilities into core CIM products to enhance predictive analytics, automation, and operational intelligence. Act as the key technical voice for clients and partners, building trust through deep domain expertise and solution-oriented leadership. Ensure robust technical governance, including code quality, system scalability, security, and comprehensive documentation. Mentor senior technical talent and promote a culture of engineering excellence, collaboration, and continuous learning within a globally distributed team. Essential Requirements: Advanced degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field; PhD is a plus. 10+ years of progressive technology leadership experience, with a proven track record in architecting and delivering enterprise-level software products, preferably in manufacturing, IoT, or industrial domains. Deep, hands-on architectural experience with manufacturing execution systems (MES), equipment automation programs (EAP), or related core manufacturing platforms. Deep hands-on expertise in one or more core technologies such as C#, Java, or modern front-end frameworks; practical experience with AI/ML in production environments is highly desirable. Strong business acumen with experience in aligning technology development with commercial objectives and client needs in global markets. Exceptional communication skills in both English and Chinese, with the ability to articulate vision to technical and non-technical stakeholders. Willingness and ability to travel internationally as required, including extended periods for key client engagements, team integration, and strategic project oversight. Preferred Qualifications: Direct domain experience in semiconductor manufacturing or high-tech industrial operations. Established thought leadership in Industry 4.0, digital twins, or industrial AI solutions. Experience in managing geographically dispersed R&D or product engineering teams. A history of successful cross-cultural collaboration and global market expansion.

title: Simulation Senior Staff Engineer
description: Job Responsibilities Develop and validate complex mathematical models and algorithms to simulate the physics of the slit coating process, including fluid flow,material properties (viscosity, surface tension, rheology), tool parameters (coating speed, coating gap, slot die head design), and thermal dynamics. Utilize CFD software to analyse and screen a broad range of tool parameters and material variables to identify optimal die geometry, ideal material properties (e.g., a target viscosity range), and a stable operating window without consuming expensive materials or time. Design and execute simulation experiments to test hypotheses, identify critical process parameters (e.g., coating speed, flow rate, die gap, substrate speed), and validate model accuracy against physical experimental data. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including design engineers, material scientists, and manufacturing teams to design highly focused DoE guided by the insights from simulation and validate the simulation models and confirm the optimized parameters in a real-world setting Analyse simulation results, interpret findings, and provide actionable insights and recommendations to improve coating quality, reduce defects, and enhance process efficiency. Document simulation methodologies, assumptions, and results comprehensively in technical reports and presentations for internal and external stakeholders. Stay current with advancements in simulation software, numerical methods, and relevant industry standards related to thin-film deposition and organic electronics. Job Requirements Required Education: Master's or Ph.D. in a relevant engineering or science field with a focus on computational methods or fluid dynamics. Experience: Proven experience using simulation tools for complex engineering problems, ideally in fluid dynamics or coating processes. Technical Skills: Proficiency in simulation software (COMSOL, ANSYS Fluent), programming (Python, MATLAB, or C++), fluid dynamics, surface tension, and material science. Knowledge of quality control and data analysis is also required. Soft Skills: Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. Ability to work both independently and as part of a team. Preferred Experience with slot-die coating or other thin-film deposition techniques. Familiarity with CAD software. Knowledge of machine learning/AI for simulations. Experience in the slit coating of organic dielectrics.

title: Design Engineer
description: Company Overview NACOS Marine is a globally integrated group of companies at the forefront of maritime automation, navigation, and control systems. Building on decades of expertise and a heritage of trusted solutions, NACOS Marine continues to pioneer technologies that set the standard for safer, smarter, and more efficient ship operations worldwide. Role Overview We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Design Engineer (m/f/d) to join our team in Singapore . In this key role, you will contribute to the design, documentation, and delivery of automation and control systems for complex maritime applications. You will work closely with engineers, project managers, and other stakeholders to ensure high-quality, efficient, and compliant technical deliverables including drawings, documentation, and HMI design. Your work will play an essential role in ensuring that NACOS Marine systems meet the highest standards of quality, safety, and reliability across global marine and naval projects. Key Responsibilities Deliver accurate and high-quality project documentation, drawings, and HMI designs for OT systems. Collaborate with project teams to develop and implement technical solutions aligned with project objectives. Evaluate and advise on design modifications and assess potential technical or cost implications. Contribute to project planning and ensure timely completion of design-related deliverables. Participate in internal project meetings, design reviews, and milestone discussions. Ensure compliance with internal and customer-specific quality, safety, and cybersecurity standards. Support colleagues with technical input and resolve design-related challenges promptly. Maintain confidentiality in all project-related matters, particularly in Naval contracts. Willingness to travel internationally for design coordination, testing, or customer engagements. Key Requirements Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Electrical, Electronic, or Mechanical Engineering from a recognized institution. Proven experience in EPLAN and SolidWorks for design and documentation. Excellent proficiency in English, both written and verbal. Strong communication, teamwork, and organizational abilities. Capable of working independently and effectively within cross-functional teams. Willingness and ability to travel internationally as required. Why choose us and why we’d choose you At NACOS , we’re about more than getting the job done, we empower people to take ownership, embrace responsibility, and shape their own path. Innovation thrives here through collaboration, curiosity, and the courage to drive change. Authenticity is key. When you can truly be yourself at work, great things happen. We believe in openness, honesty, and continuous learning, creating an environment where every voice matters, and people feel valued and supported to grow. Our team is passionate about building smart technologies that help create a safer and more sustainable maritime future, and we’re looking for a colleague who shares that drive. We value and embrace diversity in all forms and are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone can succeed based on their talents and contributions. Does this sound like the right opportunity for you? We’d love to hear from you. Please submit your application today, and we’ll be in touch soon.

Effective collaboration with cross-functional teams (Production, Industrialization, Engineering, IT/Data).

Be competent to set requirements and review results of Finite Element Analysis on new or existing designs Liaise with other departments including engineering, planning, sales, contract administration, legal, workshop and procurement departments Site visits at customers for technical support Provide technical leadership for project/product assigned. Project Schedule To create and present a planned schedule of a task Keep all relevant and appropriate parties informed of progress and outcomes.      Resolving issues as they become apparent. Other Duties Travel as required to support product sales, field runs and troubleshooting      exercises. Communicate with all levels of internal and external personnel as needed providing clear and concise reports. Know and fully understand Quality Policy and comply with all requirements of      the Quality Systems Manual, Operating and Technical Procedures and      Workplace Instructions. Must understand and comply with all safety rules and company policies. Work assignments carried out to the highest quality level. Perform various other duties and activities as assigned by supervisor within the      physical constraints of the job. What You Have: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent. Minimum 12 years’ experience in the product design of oil field equipment, preferably in either the Surface or Subsea Wellhead disciplines. Proven ability to work under pressure to meet all agreed deadlines and time scales. The ability to work and communicate with all levels within the organization to accomplish common goals and directives. Good written and verbal English communication skills. Fluent use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, etc). Has advanced hands-on skills in 2D and 3D Computer Aided Design  (CAD), such as Solidworks and AutoCAD. Additional Info: ​ This position requires working onsite five days a week. Caterpillar is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status; however, as a global company, Caterpillar offers many job opportunities outside of Singapore which can be found through our employment website at www.caterpillar.com/careers . title: QA & QC Engineer(Semiconductor Plant Project)
description: Job Responsibilities Responsible for the management of project Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) documents, including formulating and maintaining the Project Quality Plan (PQP) to ensure it is in line with client standards and international standards; developing Inspection and Test Plans (ITPs) covering fields such as Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC), piping (utility and process), and equipment installation; managing documents like work method statements (involving the fit-up of various pipe joints, such as in scenarios of underground pipes, welding, flushing, equipment installation, etc.), Welding Procedure Specifications (WPS)/Procedure Qualification Records (PQR)/welder qualifications (in line with the requirements for piping and structural works in ASME Section IX), as well as calibration certificates for torque wrenches, gauges, particle counters, airflow meters, etc. Implement Non-Conformance Report (NCR)/Corrective Action Report (CAR) procedures, which should include root cause analysis and corrective actions when handling non-conformities. Conduct material control work, carry out receiving inspections of materials, verify whether materials conform to the Approved Vendor List (AVL), and perform receiving inspections for each batch of incoming materials. Responsible for QA/QC work in the fabrication & assembly phase, including welding inspection of carbon steel pipes/stainless steel pipes: Pre-welding inspection: Review Welding Procedure Specifications (WPS) and Procedure Qualification Records (PQR) to ensure they are approved for carbon steel pipes and comply with ASME Section IX and project specifications; verify welders' qualifications in terms of welding processes (SMAW, GTAW, GMAW, FCAW) and positions; confirm that the material grade, diameter, thickness, and heat number of pipes, fittings, and flanges are consistent with drawings and Material Test Certificates (MTC); check whether the type of electrodes/filler materials is approved, storage is appropriate (baking, holding ovens), and batch/lot traceability is maintained; inspect whether the fit-up and joint preparation meet the bevel angle, root face, and root gap specified in the WPS, whether the alignment is within tolerance, and whether tack welds are qualified (no cracks, correct size). Welding inspection: Pay attention to orbital welding (parameter documents, welding videos/data logs) and welding coupon testing for critical pipes; conduct Non-Destructive Testing (RT/UT/PT) and visual inspection according to the importance of the system; for stainless steel pipes, verify surface quality and chemical cleaning records (passivation and pickling). Responsible for QA/QC work in the installation phase: Equipment installation: Check the precision alignment, leveling, and vibration isolation of pumps, chillers, compressors, etc. HVAC and cleanroom systems: Conduct duct leak testing in accordance with SMACNA/ASHRAE standards. Piping installation: Inspect slopes, supports, anchors, and spring hangers; check gaskets, flange torque, and tightness; supervise the implementation of clean construction protocols such as nitrogen purging during welding. Responsible for testing and pre-commissioning work, create test boundaries (test packs) for each construction system; conduct hydrostatic tests/pneumatic tests in accordance with ASME B31.3 (process utility piping); supervise third parties in carrying out airflow balance testing (TAB - Testing, Adjusting & Balancing) in HVAC testing; perform system flushing/cleaning until turbidity and particle counts meet the acceptance criteria. Responsible for handover and document management work, organize as-built documents (red-line drawings and updated P&IDs), weld logs and weld maps (for piping), material traceability records (if required), final test reports (hydrostatic test, leak test, airflow), mechanical completion packages (punch list cleared, signed off by the client), and system turnover dossiers (organized by subsystem for the commissioning team). Job Requirements Familiar with relevant international standards such as ASME and client standards, and understand the processes and methods of engineering quality control. Have more than 3 years of relevant engineering QA/QC work experience, with experience in chemical, construction, and other projects being preferred. Hold relevant professional qualification certificates, such as welding inspector certificates.CSWIP 3.1, CSWIP 3.2 , AWS OR CWI, Possess good communication skills, strong sense of responsibility, be rigorous and meticulous, and able to work under certain pressure.

title: Quality Assurance Executive
description: Roles & Responsibilities Position Snapshot Location: Tuas, Singapore Company: Wyeth Nutritionals, Singapore Type of role: Permanent Joining Nestlé means you are joining the largest Food and Beverage Company in the world. At our very core, we are a human environment – passionate people driven by the purpose of enhancing the quality of life and contributing to a healthier future. A Nestle career empowers you to make an impact locally and globally, as you are provided with the opportunity to make a mark and stand out, as long as you seek it. With Nestle, you are enabled and encouraged to grow not only as professionals, but also as people. The QA Executive guides and supports the implementation of the Nestle Quality system in a production plant in collaboration with the production team and all other departments. A day in the life of a Quality Assurance Executive.. Quality System: The QA Executive guides and supports the implementation of the Nestle Quality System in the plant in collaboration with the other departments. He / she validates and verifies that QMS, HACCP, and all other SOP’s are effectively implemented. He / she assist the plant to maintain a high hygiene standard at all times. Finished Product / line Control:  Following prescribe guidelines the QA Executive establishes and reviews all quality control checks in the conversion of raw materials to Finished Goods. He / she is responsible to validate and verify that all quality checks are properly executed. He / she ensures that all manufacturing materials and finished products meet the prescribed specifications before release by close monitoring of results and advising on the corrective action to be taken in case of deviations. Raw / Packing material control: Following prescribed guidelines the QA Executive establishes, implements and reviews all quality control checks for raw/ packing materials on arrival. He / she is responsible to ensure that all checks are properly executed. He / she is able to anticipate, trouble-shoot and resolve quality deviations in collaboration with the suppliers and the QA Manager. When there are slow moving materials / aging stocks as identified by the Resource Unit / Production teams (WIP material), the QAE arrange for re-sampling and re-evaluation . Feedback results obtained to Resource Unit Manager and QA Manager. Participates in vendor assessment program. Training: Establishes in collaborations with the QA Manager, the training need for QAAs and operators of various sections, on topics such as Nestle Quality System (NQS), net weight control, sensory evaluation, alternative method, quality checks, hygiene training etc. He / She designs the training material and conducts the training sessions for QAAs and the operators. Cost Management: (a) Following prescribe guidelines, he / she controls analytical costs. (b) Continuously seeks opportunities on cost savings without impacting quality of products. Good Lab Practices - Ensures correct functioning of the laboratory with proper and adequate equipment. Trains personnel and observes Good Laboratory Practices. Sets up and maintains comprehensive ICP. Ensures safe environment in the laboratory by proper storage and handling of chemicals and safe laboratory practice. People: (a) He / she coaches, trains, and motivates his / her team to achieve KPI targets. (b) He / she maintains good work discipline in his / her department. He / she establishes the performance plan of his / her staff and conduct reviews regularly with the staff. He / she appraises their performance on an annual basic. (c) He / she leads and guides the team on safety practices and in identifying and resolving potential safety hazards in the line and the laboratory. He / she also participates in safety discussions with the plant. Continuous Improvement: (a) He / she continuously reviews the Quality system in collaboration with the Production Manager, Quality Assurance Manager, Engineering Manager, and the production team in order to improve food safety and competitive quality. In this respect the QA Executive supports the Management Review of Quality carried out at least annually. He / she is involved in the formulation of quality strategies and factory objectives. (b) He / she promotes quality improvement culture in the plant. He / She actively involves in the implementation of C.I. projects. What will make you successful: Degree in any Scientific Discipline (e.g. Food Technology, Chemistry, Food Science , Mathematics, etc.) Minimum 2 to 3 years experience in Food Manufacturing

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer-Building Services
description: The Team We are an award winning global consultancy and have been named Building’s Engineering Consultant of the Year in 2024, 2022 and 2021. We are at the forefront of the industry, priding ourselves on our exemplary track record of designing innovative and sustainable projects. Sustainability runs through everything we do at Cundall, and our people are empowered to drive change and decarbonise the built environment. By the end of this decade, we have set ourselves an ambitious goal to only work on projects that meet our zero carbon design criteria, a commitment we have called our Zero Carbon Design 2030 goal. We need engineers who share this passion for sustainability and thrive in designing energy efficient buildings and communities. Our Building Services teams at Cundall have a mission: they want to play a key role in making buildings more energy efficient, cost effective to run and, ultimately, more sustainable. To do this, we’re looking for graduates who have a desire and enthusiasm to make a difference: in our homes, our offices, our projects, our industry and our community. Working across a variety of industry sectors, our Building Services engineers are a source of world leading expertise; producing innovative and award-winning sustainable engineering solutions throughout the lifecycle of any building project. Our Building Services teams have accomplished amazing projects like an innovative office for Google, the largest arts centre in Scotland, and an energy efficient data centre for Facebook. As a graduate in the team, you could play a key part in our next big project. The Role In this Graduate Engineer role based in our Singapore office, you’ll join a team who specialise in the design of some of the most exciting construction projects in Asia and worldwide. Working closely with colleagues across our multi-disciplinary practice, you’ll provide specialist consultancy input on projects across a wide range of sectors including education, healthcare, workplace, retail and critical systems (data centres). Under the guidance of our team of experienced engineers, we’ll equip you to undertake detailed building services engineering design and analysis, defining and translating clients’ needs into designs for buildings, facilities and services - all with sustainability at the forefront of the design. You’ll have the opportunity over time to assume greater levels of responsibility and autonomy as your professional skills develop and with accredited professional development programmes from CIBSE and IET, you’ll be fully supported towards achieving chartership. The Skills Accredited with, or on course to achieve an MEng degree level (or equivalent) in either Electrical, or Building Services Engineering, you’re passionate about innovative and sustainable Electrical engineering for the built environment. Ideally, you’ll have furthered this interest by undertaking some form of relevant work experience or placement/internship. You have an appreciation for the core elements of building services engineering and crucially, have a love for creative and intuitive design. Digital engineering is a big focus at Cundall, so ideally, you’ll have an understanding of Parametric Modelling Techniques (Grasshopper or Dynamo), or at least one modern programming language (e.g. Python, C#, VB). An understanding of Engineering Optimisation Techniques, the principles of structured programming and basic knowledge in the application of Machine Learning would also be desirable. You are organised and methodical in your approach to managing your tasks and priorities, you strive to meet deadlines and have a close appreciation of the commercial factors which affect design solutions. Collaborative in nature, you’ll be able to empathise with your clients’ needs and utilise excellent communication skills, both in writing and verbally, with internal and external stakeholders. You’re used to using your initiative to overcome challenges and have a positive, can-do attitude. What’s in it for you We offer a competitive starting salary which grows as you develop, as well as many training and development opportunities to make sure you keep progressing. title: R&D Senior Business Analyst
description: As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer. The Sr. Business Analyst role will be responsible primarily for preparing product requirements and specifications, participating software hazard analysis activities, contributing to user interface design, and improving the user experience of our software products. The Sr. Business Analyst will also serve as a customer facing resource in a technical capacity to determine workflow configurations for our instrumentation. The role will conduct requirements workshops with team members and meet with individuals of various key partners: Product Managers, SW Leads, R&D, FAS, Marketing, UX, etc.) and product development teams to capture and detail measurable, testable, and traceable requirements and specifications. Responsibilities will also include interviewing customers for feedback, conducting software usability testing, and contributing to UI/UX design activities. The candidate’s experience with QMS will provide design compliance input to the software engineering teams, including, but not limited to authoring Software Requirements and Specifications; and, reviewing, and approving software-related documents, such as Risk and Hazard Analysis, Design Verification, and Validation Plans. The candidate will provide support to other business units such as service/support team to assist in preparing training materials of the new workflow and technical writer team to review customer-facing documents (e.g. user guide, white paper, help).  The candidate will also contribute to process improvement. Attention to technical detail, proven written and verbal communication skills, and the ability to analyze and navigate technical problem domains are required. Responsibilities: Prepare well-written technical requirements, functional specifications, and diagrams (e.g. UML, data flow, etc) for software and life sciences instrument systems, including detailing workflows and error-handling scenarios. Apply standard process methodologies in developing and maintaining requirements, including user stories, use cases, functional requirements, non-functional and performance requirements, and business rules. Facilitate requirements workshops with cross-functional science, engineering, and product management teams to elicit and review technical requirements and risk controls for new and sustaining software products. Manage requirements, prepare analysis models and diagrams, and generate and generate reports using tools (e.g. JAMA, JIRA, HPALM, Visio and Miro or similar). Collaborate with the test team to ensure requirements and specifications are concise, accurate, measurable, and testable. Establish traceability between customer requirements, product requirements, and specifications. Prepare trace matrix reports. Participate in customer research and interview activities to elicit user needs for product development. Contribute to user interface design and usability testing activities to provide a seamless user experience in navigating complex scientific laboratory workflows. Collaborate with UX engineers and UI designers to prepare mockups and prototype user interfaces for complex life-science domain software that meets the needs of our customers. Prepare and contribute to design compliance documentation and best-practice disciplines across all aspects of software development within the SDLC. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent. Minimum 5 years of experience with software development best practices, such as Agile or SCRUM. 8+ years industry experience in a systems/business analyst role. Proficient in the use of business analysis tools and techniques. (e.g., Jira, Miro Board, Visio) Understanding of computer systems, networking of Windows and Linux-based systems. Possess strong written and verbal communication, presentation, and facilitation skills. Comfortable working as part of a distributed, global, and cross-functional team. Prepared to work to aggressive timelines in an iterative software development environment. Highly Preferred: Experience in Life Sciences and genomics industries and/or DNA technology Certification in Business Analysis (e.g., Certified Product Owner, Certified Scrum Master)

title: CSV Validation Engineer
description: About No deviation At No deviation, we are dedicated to delivering patient-centric solutions that revolutionize the pharmaceutical landscape. As a dynamic expert in engineering consultancy, Commissioning Qualification Validation (CQV), and Quality, Compliance, and Regulatory services, we are looking for individuals who are excited to contribute to impactful projects and drive innovation. We believe that great things happen when Empathy, Integrity, and Transparency guide our actions. That's why we foster a culture of collaboration, inclusion, and continuous development – empowering our team members to grow personally and professionally. Position Overview This position supports the validation lifecycle of automation systemsprimarily DeltaV and other major DCS/SCADA/PLC platformswithin a regulated pharmaceutical manufacturing environment. The role involves creating, reviewing, and maintaining CSV documentation such as URS, design reviews, risk assessments, and qualification protocols (SAT, IQ, OQ), as well as assisting in the execution and troubleshooting of validation activities. The candidate must possess strong technical expertise in DeltaV coding and configuration to ensure accurate protocol development and review. The role requires close collaboration with clients, vendors, and test performers, adherence to cGMP and GAMP guidelines, and active participation in meeting project milestones. Depending on performance, there is an opportunity to convert to a client-side position after six months. Key Responsibilities Develop, review, and update validation documentation including URS, DR, criticality assessments, SAT/IQ/OQ protocols, reports, and exception reports. Generate and review CSV protocols specifically for DeltaV processes, ensuring alignment with regulatory and client requirements. Review vendor documentation and ensure completeness and compliance. Support protocol execution by assisting test performers and troubleshooting identified issues. Review executed protocols and reports in accordance with GMP and document quality standards. Monitor project progress and contribute to regular status reporting. Identify issues, escalate appropriately, and communicate effectively with client counterparts. Operate within defined project schedules and resource constraints to meet milestones. Support automation commissioning activities involving DeltaV, WinCC, Siemens, and Rockwell systems. Ensure adherence to safety standards and promote safe work practices at all times. Required Qualifications Degree in Engineering or Science disciplines. Minimum 3 years of relevant CSV experience in a regulated (preferably pharmaceutical) environment. Strong understanding of DeltaV coding, configuration, and process design. Hands-on commissioning experience with DeltaV, WinCC, Siemens, and Rockwell automation systems. Experience in DCS operations, automation testing, and protocol development. Solid knowledge of cGMP, GAMP, GDP, 21 CFR Part 11, ICH Q10, and ASTM E2500 risk-based approaches. Ability to manage schedules, resources, and deliverables effectively. Proactive, self-driven, and capable of independent or team-based work. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. IT-literate with strong proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Ability to work flexible hours or shifts according to project phases. Proven track record of adhering to and promoting safety requirements. Why join us? Generous Leave Policy. Comprehensive Health & Wellness Coverage: Receive robust medical and dental coverage to support your health needs. Professional Development Opportunities: Access continuous learning programs and resources for career growth within the company. Inclusive Culture: Work in an environment where innovation, collaboration, and diverse perspectives are encouraged and celebrated. Shape the Future: Make a real difference in the pharmaceutical industry while advancing your career. How to apply Are you ready to play a key role in shaping the future of pharmaceutical solutions? If you're passionate about making an impact, we want you on our team! Please submit your resume, outlining your qualifications and experience relevant to the role, here.

title: Engineer CE PYE (Physical Failure Analysis/Fault Isolation)
description: We are currently looking for motivated individuals to join Micron Technology Inc. Central Engineering (CE) - Product Yield Enhancement (PYE) Engineering Team. As CE PYE Failure Analysis (FA) Engineer for Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible for providing full fault isolation and failure analysis support for qualifications, customer returns, new device introduction, and test failures. You will become the subject matter expert on fault isolation and physical failure analysis methods. As the CE PYE FA Engineer, you will be responsible for understanding all relevant methodologies to isolate electrical failures by working closely with Product Engineering Teams. You will also have a thorough understanding of the product architecture, process flow, and test sequence to work with Process Integration, Product, Test, and Quality Reliability Assurance Engineering groups ensuring physical defects are root-caused and classified. Job Description: Responsibilities: •             Perform sophisticated electrical device characterization to determine electrical fail mechanisms. •             Operate fault isolation equipment to accurately narrow down failure defect locations. •             Conduct physical failure analysis to identify and classify physical defects. •             Establish and hypothesize potential electrical or process fail mechanisms based on detailed electrical fail models and physical failure analysis characterization, supporting process improvement. •             Collaborate closely with a diverse range of collaborators, including teams from frontend to backend and the Global Quality (GQ) department, to address and resolve defectivity issues. •             Prepare and present comprehensive electrical and physical failure mechanisms on a weekly basis to drive continuous improvement. •             Develop and implement new fault isolation and physical failure analysis techniques to stay ahead of industry standards. •             Coordinate the maintenance and high uptime of fault isolation and physical failure analysis tools to ensure world-class operational efficiency. Requirements: •             Bachelor's or equivalent experience, Master's or equivalent experience, or PhD degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or Material Science. •             Proven experience as a great team player with outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills and a strong drive to excel. •             Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex information effectively in large meetings. •             Highly motivated individuals with a passion for innovation and excellence. •             Knowledge of fault isolation equipment, techniques, and physical failure analysis is a significant advantage. •             Strong understanding of NAND/DRAM/HBM devices and knowledge of Fab processes is a desirable asset. •             Ability to support shift rotation as required to meet operational needs. Manages production support engineering for a specific product or group of products after transfer from design to high volume production. Interfaces with design, process, test and reliability engineering to solve problems. Sustains products with cost reduction and yield improvements.

description: Job Overview We are seeking an experienced satellite Engineer with at least three years of professional hands-on experience in satellite design, development, and deployment. The successful candidate will leverage their technical expertise to conduct training programs in satellite engineering for undergraduates, young professionals and youths. This role involves both technical development and educational outreach, ensuring participants gain practical skills in satellite design, assembly, and operation. Key Responsibilities Training Program Development and Delivery •	Design and deliver comprehensive training programs on satellite building, covering topics such as: o	Systems engineering o	Subsystems (power, communication, attitude control, etc.) o	Payload integration o	Satellite testing and deployment protocols •	Develop instructional materials, including manuals, presentations, and hands-on projects, to suit various audience levels (youth, professionals, and educators). •	Conduct hands-on workshops and labs, guiding participants through real-world satellite assembly and testing activities. •	Collaborate with academic institutions, industry partners, and space agencies to expand training opportunities and resources. •	Other Topics outside of Satellite Engineering that would be good to cover: o	Space mission design and planning o	Principles of Space Engineering and Applications o	Project Management in Space Evaluation and Continuous Improvement •	Evaluate training program outcomes through feedback, assessments, and participant performance. •	Continuously update training content to reflect advances in satellite technology and industry trends. •	Provide mentorship to participants during and after training programs to support their career or project goals. Qualifications and Skills Required •	Bachelor’s degree in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. •	Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience in satellite development, including design, integration, and testing. •	Strong understanding of satellite subsystems, including power systems, communications, payload integration, and attitude determination and control. •	Proficiency with satellite design tools (e.g., STK, MATLAB, SolidWorks, or similar). •	Experience with satellite testing equipment and procedures, including vibration testing, thermal testing, and deployment mechanisms. •	Excellent communication and teaching skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts for diverse audiences. •	Experienced in conducting training programmes for youths, undergraduates and professionals •	Experiences: Satellite Engineering •	Apply expertise in designing, building, and testing satellites, including CubeSats and other small satellite platforms. •	Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure training programs align with the latest developments in satellite technology and industry standards. •	Maintain and operate testing facilities, including cleanrooms, thermal vacuum chambers, and other satellite testing equipment. description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Design Technology teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the ISTE standards and have experience closing the achievement gap. This position is specifically for someone with a background in STEM, however other applicants will be considered. They must also develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Experience working with students Knowledge and experience teaching STEM is preferred.

Proficiency in front-end and back-end technologies. Experience with API development and integration. Experience with version control systems and familiarity with Agile development methodologies. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. Ability work independently and as part of the cross-functional team. Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with a passion for building high-quality digital applications. You will work closely with cross-disciplinary teams to design, implement, and optimize USV solutions, ensuring safety, efficiency, and performance on the water. Ensure that the systems meet required performance standards, safety protocols, and regulatory compliance. title: CIM Presales Solution Architect
description: Mission of the Role As a CIM Presales Solution Architect, you will be the pivotal technical bridge between our innovative CIM/AI product suite and our global clients. Your mission is to architect compelling, technically robust solutions that demonstrate how our technology solves critical manufacturing challenges, ultimately driving commercial success and forging trusted advisor relationships with key stakeholders in the semiconductor and advanced manufacturing industries. Key Responsibilities Partner with the sales team to develop and execute account-specific technical sales strategies for targeted clients. Lead deep-dive discovery workshops to analyze client manufacturing processes, pain points, and technical requirements. Design and present customized solution architectures, demonstrating how our CIM platform integrates AI capabilities to address operational efficiency, yield management, and Industry 4.0 objectives. Develop and deliver high-impact proofs of concept (POCs), technical presentations, and product demonstrations tailored to both technical and executive audiences. Create detailed technical proposals, RFP/RFI responses, and solution documentation that clearly articulate value propositions, implementation approaches, and competitive advantages. Serve as the primary technical advisor throughout the sales cycle, building credibility and managing technical objections. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence to inform product strategy and roadmap priorities. Essential Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in Industrial Engineering, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field. 5+ years of combined experience in a presales/solutions architecture role AND hands-on implementation or engineering within manufacturing systems (MES, EAP, CIM, SCADA, or related platforms). Strong foundational understanding of semiconductor manufacturing processes (e.g., Fab, Assembly & Test, PCB) or related high-precision industries. Excellent presentation, communication, and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value. Professional fluency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is mandatory for client engagements and internal collaboration. Willingness to travel internationally (approximately 70-90%) to support regional sales activities, conferences, and key client meetings. Preferred Qualifications Direct experience with Industry 4.0 standards, digitization projects, or AI/ML applications in a production environment. Practical knowledge of key technologies such as SQL, data modeling, system integration (APIs, SEMI standards), and cloud infrastructure. Proven ability to thrive in a global, cross-cultural team environment.

Setting the requirements for raw materials from suppliers and monitoring their compliance. Supervising inspectors, technicians, and other staff members and providing guidance. Ensuring legal obligations are followed and ensuring compliance with regulatory bodies and with health and safetyguidelines. Overseeing product development procedures to identify any deviations from quality standards. Inspecting the final output, comparing it to the requirements, and approving or rejecting the final products. Keeping accurate documentation and performing statistical analysis. Gaining feedback from the clients, attending meetings, submitting reports, and assisting external auditors and inspectors. Qualifications: Previous experience in production or manufacturing. In-depth knowledge of quality control procedures and legal standards. Strong knowledge of mathematics, data analysis, and statistical methods. Excellent communication and listening skills. Good technical and IT skills.
