title: T&C Engineer - Electrification
description: T&C Engineer - Electrification Projects Navsar Global is a premier specialist services firm, connecting top-tier talent with leading companies in critical infrastructure sectors such as power generation, data centres, and oil & gas. We are currently seeking a highly skilled and dedicated T&C Engineer to join our client's dynamic team, focusing on impactful Electrification Projects. The Role: As a T&C Engineer, you will be a pivotal member of the project team, coordinating daily testing and commissioning workflows on-site. You will take full ownership of all testing activities for complex Electrification Projects, ensuring meticulous adherence to defined standards, customer requirements, economic objectives, quality benchmarks, schedule timelines, and stringent Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) protocols. Key Responsibilities: Collaborate effectively with internal teams, various departments, clients, and system-wide contractors and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities seamlessly. Actively participate in critical meetings such as Works Train Notice Meetings, Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings, and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e and HGBD works). Prepare and submit necessary documentation, including SDAR (Site Daily Activity Report) and TAR (Track Access Request), to secure track access and ensure testing proceeds on schedule. Lead and manage commissioning activities across the entire lifecycle, from Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT), Pre-Site Acceptance Testing (PRE-SAT), Site Acceptance Testing (SAT), System Integration Testing (SIT), through to final handover to the client and successful service commencement. Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, detailed step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork. Develop and implement robust systems and platforms for monitoring project progress on a weekly basis, providing timely feedback to the project planner as assigned. Supervise and provide hands-on support to installation teams for SKD RT A&A (Installation activities) at the site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient working environment. Assist the Manager in preparing comprehensive work schedule programs. Undertake ad-hoc duties assigned by the Manager that are critical to the project's success. Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative effort. Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate. Exhibit strong decision-making capabilities in fast-paced project environments. Provide support to other project teams as and when required to achieve overall project objectives. Qualifications: Education & Training: High School Diploma or equivalent. Diploma in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical Engineering. Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) in Electrical/Electronic Engineering is highly preferred. Experience: Minimum of 3 years of hands-on working experience in Electrification projects. Proven track record of taking responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and outcomes. Demonstrated experience in using requirements traceability. Knowledge of Siemens electrification solutions is a significant advantage. Experience in reviewing and updating drawings, specifications, Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) plans, FAT reports, and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) reports. Hands-on experience with high voltage and secondary voltage test equipment in T&C works. Strong understanding of safety significance in the operation and maintenance of High, Medium, and Low Voltage systems, as well as Traction Power Systems in a railway environment, with safety as the highest priority. Proven ability to take complete responsibility for Site Acceptance Testing (SAT/ITC/TTR). Hands-on experience or knowledge of PLC systems (e.g., Siemens, Mitsubishi). Knowledge in ETTS/TSSS systems. Technical Competencies: Basic knowledge of competitor systems in the market. Basic understanding of Functional Safety (IEC 61508) fundamentals and safety functions within core components. Proficiency in Global Business English, both speaking and writing, to facilitate clear international communication. Strong communication skills, including active listening, clarifying, interpreting, and adapting to various situations and personalities. Basic knowledge and application of virtual communication and collaboration tools. Advanced understanding of internal and external laws and regulations to ensure ethically proper business acumen; ability to advise colleagues on compliance-related issues. Basic knowledge of contract formats, practices, and policies, understanding when and whom to involve for assistance. Basic understanding and application of information security practices to protect data and systems. Basic knowledge of regional labor laws and regulations, applying them to daily work and knowing escalation procedures. Basic knowledge of the relevant product, system, and service portfolio within the area of responsibility, including ability to suggest process improvements and solve simple technical problems. Basic technical product and system knowledge relevant to the organization, understanding technical differences and utilizing documentation effectively.

title: WSH Manager/ Supervisor (Chemical Plant/ Jurong Island)
description: This role responsible for ensuring the plant complies with environmental, safety, and health (ESH) regulations. This includes implementing and maintaining ESH programs, policies, and procedures to reduce environmental impact, prevent accidents, and ensure a safe workplace. The role involves working with various teams, providing training and guidance, and promoting a strong ESH culture across the organization. The ESH Manager also monitors and reports on ESH performance, coordinates with regulatory authorities, and supports audits and inspections to ensure compliance. Responsibilities: Lead all activities related to the Safety Case in line with MHI (Major Hazard Installation) regulations. Keep plant process safety information up to date. Oversee the ESH budget and spending. Approve purchases of ESH-related consumables and equipment. Ensure compliance with legal, regulatory, and stakeholder requirements. Support major plant shutdown activities. Maintain the legal register and monitor compliance. Organize required assessments (e.g., stack emissions, IHM, noise). Coordinate employee health assessments. Lead risk and EAI assessments for any process changes. Develop, review, and update ESH-related work procedures and SOPs. Handle ad hoc ESH tasks as assigned. Liaise with authorities for licensing or changes in hazardous substance limits. Handle incident investigations and report non-conformities to relevant authorities. Plan and coordinate internal and external ESH training programs. Conduct regular coaching and feedback sessions. Monitor employee performance and ensure required competency levels. Requirements: Degree in ESH, Chemical Engineering, or a related field. Certified WSH Officer (Level C). Certified Confined Space Assessor and Work-at-Height (WAH) Assessor. BizSafe Level 2 certification. At least 5 years of relevant experience, including 3 years in a similar role. Experience in vendor management and ISO systems. Familiar with liaising with government agencies. Proficient in Microsoft Office (intermediate level). Strong leadership skills to manage the ESH team and meet department KPIs. Knowledge of ISO 45001, SS651, and ISO 14001 standards. Skilled in conducting risk assessments and EAI assessments. Experienced in leading accident investigations. Understanding of the ACTA framework. Good planning and organizational skills to manage tasks and resources effectively. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Passionate about workplace safety and health (WSH). Demonstrates integrity and professionalism. Firm yet fair when addressing issues and managing staff. Flexible, proactive, and willing to go the extra mile. Wecruit Pte Ltd | 20C0270 Tew Jie Wei | R22106822

Finalise terms & conditions and prepare contract agreement and/or purchase order to vendor. Monitor and control materials usage to minimize waste and ensure project efficiency. Monitor Project for any variations or additional work scope. Provide technical guidance to vendors during the tender to construction phases Perform quantity take-offs directly from BIM models (Revit / Navisworks / CostX) Maintain 5D BIM cost models and update costs when designs change Qualifications and Requirements Education: Diploma in quantity surveying, Mechanical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field. Preferance with BIM certification (e.g. Autodesk / buildingSMART) Experience: Minimum of [2] years in quantity surveying or a similar role in the construction industry, with specific experience in: Mechanical Wet Systems (CHW, CDW & DR). Mechanical Dry Systems (HVAC & MV Ductwork). Process Wet Systems (PCW, CDA, N2, Drain). Process Dry Systems (Exhaust Ductwork). BIM-based cost management Technical Skills: Proficient in AutoCAD, Revit, or other drafting software. Strong knowledge of SOW and BoQ preparation. title: Principal Software Test Engineer (Lifecycle Management)
description: Job Description Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal Software Test Engineer Life Cycle Management is responsible for developing, coordinating, and implementing software testing policies and procedures, as well as performing system-level verification. This role provides feedback and recommendations to support continuous improvement in software quality. The position requires independent work, a results-driven approach, and offers technical guidance to team members. This position reports to the Senior Manager Design Transfer & Life Cycle Management and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Collaborate with development teams to ensure products meet and exceed end-user expectations. Design, develop, and maintain test scripts for system and user acceptance testing based on product requirements. Execute tests and manage test coverage, including creating test data, producing logs, and reporting issues. Lead troubleshooting and root cause analysis to resolve software-related problems effectively. Drive automation testing initiatives using tools like SpecFlow and programming languages such as C# for efficient processes. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering or Science, with strong knowledge of software verification methods, at least one high-level programming language, and familiarity with automated testing tools. 5 years’ experience in device/embedded software testing, including end-to-end applications interacting with hardware. Proficiency in software development and test methodologies, preferably Agile/Scrum. Experience with medical device quality standards such as IEC 62304, ISO 13485, and ISO 14971, and exposure to cybersecurity domain. Proven ability to collaborate effectively with global teams and stakeholders in a distributed model. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

description: Position Overview: The Quality Control Specialist for the central kitchen plays a pivotal role in ensuring the production of high-quality food products that meet established standards and exceed customer expectations. This position is responsible for monitoring and maintaining quality assurance processes, conducting inspections, and supporting continuous improvement efforts within the central kitchen. Key Responsibilities : 1. Product Inspection: Conduct thorough inspections of all food products at various stages of production, from raw ingredients to finished dishes, to ensure they meet quality and safety standards. 2. Supplier Collaboration: Collaborate with procurement and supplier teams to monitor the quality of incoming ingredients and packaging materials, and implement corrective actions when necessary. 10. Equipment Maintenance: Participate in the maintenance and calibration of quality control equipment and tools used in inspections and testing. Qualifications and Requirements: · Proven experience in food quality control or quality assurance, ideally in a central kitchen or food production setting. · Strong understanding of food safety regulations, including HACCP principles. · Excellent attention to detail and analytical skills. · Effective communication and teamwork abilities. · Proficiency in using quality control tools, such as laboratory equipment and data analysis software. · Problem-solving skills and the ability to drive process improvement. · Knowledge of food production processes and ingredient characteristics. · Willingness to work in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. The Quality Control Specialist for the central kitchen is an essential contributor to ensuring the safety and satisfaction of customers by maintaining consistently high-quality food products. title: Automation Engineer - Testing & Commissioning
description: About Navsar Global: Navsar Global is a leading specialist firm dedicated to connecting top-tier talent with pioneering opportunities across critical infrastructure sectors such as power generation, data centres, and oil & gas. We partner with industry leaders to build the teams that power the future, ensuring precision, reliability, and innovation in every placement. We are looking for Automation Engineer (Testing & Commissioning) to join us in shaping the next generation of industrial excellence in Urban Mobility (LTA/MRT) Role: Automation Engineer - Testing & Commissioning Contract Duration: This is a 12-month contract position, with potential for extension based on project requirements and performance. Location: Singapore (Client Project Sites) Role Overview: As an Automation Engineer specializing in Testing & Commissioning, you will play a pivotal role in ensuring the seamless integration and operational readiness of critical electrification projects. You will take ownership of ETTS/TSSS testing activities, coordinating daily workflow at site while upholding the highest standards of quality, safety (EHS), and project schedule. This role involves managing complex railway power supply projects from communication and control architecture design through to system commissioning. Key Responsibilities: Lead and coordinate daily testing and commissioning activities on-site for electrification projects, ensuring adherence to project timelines and specifications. Take full ownership of ETTS (Emergency Traction Tripping System) and TSSS (Traction Substation SCADA System) testing activities. Manage railway power supply projects, encompassing communication systems, control architecture design, prototyping, integration testing, and comprehensive system commissioning. Collaborate effectively with internal departments, clients, system-wide contractors, and subcontractors to arrange and execute site activities. Actively participate in weekly Works Train Notice Meetings to secure track access and ensure testing is executed on schedule and in compliance with testing standards. Attend Operator / LTA Track work Access meetings and LTA manpower planning meetings (e.g., for C853e), and submit necessary SDAR and TAR documents to gain track access for CCL/NEL works. Support the Project Manager in the preparation of essential project documentation, including Method Statements, Risk Assessments, step-by-step work plans, and other required paperwork. Develop and maintain systems and platforms for weekly project progress monitoring, providing timely feedback to the project planner. Supervise and support installation teams in carrying out Addition & Alteration (A&A) activities on-site, fostering a self-motivated and efficient work environment. Assist the Project Manager in preparing and managing work schedules and programs. Undertake ad-hoc duties as assigned by the Manager, contributing to the overall success of the project. Demonstrate a strong commitment to teamwork, ensuring smooth project completion through collaborative efforts. Be willing to work extended hours, including night shifts (engineering hours) and public holidays, as project demands necessitate. Exercise strong decision-making capabilities to resolve on-site challenges efficiently. Provide support to other project teams as required, fostering a cohesive project environment. Apply expertise in Station RTU, PLCs, and cybersecurity principles relevant to industrial and electrification projects. Coordinate closely with project, interfacing, and T&C teams, as well as external interface partners. Maintain strong professional relationships with clients and vendors. Qualifications & Experience: Bachelor's Degree in Electronics/Electrical Engineering or a related field (e.g., Diploma/Bachelor in Electrical & Electronic or Mechanical field). Minimum of 5 years of progressive experience in automation, PLC, and SCADA systems, with a strong preference for experience in electrification or railway projects. At least 3 years of direct working experience in ETTS/TSS Electrification projects is essential. Demonstrated experience in MRT electrification projects is highly advantageous. Proficiency in Siemens PLC, SCADA, and HMI technology, including practical experience with software such as S7-300/400, ET200SP, SIMATIC STEP7, TIA Portal, and S7-1500. Experience or knowledge of Siemens Protection Relay systems (e.g., SIPROTEC, Sitras MDC, PLC). Familiarity with Emergency Traction Tripping System (ETTS) SIL system. Experience with third-party PLC, SCADA, and software platforms is beneficial. Proven skills in testing and commissioning PLC systems, including interfacing with AC/DC switchgear and traction power station equipment. Strong competency in PLC software development. Preferred knowledge and experience in network technologies such as Ethernet, TCP/IP, MODBUS RTU, PROFIBUS, and PROFINET. Added advantage if you possess expertise in cybersecurity, system architectures, and industrial security. Skills & Attributes: Independent, self-motivated individual with strong initiative and analytical problem-solving skills. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, capable of effective interaction with diverse stakeholders (colleagues, clients, vendors). Ability to take full responsibility and ownership of assigned tasks and project outcomes. Strong understanding of functional safety principles, particularly IEC 61508. Advanced knowledge of compliance with internal and external laws and regulations, ensuring ethically sound business practices. Basic understanding of contract formats, practices, and policies. Awareness of information security best practices and labor laws relevant to daily work. Proficiency in Global Business English, both written and verbal, for clear international communication. A team player willing to collaborate and support colleagues for project success. Why Join Navsar Global? At Navsar Global, we believe in empowering talent to drive innovation. We offer unparalleled opportunities to work on critical, high-impact projects with leading industry players. As part of our network, you'll gain exposure to cutting-edge technologies and contribute to infrastructure that shapes the future. We are committed to your professional growth and success, providing a platform where your expertise is valued and your career can flourish.

Advantages: ·  Experience with Python scripting. ·  Working knowledge of rechargeable battery systems, including Li-ion(NMC), LFP, NiMH. ·  Experience with automated testing and CI/CD tool integration for embedded systems. ·  Experience in implementing communication protocols over one or more interfaces, such as CAN, USB,
Bluetooth or Wi-Fi.

title: Robotics Integration Engineer
description:
Job Title - Robotics Integration Engineer Location - Singapore Start Date – ASAP Contract - 1 year extendable Job Description Develop and support interfaces for robotic arms and gantry robots on
the production floor (e.g. robots handling drive components or transferring units between stations). Integrate these robots with MES and other control systems such that robotic actions (pick, place, assemble, etc.) are triggered by software commands and reported to systems in real time. Work with PLC/SCADA systems that control conveyors, sorters, turn tables, shuttles, and other auxiliary devices;
implement middleware or IoT gateways to bridge PLC signals with the factory IT layer. For example, ensure that when a robot places a unit on a conveyor, the PLC detects it and the MES is immediately updated with the unit’s location. Must Have Skills Experience integrating industrial robotic arms into automated production lines, including using vendor APIs or standards (OPC UA, ROS-I) to command and monitor robots. Solid
understanding of PLC/SCADA systems and how to interface with them –able to interpret and utilize signals from PLCs to synchronize robot actions with conveyors or other equipment. Strong programming skills (C# or Java preferred) for writing middleware that coordinates between robots, PLCs, and higher-level IT systems. Good to Have Skills: Hands-on knowledge of PLC programming or configuration, which aids in troubleshooting and fine-tuning the interface between IT and control systems. Familiarity with robot motion programming and error handling, enabling quicker diagnosis of robot faults or misalignments. Understanding safety standards and interlocks for robotic cells (to ensure the software integration upholds all required
safety stops and guard checks).

title: Regional Applications and Sales Engineer
description: Summary: Due to consistent growth domestically and internationally, Flexicon is seeking an exceptional candidate to join our technical sales team based in Singapore. This is a technical sales role combining deep technical expertise with regional sales development. The ideal candidate is an exceptional communicator with proven expertise in powder handling and bulk material handling systems. They will demonstrate strong technical aptitude and business-oriented mindset to drive growth in the region. The successful candidate will operate in a small, lean team environment, working remotely from the main manufacturing base. The role requires a highly self‑motivated individual with a strong level of self‑discipline. The candidate must be able to work independently while also collaborating effectively with the regional manufacturing base in Australia. Key Duties and Responsibilities: Technical and Application Management: Conduct technical evaluation of customer bulk material handling projects, including review of specifications and development of customised system designs Provide technical guidance on powder handling equipment and system integration, including feasibility studies for complex applications and creation of process flow drawings, project schedules, and system specifications. Prepare and maintain detailed quotations with accurate pricing and supporting technical documentation as project scope evolves. Lead technical discussions and negotiations with customers, acting as a trusted subject matter expert who provides clear professional recommendations. Consultative Sales Management: Develop and grow the regional sales territory (Singapore and Southeast Asia) by identifying and pursuing new business opportunities through a consultative sales approach. Establish and maintain strong relationships with new prospects and existing customers via on-site visits, facility assessments, and regular follow-up supported by accurate CRM pipeline management. Achieve assigned sales targets and revenue growth objectives while maintaining strong on-going relationships with customers. Ability to organise and lead in‑person and / or virtual meetings with geographically dispersed customers and internal teams. Allocate approximately 30 – 40% of working time to field‑based customer engagement within Singapore and key Southeast Asian markets. Requirements: Minimum 5 – 7 years’ of direct experience in powder or bulk material handling systems, with hands on exposure to conveying systems, pneumatic/mechanical transfer, and bulk bag handling solutions At least 3 years’ of direct field sales experience or in supporting field sales, for technical or capital equipment. Ability to demonstrate consultative selling mindset, understanding the technical sales cycle and customers’ buying process. Proven track records of meeting sales targets consistently and with the ability to balance technical accuracy and commercial objectives. Degree in Engineering or related technical field (highly preferred), with solid knowledge of bulk material handling systems and powder properties. Proficiency in Microsoft Office, 2D AutoCAD or process design tools, CRM, and common video conferencing platforms. Strong technical aptitude, analytical and problem-solving skills, excellent communication and presentation ability (fluent English essential; additional regional languages an advantage), and the ability to build trust and long-term relationships. Self-motivated, results-oriented professional with strong organisation and time management skills Able to manage multiple complex projects, work effectively both independently and in a lean team, and adapt to changing business requirements.

title: Director Automation & Systems Integration (Tuas)
description: At ResMed, we’re on a mission to change lives with every breath — and our global supply chain is rapidly transforming to enable growth, resilience, quality, and value at scale. We’re hiring a Director of Automation & Systems Integration to lead the design, standardisation, and deployment of automation and digital-integration platforms across our manufacturing and distribution network. This role builds the automation backbone of our next-generation supply chain; engineering scalable, reliable, and intelligent systems that increase productivity, improve asset performance, and enable scale. The Opportunity As Director of Automation & Systems Integration leads the global engineering organization responsible for robotics, automation, controls, and systems integration across all supply chain sites. You will define automation design principles and standard platforms, and form strategic engineering partnerships with technology providers and Automation OEMS — going well beyond a “smart customer” to co-create, refine, and scale automation solutions across the network. This role integrates automation with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/controls, and digital data layers to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and improved decision making. You’ll work closely with Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Global Logistics & Distribution, Planning, Quality, Digital Supply Chain, and Product Development to ensure automation solutions deliver throughput, reliability, safety, and long-term capacity. This role is foundational to our Network 2030+ vision: a globally consistent, modular, automation-ready network capable of scaling with speed, resilience, and confidence. Key Responsibilities Automation Strategy & Network 2030+ Alignment Build and execute a global automation strategy that accelerates automation as the core productivity driver of our next-generation supply chain. Define standardised automation design principles, modular architectures, controls frameworks, and platform strategies deployable across regions. Develop a multi-year roadmap for robotics, AGVs/AMRs, AS/RS, automated test platforms, and material movement automation aligned to network growth and supply chain transformation. Serve as a strategic partner in evaluating automation opportunities and ensuring investments align with business value, operational needs, and supply chain transformation priorities. Systems Integration & the Digital Thread Lead systems integration of automation assets with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/Controls IoT, and enterprise data systems Establish integration toolkits, templates, interface libraries, and testing protocols to ensure consistency and reliability. Develop and partner to create standard integration architectures, integration toolkits, and interface libraries that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reliability. Partner with Digital Supply Chain teams to connect automation performance data to real-time dashboards, predictive analytics and digital twin, enabling smarter CapEx and planning decisions . Ensure new and existing automation platforms are integrated securely and consistently, enabling faster deployment and reduced engineering rework. Robotics, Automation Platforms & Deployment Excellence Lead engineering evaluation, technology selection, and deployment of robotics, automation technologies across manufacturing and distribution environments (paperless systems, robotics, LSR automation, AGVs/AMRs, material handling systems, automated test and inspection). Govern engineering specifications, vendor selection, lifecycle cost models, standard controls architecture, FAT/SAT protocols, and commissioning excellence. Establish an Automation Center of Excellence to accelerate standardisation, replicate best practice, and scale solutions globally at pace. Champion design-for-replication to enable rapid, reliable deployment of automation platforms across sites. Asset Performance, Reliability & Automation Health Ensure all automation and integrated systems produce accurate, high resolution performance data to support OEE improvement, predictive maintenance, and capacity optimization. Partner with Industrial Engineering and Asset Performance teams to leverage downtime analytics, bottleneck analysis, TPB aligned reliability engineering practices. Embed automation health monitoring, diagnostics, and predictive analytics to improve uptime, throughput, and line stability. Enable real-time and global automation dashboards, linking physical automation performance directly into planning and continuous improvement. New Site Establishment & Automation-Ready Design Define automation and integration requirements for new supply chain sites, ensuring automation readiness from concept to commissioning. Partner with Facility and Industrial Engineering to embed automation early in layout design, utilities planning, safety architecture, and CVS (Commissioning, Validation, Scale) processes. Deploy modular automation solutions that reduce commissioning times, improve CapEx efficiency,  and enable predictable scalability across the network . Leadership, Governance & Capability Building Build a world-class global team of automation, robotics, controls, and systems integration engineers. Establish engineering governance to ensure global standardization, interoperability, and consistent decision-making across all sites. Create training pathways and capability-building programs to develop future-ready, digitally enabled automation talent. Foster a culture grounded in engineering rigor, innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. About You 12–15+ years in automation engineering, robotics, systems integration, or advanced manufacturing engineering, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles. Deep expertise in automation platforms including robotics, AGVs/AMRs, automated test systems, material storage and handling automation, and controls architecture and digital integration (MES, SCADA, PLC, WES/WCS, IoT). Strong manufacturing automation experience is essential; exposure to distribution automation is valuable. Experience with TPM, reliability engineering, or maintenance strategies for automated systems is highly beneficial Demonstrated success delivering global automation deployments in highly regulated, multi-site supply chain environments. Strong understanding of OEE, bottleneck analysis, predictive maintenance, flow modeling, and asset performance improvement. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Automation, Mechatronics, Systems, Electrical, or Manufacturing Engineering; advanced digital or robotics certifications a plus. Exceptional leadership and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders across global supply chain landscapes. Success Metrics Automation Excellence Deliver measurable productivity and flow improvements through scalable automation solutions. Platform Standardization: Drive adoption of standardized automation architectures across the majority of applicable sites within the first 18–24 months. Asset Performance & Reliability Improve uptime and automation reliability through enhanced diagnostics, predictive maintenance, and data-driven monitoring Digital Thread & Integration Velocity Reduce integration time and engineering rework by implementing standard architectures and reusable components. Speed, Scalability & Capital Efficiency Shorten automation deployment timelines at new or expanding sites through modular design and engineering governance. People & Capability Build and sustain a high-performing automation engineering team with strong engagement and development progression. Why Join ResMed This is a rare opportunity to define the automation and integration blueprint of a global, next-generation supply chain. You’ll architect intelligent, connected automation systems that power how we grow, scale, and deliver — enabling extraordinary outcomes for patients worldwide. At ResMed, you’ll join a global team committed to excellence, innovation, and purpose. Together, we’ll build the most integrated, data-driven, and scalable supply chain engineering network in the world.

title: High School Science Teacher
description: REPORTS TO: Middle & High School Principal JOB GOAL: A High School Science teacher at ICS must have a strong understanding of the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) and have experience closing the achievement gap. They must develop engaging and innovative lessons that allow our students to grow academically, socially and emotionally. The teacher should be confident in incorporating technology (including emerging technologies such as GenAI) and hands-on learning experiences into the classroom with enthusiasm and also believe in collaboration with other colleagues, promoting understanding and communication in a diverse learning and cultural environment. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Plan, prepare, and execute appropriate lessons, utilizing appropriate resource materials and activities. Follow the prescribed scope and sequence of curriculum and assessment as scheduled. Employ a variety of instructional aides, methods, and materials that will promote best practices in order to reach the whole child as stated in the ICS mission statement. Assess student learning against the appropriate standards on a regular basis and provide progress reports as required. Identify the needs of students through diagnostic procedures and align instructional objectives to these needs. Effect student learning through mastery of the subject material by utilizing valid teaching techniques to achieve curriculum goals within the framework of the school’s philosophy. Eager to explore and learn about emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, with a commitment to integrating them effectively into the classroom to foster critical thinking and guide students in developing ethical and responsible use. Ensure Biblical Worldwide Integration (BWI) is mapped throughout the curriculum. Complete lesson/unit plans, Atlas, ESO documentation, and PLC expectations within specified time frames. Maintain student records as required by the school. Communicate with parents, students, and other professional staff regarding student progress (behavioral and academic). Follow school policies, procedures, rules, regulations, and guidelines and the provisions of the contract. Maintain proper discipline in the classroom and on the school premises utilizing teacher created discipline plans in conjunction with a school-wide discipline plan. Inform the administration if/when unable to fulfill any duty assigned. Prepare adequate information and materials for a substitute teacher. Plan and lead a student Chapel when called upon. Supervise extracurricular activities, organizations, and outings as assigned. Complete the Christian Philosophy of Education in-service program within the first three years of teaching at ICS. Attend and participate in devotions, teachers’ meetings, evening programs (if involving his or her students or as requested by the administration), and other meetings as assigned. Carry out supervisory duty as assigned (before school, break, lunch, after school). Maintain a classroom in a clean and educationally stimulating environment. Maintain regular and accurate attendance and grade reports that provide comprehensive information for each student’s learning progress. Keep students, parents, and the administration informed of students’ progress or deficiencies, and provide sufficient and timely notice to parents when students fail to meet learning standards. Submit all appropriate reports to the administration, guidance office, and business office as required by school regulations or as requested. Work with the administration in implementing all policies, procedures, and directives governing the operation of the school. Complete professional development programs as required by the administration and/or ICS Board. Maintain teaching credentials as required through sponsoring agencies (state/provincial education department, ACSI, etc.). Acquire and maintain teaching credentials as required by the laws of Singapore, NICS, and/or ICS. Demonstrate the character qualities of enthusiasm, courtesy, flexibility, integrity, kindness, self-control, perseverance, and punctuality. Exemplify strong interpersonal and communication skills. Meet everyday stress with emotional stability, objectivity, and optimism. Develop and maintain rapport with students, parents, and staff by treating others with friendliness, open-mindedness, dignity, and consideration. Respectfully submit to and demonstrate loyalty to constituted authority. Maintain a personal appearance that models cleanliness, modesty, good taste, and is in agreement with school policy. QUALIFICATIONS: Bachelor’s Degree Teaching credential from a US state, Canadian province or from ACSI (exceptions will be approved by the Academic Board) Expertise in working with students and teaching Advanced Placement (AP) courses Knowledge of specific subject(s)

If you have experience that aligns with the responsibilities of this role, we encourage you to apply. As one of the top five life science companies, we are a global leader in developing, manufacturing, and marketing a broad range of high-quality research and clinical diagnostic products. We help people everywhere live longer, healthier lives. Recently voted a Best Place to Work, Bio-Rad offers a unique employee experience with collaborative teams that span the globe. Benefits: Bio-Rad's biggest asset is our people, and this is the reason why our Total Rewards deliver programs that provide value, quality, and inclusivity while satisfying the diverse needs of our evolving workforce. Our offerings serve to enrich the overall health, wealth, and wellbeing of our employees and their families through the various stages of an employee’s work and life cycle. We’re proud to offer comprehensive medical plans and insurance, flexible benefits, wellness programs, extensive learning and development opportunities and more. EEO Statement: Bio-Rad is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer, and we welcome candidates of all backgrounds. Veterans, people with disabilities, and people of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, and orientations are encouraged to apply. title: Software Developer (Learning Sciences & Assessment Department) [NIE]
description: ABOUT THE NATIONAL INSTITUTE OF EDUCATION (NIE) The National Institute of Education (NIE), Singapore , is Singapore’s national teacher education institute and we are proud to be an integral part of the nation’s education service. We play a key role in the preparation of teachers and in the provision of teacher professional and school leadership development programmes. We are committed to our vision of being An Institute of Distinction: Leading the Future of Education and our mission to Inspire Learning, Transform Teaching and Advance Research . Read more about NIE here . NIE invites applications for the position of part-time software developer for the project “PhET-style simulations to promote AI literacy”. The project’s goal is to develop a curated library of interactive, high-quality online resources for teachers that puts NIE at the forefront of AI literacy. To work towards this goal, the project will start out by developing customizable web-based simulations and corresponding avatar-based narrative instructional videos catering to three fundamental topics in AI literacy - Personalization using Machine Learning and Neural Networks , Ethical Issues in AIED , Human-AI Collaboration. These simulations would allow student teachers to experiment with relevant critical task features in a hands-on manner, thus improving their understanding of abstract technical concepts in AI and the implications of their usage in AI systems for education. Project evaluation would focus on usability evaluation and learning evaluation of the programmed simulations. Facilitation resources and validated design guidelines for developing effective AI literacy simulations will be made available to educational stakeholders upon project completion. This is a contract position till 30 November 2026. Responsibilities Gathering requirements for simulation design from domain experts Agile prototyping of mock UI/UX interfaces for the simulations Programming and iteratively refining the simulations based on expert feedback and usability testing Building data logging capabilities in the designed simulations Developing facilitation guides to assist stakeholders’ work with designed simulations Working with NIE’s IT teams to host simulations securely and ensure seamless interoperability with existing NIE platforms Managing the project and ensuring timely completion of milestones Requirements Essential A Polytechnic Diploma Full stack software development and debugging experience Knowledge of computer graphics and interface design Fluent in coding with generative AI Solid understanding of fundamental machine learning and AI techniques Creativity, good communication skills and ability to work as team player Able to work independently with minimal supervision Preferred Work, teaching, or corporate training experience in AI for education Familiarity with AI literacy efforts worldwide, in APAC and Singapore Up-to-date with latest advancements in generative AI (for education) Passion for improving teaching and learning with technology Application We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Other Information NIE staff can take chartered buses at their own expense from or near their home to the NIE campus. This is subject to the availability of seats.

 Management Science conferences and journals. On our faculty are several
 world-class scholars and rising stars in their respective disciplines. We are committed

 generous support for high-quality research proposals. Our Education. SCIS offers the Ph.D. in Information Systems and Ph.D. in Computer Science programmes. Ph.D. students can

 management science oriented Information Systems & Management research
 area. SCIS launched a
 Professional Doctoral Degree, Doctor
 of Engineering (EngD) in 2020. The
 programme aims to train students to become IT leaders with deep technical
 expertise for innovating, designing and managing complex IT systems. SCIS also
 offers Master of IT in

 analytics, technological platforms and business strategies, the MITB programme
 delves into five specialisation tracks: Financial Technology & Analytics, Data
 Science & Analytics, Artificial Intelligence, Digital Transformation and
 Cybersecurity. Each of these tracks uniquely resources students to lead
 competently and decisively . Additionally, the
 undergraduate programmes offerings consist of the B.Sc.
 (Computer Science) , B.Sc.
 (Information Systems), B.Sc.
 (Computing & Law) and B.Sc. Software Engineering programmes. The programmes have been remarkably successful in
 demonstrating educational innovations and creating a culture of learning,
 establishing external linkages and partnerships with industry, government and
 the social sector, and with job placement. We have an entrepreneurial student
 body, and many SCIS undergraduates have gone on to create or participate in start-ups . Job Requirement Candidate

      degree in a closely related discipline from a well-recognized university . o   For example, a
 Masters or PhD in Computer Science, Information Technology, Information
 Systems, Statistics, Management Science, Decision Sciences, Marketing
 Analytics, Computational Physics, Computational Engineering or Science, and
 the like. Outstanding
      communication skills A

title: Principal Engineer, Supplier Development
description: Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture. ​ For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving.  But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world.  Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. Job's mission As a key member of our Global Supplier Development team, this role supports the organization's objective to strengthen supplier capability, elevate design and manufacturing performance, and ensure long-term supply resilience across our global network. The mission of the team is to proactively build supplier maturity, drive continuous improvement, and align supplier capability growth with mid- and long-term category strategies to support product quality, operational excellence, and New Product Introduction (NPI) readiness. What you will be working on Assess supplier quality and technology gaps, and execute plans to address them. Review and influence Design Engineering early in design phase to achieve target costs and DFx. Identify areas for cost down during design phase plus manufacturing methods and eliminate waste. Evaluate supplier processes and risks; collaborate to improve capabilities through lean supplier development methods (i.e. six sigma) and implement mitigation plans. Apply engineering and material science expertise with suppliers to boost capacity, product quality, yield and reduce costs. Partner with Category leaders to drive long-term category strategies to support aggressive production scaling. Align development efforts with long-term category strategies and develop partners within the suppliers. Conduct on-site supplier assessments (capabilities, MSA, PCS/SPCE, etc) and identify gaps during selection. Implement improvement plans with suppliers to ensure readiness and support NPI. Coordinate globally with Business/Key Product Unit Engineering teams to meet end-use specifications. Advance manufacturing capabilities, DFMEA / FMEA, and implement Process Control Systems, PFMEA, at key sites. Support statistical PCS / SPC (lean six sigma) data analysis for internal and external stakeholders. Perform root cause failure analysis and report findings to relevant teams. Lead supplier engagement, support surveillance audits, and qualification activities globally. Independently manage supplier improvement projects, including onboarding training and qualification. Oversee multiple projects to resolve issues and enhance product performance, reliability, and efficiency. Lead / mentor group of Supplier Development team on best practices and develop skillsets to support group growth and expansion What we are looking for MS, or PhD in Engineering, Science, or related technical field (Engineering, Physics, Material Sciences) relevant to semiconductor equipment, or proven expertise. At least 10 years of experience or significant expertise in engineering, designing, and manufacturing of complex mechanical systems. Experience in the semiconductor capital equipment industry with technical background in semiconductor manufacturing technologies required. Strong English communication skills for complex technical topics. Effective collaboration across regions, cultures, and disciplines. Proven Lead Engineer in matrix environments and ability to work independently while managing a team. Proficient in project and time management skills required Drive business process efficiencies and improvements with key internal stakeholders Candidate should have knowledge of the following tools, but will be trained to more advanced levels in: Applied Operational and Engineering Statistical Methods Process Control Systems (PCS) and Statistical Process Control (SPC) FMEA include Design FMEA (DFMEA) and Process FMEA (PFMEA) Structured Problem Solving (8D) and Effective Problem Solving (EPS) Operational Excellence tools including Failure Analysis, Lean Manufacturing, Value Stream Mapping Project Management, Team Management, Escalation Management and Communication Techniques across a broad range of business escalation types, (Customer, Supplier, Technical, Manufacturing, etc.) Should costing estimate modeling (aPriori) Apply today to be part of what’s next. We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn , Facebook , Instagram, X and YouTube . title: Principal FPGA Verification Engineer
description: Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal FPGA Verification Engineer is responsible for leading the development and implementation of comprehensive testing procedures and protocols for FPGA-based systems. This role focuses on verifying and validating the performance, functionality, and integrity of FPGA designs for advanced microscopy products. The successful candidate will leverage their expertise to develop effective verification and test solutions, collaborating closely with the Research and Development team to identify and address issues throughout the product development cycle. Additionally, the engineer will work with cross-functional teams—including Operations, Supply Chain, Quality, and Logistics—to ensure that product quality, reliability, and robustness meet established standards and exceed customer expectations. This position reports to the Verification Manager and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Verification Plan and Report Development: Create detailed verification plans, strategies, test cases, and reports to evaluate FPGA functionality, performance, and compliance with project requirements. Provide clear, concise reports with insights and recommendations for improvement. Verification and Validation: Perform comprehensive verification of FPGA designs using various testing tools and equipment to ensure compliance with specified criteria such as speed, reliability, and power consumption. Issue Identification and Debugging: Identify, record, and diagnose issues in FPGA designs, ensuring thorough documentation and efficient resolution in collaboration with the development team. Automated Testing: Develop and implement automated test procedures to enhance testing efficiency and accuracy. Continuous Improvement and Quality Assurance: Oversee continuous improvement in FPGA design verification and validation processes, contribute to quality assurance by evaluating designs against industry standards, and collaborate with cross-functional teams for seamless integration. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or a related field, with strong knowledge of FPGA technologies, FPGA development tools (Xilinx, Altera, etc.), and FPGA testing methodologies. Proficiency in FPGA testing equipment and software, ensuring accurate and efficient verification processes. Experience with scripting and programming languages for test automation, such as Python, VHDL, and Verilog. Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail, with the ability to diagnose and resolve complex technical issues. Preferred background in the Medical Device industry, bringing familiarity with regulatory and quality standards. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Research Fellow (Food Science & Technology)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The successful candidate will work with Asst Prof PANG Bo on research related to protein-based food materials and processing, under a nationally funded project focused on improving the nutritional and functional performance of alternative protein products. The main responsibilities of the position include: Design and conduct laboratory- and pilot-scale experiments related to protein-based food materials. This includes material preparation, processing, characterisation, and performance evaluation relevant to food applications. Collect, process, and analyse experimental data using appropriate scientific methods and analytical tools. Prepare scientific manuscripts, technical reports, and presentations for dissemination of research findings in peer-reviewed journals and conferences. Provide guidance and supervision to undergraduate and postgraduate students involved in related research projects. Contribute to the daily operations of the laboratory, including equipment use and maintenance, documentation, and adherence to laboratory safety and regulatory protocols. Qualifications PhD in Food Science, Food Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Materials Science, Polymer Science, or a closely related discipline, with strong emphasis on protein-based or biopolymer systems. Skills: Strong expertise in protein chemistry, enzymatic modification, protein–protein interactions, and structure–function relationships; Strong background in soft matter and food biophysics, particularly protein-based structured materials. Hands-on experience with food processing technologies, preferably extrusion or high-shear thermal processing; Hands-on experience with advanced structural characterisation techniques (e.g., scattering methods, SAXS, WAXS, AFM, CLSM, EM); Ability to independently design experiments, analyse complex datasets, and integrate results across multiple techniques; Strong scientific writing and communication skills. Experience: Prior postdoctoral or equivalent research experience in protein-based materials, alternative proteins, or food biopolymers is preferred; Experience working on multi-year, milestone-driven funded projects and contributing to peer-reviewed publications; Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in interdisciplinary research teams and to supervise or mentor junior researchers.

description: Role Overview We are looking for a meticulous and well-structured Physical Operator to manage the operational execution of physical petrochemical trades. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Logistics, Maritime Studies, Business, or a related discipline. 3–5 years of hands-on experience in physical operations within the petrochemical or energy trading sector. Solid understanding of international shipping practices and trade documentation workflows. Working knowledge of vessel operations, charter party terms, and port procedures. Strong communication skills with a proven ability to organize, prioritize, and resolve issues effectively. Desired Attributes Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy. description: Micron Technology, Inc. is seeking an Electrical Engineer for our Global Facilities Engineering department. Based in Boise, this role provides a unique chance to influence the future of facilities engineering globally, ensuring our world-class facilities operate flawlessly and sustainably. Responsibilities and Tasks Provide Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Serve as the Technical Field Authority, offering consultation and support to Global, Regional, and Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate strictly with internal partners, including the Technology Development Team, Procurement, Finance, and Legal, to determine and successfully implement engineering solutions. Develop, review, and maintain Micron's global standards, specifications, and best-known methods (BKMs). Ensure standardization and alignment across Micron's global network for engineering and design concepts, construction project execution, reliability programs, and operations and maintenance practices. Analyze system designs to identify improvements for better performance, efficiency gains, reduced maintenance, and cost savings. Provide problem analysis support using standard tools and methodologies. Compare engineering standard methodologies used within Micron and across the industry. Stay up-to-date with brand new technical knowledge in your subject area, including industry trends, technological advances, design innovations, and sustainability. Develop, review, and maintain global design and construction standards and specifications. Develop system design concepts for deployment in design and construction projects. Ensure effective communication with local facilities, design, construction, procurement, and manufacturing partners to achieve project objectives. Review design packages and provide detailed feedback promptly. Support cost and schedule estimates and justifications for approvals. Supervise and coordinate consultant resources as needed. Ensure scope meets customer needs and organizational change procedures are followed. Coordinate progress and provide construction support as required. Ensure designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Review databases and system operating trends to assess network capacity and validate utilization and diversity factors. Lead capacity and scenario planning across the network, evaluating system and distribution capacity impacts. Provide Global Initiatives Program Support, Leadership, and Expertise Develop, review, and maintain global best-known methods (BKMs) centered around operations and maintenance reliability and performance. Lead global facilities programs and initiatives, defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead global sustainability programs, including emissions, energy, water, wastewater, and green infrastructure design pillars, supporting Micron’s corporate sustainability program. Collect and consolidate lessons learned and feedback to incorporate into global standards and BKMs. Minimum Qualifications: Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience in Electrical Engineering. Minimum 7 to 15 years of experience in the semiconductor and facilities-related industry or other process industry. Technical expertise in facilities and semiconductor electrical systems, including high, medium, and low voltage systems, critical and emergency backup systems, sag mitigation systems, uninterruptible power supplies, variable frequency drives, lighting, and electrical distribution systems. Ability to travel internationally 25% of the time on average, with up to 50% during critical project phases or site activities. Preferred Qualifications: Proven ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks concurrently. Outstanding organizational skills and customer-focused attitude. Excellent interpersonal and team-working skills; proficiency in English required. Self-motivated and driven to excel. Familiarity with semiconductor manufacturing and facilities systems.

Job Description The National University of Singapore invites applications for the position of Research Fellow, Research Associate, or Research Assistant in the Department of Computer Science, School of Computing (SoC). SoC is strongly committed to research excellence in all its dimensions: Searching for fundamental results and insights, developing novel computational solutions to a wide range of applications, building large-scale experimental systems and improving the well-being of society. We seek to play an active role both internationally and locally in the core and emerging areas of Computer Science and Information Systems The successful candidates will work closely with Principal Investigator Prof. Tulika Mitra on a recently funded Green AI project. This ambitious research initiative targets energy-efficient AI inference, co-optimizing across the full stack - from algorithms to architecture. The goal is to rethink data center computation and communication systems to make AI workloads significantly more sustainable. The ideal candidate will have experience in computing systems, such as ML systems, computer architecture, FPGA/GPU/accelerator design, compilers, or operating systems. We are looking for individuals who are passionate about systems-level innovation, eager to work across abstraction layers, and motivated to push the frontiers of sustainable and scalable AI Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. title: Laboratory Technician (GMP Cell Culture & Processing Operations)
description:
Job Title: Laboratory Technician (GMP Cell
Culture & Processing Operations) Job Description: We are seeking a meticulous, detail-oriented, and hands-on Laboratory Technician to perform cell culture, laboratory, manufacturing and production activities in a GMP-compliant facility. The
successful candidate will be responsible for executing routine laboratory and quality control procedures according to established Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) using aseptic techniques where applicable, maintaining proper documentation, assisting in commercial product manufacture and packaging, and performing other laboratory activities including but not limited to housekeeping, reagent preparation, equipment and facility maintenance. Key Responsibilities: Perform all designated activities strictly according to established SOPs and using aseptic techniques where necessary. Perform cell culture and other laboratory activities including but not limited to quality control, downstream processing, and bioassays. Operate and maintain cleanliness of laboratory equipment and facility. Prepare, label, and document reagents according to specifications and quality standards. Where necessary, assist in manufacture, dispensing, packaging and labelling of commercial products following strict instruction, ensuring product quality and safety, packaging integrity, labelling accuracy and traceability. Housekeeping activities e.g. washing labware, inventory management, waste disposal Maintain accurate and complete documentation in logbooks, batch records, and checklists. Support environmental monitoring, inventory management, and routine equipment maintenance as needed. Support continuous improvement initiatives to enhance quality, workflow, safety, and compliance. Requisite Skills and Qualifications: Education: Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Biotechnology, Biomedical Science, Biochemistry, or a related life science field. Technical Skills (Optional but
preferred): Basic experience in mammalian cell culture and aseptic techniques with experience in cell culture and laboratory reagent preparation. Basic experience in basic laboratory techniques such as aseptic techniques, pipetting, reagent preparation, and equipment operation & maintenance with sound understanding of cleanroom or biosafety practices. Able to perform routine repetitive work with precision and consistency in controlled environments. Able to understand and adhere to GMP/GLP concepts, documentation and compliance requirements. Able to follow SOPs with precision, with good documentation skills and attention to detail. Soft skills · Meticulous, disciplined, and consistent in following procedures. Strong hands-on ability and able to perform repetitive tasks. Able to work both collaboratively and independently under supervision and manage
time effectively. Quality and safety-conscious mindset Organized, with good communication skills and a proactive learning attitude. Able to work extended hours including holidays and weekends when required. ***Immediate availability preferred. Fresh graduates welcome to apply. What We Offer Training in GMP and aseptic processing. A collaborative and supportive team in a growing biotechnology company. Exposure to product manufacturing processes. Career progression pathway. How to Apply Interested candidates are invited to submit their resume and a short cover letter to yokkim@celligenics.com or application link. Please include “ Laboratory Technician
Application ” in the subject line.

title: Facility Engineer (Sustainability)
description:
About the Role We are seeking a Facility Engineer with sustainability experience to support the operation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of building
facilities, with a strong focus on energy efficiency, resource optimisation,
and sustainable facility management practices . The successful candidate will work closely with operations, vendors, and
stakeholders to ensure facilities are maintained to high standards while
supporting the organisation’s sustainability and compliance objectives. Key Responsibilities Facility Operations & Maintenance Oversee day-to-day facility operations including M&E systems (e.g. HVAC, electrical, plumbing, fire protection). Coordinate and supervise term contractors and service providers to ensure compliance with contractual requirements. Conduct routine inspections, preventive maintenance, and corrective works to ensure operational reliability. Assist in troubleshooting facility issues and implementing effective solutions. Sustainability & Resource Management Support sustainability initiatives such as energy efficiency, water conservation, and waste reduction programmes. Monitor and analyse utility consumption data (electricity, water, gas) and identify opportunities for optimisation. Assist in the implementation and tracking of sustainability KPIs and performance metrics . Support audits, certifications, and reporting related to sustainability standards (e.g. Green Mark, ISO 14001 / ISO 50001 / ISO 46001 where applicable). Compliance & Documentation Ensure compliance with statutory regulations, safety requirements, and environmental standards . Maintain proper documentation, reports, and records for facility operations and sustainability initiatives. Support internal and external audits, inspections, and assessments. Stakeholder & Project Support Liaise with internal stakeholders to understand operational needs and improvement opportunities. Support minor upgrading works, sustainability improvement projects, and pilot initiatives. Prepare technical reports proposals, and improvement recommendations when required. Requirements Diploma or Degree in Facilities Management, Building Service, Mechanical/Electrical Engineering, Environmental Engineering , or related discipline. Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in facility management, building operations, or sustainability-related roles. Basic working knowledge of M&E systems and building operations. Exposure to sustainability practices , energy management, water efficiency, or environmental compliance. Familiarity with local regulations and standards (e.g. BCA, NEA, WSH) is an advantage. Good analytical, documentation, and coordination skills. Able to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. Preferred / Advantageous Skills Experience supporting Green Mark, ISO 14001, ISO 50001, ISO 46001 , or similar frameworks. Experience with utility monitoring systems , sustainability reporting, or audits. Basic data analysis skills (Excel or equivalent). Strong communication and stakeholder management skills. What We Offer Opportunities to work on sustainability-driven facility initiatives . Exposure to operational, engineering, and sustainability projects. Career development within the facilities and sustainability domain . Competitive remuneration and benefits.

title: Senior Scientist (AFST/PCP), SIMTech
description: ABOUT THE ROLE We are seeking a motivated individual to fill the position of Research Scientist in the Integrated Composite Processing Section.  This position requires the applicant to lead and conduct research in advanced  fibre-reinforced composites forming and moulding technology, study and  characterization of composite structures. The scientist is required to support  both industry and R&D projects where process innovation, structural testing  and material characterizations are needed. Experience in project management,  grant writing and patent applications will be advantageous. Basic knowledge of  material circularity, CAD design, finite-element analysis and algorithm  development is required. JOB DESCRIPTION Research on advanced short-fibre reinforced thermoplastic composites in  multiple material systems. Working with a team of scientists and engineers to enhance the capability of  compression moulding technology. Formulate scientific research direction and innovative proposals to secure  competitive funding. Understand the advanced manufacturing domain and provide innovative  solutions to industry problems in the area. Familiar with CAD and CAE tools, SolidWorks, etc. Deep knowledge of a wide range of forming technologies of metal, composites,  and polymer materials Conduct mechanical tests, testing tool/jig design and material  characterizations Develop and test innovative solutions to optimize composite compression  moulding process technologies Prepare project progress update presentation for customers Prepare research project proposals, presentations, and reports, conduct  R&D experiments and file technology disclosures Support in the process equipment set-up, laboratory operations, and  inventory management for export control items Keep a good record of production work orders, laboratory data, methods,  results, drawings, etc Able to perform well in both industry-type and research-type  projects JOB REQUIREMENT Ph.D. in Aerospace/ Mechanical/ Material Science Engineering, etc. Candidates with strong fundamentals in material science and mechanical  engineering are welcome Experienced with composite moulding machine operations and process  troubleshooting Good track record of scientific publications in top journals and  conferences We value the following soft skills: Excellent technical presentation and scientific writing skills Can-do attitude and ability to work with different needs of various  stakeholders Proactive team player and technically motivated to solve problems and  discover new research topics. Competent in the use of 3D modelling, process simulation and FE structural  analysis will be a bonus Meticulous, fine attention to detail and technically inclined towards  process and laboratory work. Willing to learn new skills. Comfortable with customer/vendor interactions and handling project  negotiations
