title: Discipline Manager
description: LOCATION: Ayer Rajah Crescent, Singapore ABOUT THALES Thales established its presence in Singapore in 1973 to support the expansion of aerospace-related activities in the Asia-Pacific region. Throughout the last four decades, the company has grown from strength to strength and is today involved in the primary businesses of Aerospace (including Air Traffic Management), Defense & Security and Digital Identity & Security. Thales today employs over 2,100 people in Singapore across all its business areas. ABOUT AMS The Airspace Mobility Solutions (AMS) business, part of the Global Business Unit Land and Air Systems, provides leading edge Air Traffic Management (ATM), Unmanned Traffic Management (UTM), Security and Digital Aviation solutions for the Commercial and Defence sectors. The AMS business manages 11% of the world’s airspace and with revenues of over 500 million Euros worldwide, is a global market leader in airspace management solutions. In Singapore, the business focuses on the delivery and support of key ATM solutions. This role is responsible for leading the Functional Team consisting of Systems, Infrastructure, and Speciality engineers. ROLE DESCRIPTION SUMMARY This role is responsible for leading the Functional Team consisting of Systems, Infrastructure, and Speciality engineers. The Discipline Manager is accountable for enhancing engineering practices, developing team capability, and ensuring the effective application of engineering disciplines across projects to meet business objectives. • Improve the engineering discipline practices (process and tools) • Strengthen our focus on people development • Ensure the sharing and capitalisation of knowledge across the organisation • Enforce balance between engineering discipline and delivery KEY ACTIVITIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES As a Discipline Manager, you are accountable for: • Contribute and support the Engineering Manager in building a clear vision and direction for Engineering initiatives and communicate to the team • Contribute to the Systems, Infrastructure and Speciality Engineering Discipline Governance • Define and tailor discipline practices (process and tools) • Ensure the capitalisation and sharing of knowledge • Contribute to the discipline efficiency plan to increase business competitiveness • Promote adoption of new technologies • Be responsible for the engineering discipline cost model definition and updates • Assess skills required for current and future projects needs including future discipline trends • Development/upskilling of the team and career management • Conduct strategic workforce planning including the provision of resources to fulfil current and future bid and project needs • Roll-out Make Team Buy strategy for the forecasted workload (incoming activities) • Define and Manage Hiring strategy and recruitment, onboarding • Foster collaboration with other disciplines to define multi-discipline solutions with added value • Foster an innovation and entrepreneurship culture KEY KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND EXPERIENCE To be successful in this role, you will have demonstrated the following competencies and experience. We encourage applications from candidates who can demonstrate the required skills and knowledge, which may have been acquired through various career paths. Qualifications • Degree in Engineering, IT or related discipline • Postgraduate studies in Systems Engineering is advantageous. Essential Experience • Proven experience in Systems Engineering lifecycle management (10+ years) including; Requirements, Design, Integration and Verification and Validation • Proven experience in the development of products and projects in a variety of methodologies, with a particular focus on iterative/agile development • Proven experience (5+ years) in managing and coaching staff • Demonstrated ability to achieve effective outcomes in a multidiscipline, multi-culture environment • Experience with Safety, Security and Human Factors standards and practices • Experience with Infrastructure design processes • Experience with Network design and deployment processes • Ability to develop and promote high performing teams • Develop and foster a culture of continuous improvement • Coaching and sharing of knowledge and expertise • Ability to engage senior stakeholders having different objectives • Accountability, transparency and curiosity • A positive attitude combined with excellent interpersonal and motivational skills. • An ability to successfully manage change • Comfort in facing and managing complexity • Sound judgement and independent decision-making capability where necessary • Excellent written and oral communication skills in English Desirable: • Good knowledge of Air Traffic Management systems GENERAL / SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS • Assist in ensuring a harmonious work environment in all departments that you are working with by upholding Thales' key values. • An advocate for diversity and inclusion who will be actively involved in implementing change initiatives to achieve our diversity goals • An advocate for a culture of continuous improvement • An advocate for accountability, transparency and curiosity • Comply with all relevant company Occupational Health, Safety and Environmental policies and framework and work practices with the intent of preventing or minimising accidental exposures to self, colleagues, visitors and/or the environment and to ensure a safe work practises at all times. • Comply with all relevant Company policies and procedures. SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS • Occasional international travel may be required

title: TA Planner & Execution Engineer (E&I) - Bukom
description:
Your role Our major Oil & Energy Downstream Client is urgently looking for Turnaround Planner & Execution Engineer who will be responsible for planning and an executing turnaround activity safely, efficiently and in accordance with applicable premises, work processes, and standards. You will also provide support to the site electrical infrastructure, AI & MM Projects and MOC work planning and implementation. Your role includes all aspects of TA phases from strategic planning, preparation and execution which includes shutdown & decon, maintenance & project execution, commissioning & restart, and lookbacks  & closure for learning and future planning. You will lead electrical expenditure of the Turnaround including projects for various TA complexities. You will typically plans and executes the electrical works for a multi-block event. Each event may involve 18~24 months preparation period leading to a 1-3 month turnaround execution. Principal Accountabilities Electrical activities related to turnarounds and other shutdowns in a safe and efﬁcient manner, and in accordance with applicable premises, approved work processes, and standards. Familiar with all TA work processes requirements. Act always in accordance with the policy of health, safety, security & environment and plan for TA activities accordingly. Ensure all HSSE requirements, Technical, and Process Safety Standards, and local requirements i.e., Energy Market Authority Act, Electricity Act, and local Code of Practices are considered in the TA Planning process and comply during execution of work. To ensure sites assets operate safely & reliably, without suffering any process safety incident or causing life-changing events to person(s), the staff is accountable to ensure compliance to those Critical Activities assigned for the Job Position. To deliver effective TA and Project planning for all Projects and TA related activities (Maintenance, Operations, and Projects) for electrical works. Completes estimate of Direct Field Labour (DFL) hours, overheads, materials, and equipment for assigned Event for associated works. Gather scope/work packages with detailed information for planning and execution. Gather scope/work packages with detailed information for planning and execution. Lead comprehensive alignment on electrical scope optimization. Data entry/import of detail job plans and estimates into scheduling tool. Support Management of Change (MOC) process for associated TA activities, including the preparation & implementation of logistic and environment plans and necessary temporary facilities, as required. Identiﬁcation, review & escalation of high-risk activities and communicates key issues & risks to the TA Event Manager. Identify and order long-lead items, tools, equipment, materials/parts and secure services/resources required for TA execution. Ensure all relevant technical, safety & quality requirements are clearly incorporated into the deliverables. Create bills of material for assigned work, requisition material and ensure all materials are delivered before feed-out of unit. Work with block lead(s) and TA Schedulers to ensure TA Event scope is fully integrated into block plan within Primavera and being optimized into the TA Event’s integrated master schedule. Field
     Coordination: Act as ﬁeld coordinator during the execution of an event –
     coordination with contractors, update progress schedule, responsible for
     quality assurance aspects and completion status, verify daily Value of
     Work Done (VOWD), management of TA ﬁles & records, participate in
     lessons learned & look back process reviewing for improvements in
     terms of planning & scheduling. During planning stage, the planners will work on Electrical disciplines related planning activities to enhance the dual skills capability, he/she needs to undergo gap assessment according to Job Competency Proﬁle and attend training(s) for gap closure. During execution, the candidate is competent to carry out electrical infrastructure maintenance activities Upload all infrastructure equipment service reports to SharePoint as records. Management of (scope) Change report back on daily schedule and work with Operations, Discipline Engineers and TA Coordinators on changes. Develops, plans and integrates associated addendum/discovery works into Primavera schedule. TA KPIReport back on daily schedule/activities to support monitor and report TA KPI. TA Budget Support cost management initiatives where applicable – forecast cost and workforce requirements, implement agreed TA contracting strategy & contingency cost, validate/challenge invoiced cost. Upkeep and manage TA Event files and records across all TA phases, including post Event system/record updating. Provides constructive critics of TA Event activities after completion; contribute towards Lesson Learnt/After Action Reviews lookback processes. Working with site SME to develop skill sets for equipment unique to the individual site. Carry out any other related duties as required, including temporary assignments in other sections within the MTAC department on site. You are what we are looking for if you: Have Degree/Diploma or equivalent in Electrical Engineering Have minimum 8-10 years relevant working experience in electrical construction/maintenance Are an Authorized Electrical Person (AEP) in M site Have completed HV Operation Course by EMA Have LEW qualiﬁcation preferably Have Oil/petrochemical industry safety course for supervisors.

title: Principal Electronics Engineer
description: Position Overview: The Principal Electronics Engineer is a senior leadership role within EOS Defence Systems (EOS DS) and is responsible for solving problems using disciplined engineering techniques (e.g., software, mechanical, electronics, system, testing, project etc) to assure and advise on the development and/or delivery of products. The Principal Electronics Engineer possess deep technical and industry knowledge with the ability to influence and guide company direction, ensuring strategic alignment. The Principal Electronics Engineer is also responsible for developing products and designs that support the product development and product goals of EOS DS. The Principal Electronics Engineer will lead and mentor engineers, up to and including Senior Engineers. They may provide technical assistance to multiple teams and provide a high level of support to those teams. In consultation with senior leadership, suggest staffing and workflow priorities and monitor and manage performance, health and wellbeing. The Principal Electronics Engineer will have extensive engineering experience across the product lifecycle in a Defence environment, allowing them to provide leadership across engineering teams and execute technical authority as allocated. Primary Duties: Specific Functions & Tasks Provide necessary guidance and direction to produce electronics designs and analysis that satisfy defined product development requirements in the specified time frames and exercise allocated technical authority. Work with systems engineers to ensure all requirements are identified, captured and analysed. Lead conceptual design and schematic capture of complex electronic circuits. Ensure test and evaluation activities on electronics and system designs are undertaken to verify and sign-off of all design requirements. Initiate and/or participate in planning on electronics designs. Develop, review and champion the electronics engineering process within EOS DS. Ensure the preparation and review of all documentation to support designs. Perform conceptual, preliminary and detailed design of complex electronics assemblies. Simulate analogue and digital circuits. Document designs including calculations, design options and decisions. Prepare for and contribute to design reviews. Perform test, evaluation, characterisation, verification & validation of electronic subsystems in collaboration with test engineers. Prototype electronic circuits, wiring harnesses and other systems. Work closely with other engineering disciplines to ensure cohesive and complete electronic designs are delivered on time and on budget and meet project requirements. Apply DFx principles (manufacturability, testability, excellence). Review work and provide guidance and mentorship to Senior Electronics Engineers. Engage key stakeholders and subject matter experts across technical engineering and specialty areas to understand the concept of operations and user needs to elicit and decompose electrical requirements. Adhere to and ensure understanding and compliance with company procedures to achieve a safe and secure work environment. Other duties as required. Strategy and Architecture Contribute to and influence strategy for emerging, new, and current technologies, products, services within Defence Systems and have authority over and accountable for the innovation and delivery practices required to assure the implementation of this strategy. Assess and ensure a high level of leadership and deep industry, defence system knowledge and systems engineering best practice to maintain competitive advantage, exploit EOS DS technologies and minimise exposure and risk. Communicate, advocate and influence persuasively through effective negotiation and communication while fostering an environment of innovation, learning and continuous improvement within and outside the organisation. Development and implementation of Engineering Self-initiated and managed under broad direction and governance to be responsible for demonstrably leading self and others to produce designs and products that satisfy defined requirements in the specified time frames and meet group objectives. Undertake cohesive best practice designs and analysis and develop efficient work practices to support product development activities and business outcomes. Lead and work collaboratively with engineers across functional teams to solve complex problems as a team and ensure all requirements are identified, captured, and analysed such that the design/product can be developed that satisfies the safety, quality, policy, legislative and design authority requirements. Undertake clear and pragmatic tests, evaluation, and verification activities on integrated system designs and prototypes to verify and provide recommendations for sign-off of all design/product requirements to the Chief Engineer and Design Authorities based on relevant legislative requirements, standards, and contracts. Prepare and review all documentation required to support designs and provide tailored advice and recommendations to teams and decision makers. Relationship and engagement Actively listen, share knowledge and experience, provide guidance and mentorship, and work collaboratively to mobilise teams across engineering and other relevant internal and external customers to deliver engineering and business outcomes. Work with Production Engineers and Technicians to ensure the best design solutions are delivered to solve production problems and obtain business outcomes under the broad direction of the Chief Engineer. Support the leadership alongside with the EOS Executive, Project Managers, and Project Engineers to ensure design solutions are delivered on time and on budget and meet other project management requirements. Communicate insights and provide thorough engineering judgement for progress and the advancement of engineering solutions. Apply interdisciplinary, collaborative approach to requirements including analysis of functional and non-functional requirements and functional performance specifications to develop effective solutions. Delivery and operation Guide the design and execution of user acceptance testing and other verification activities to provide recommendations to the Chief Engineer and other Design Authorities. Support production and review manufacturing and supplier requirements to meet specified timeframes, engineering standards, and business outcomes. People and skills Work in cross functional multidisciplinary teams to enable self and others to solve engineering problems and implement solutions. Key Attributes: Ethical Leader. Influencer. Deep Technical knowledge. Problem solver. Engineering judgement. Details oriented. Works Collaboratively. Insightful. Communicates well. Inventive. Solution orientated. Team builder Uses experience for sound judgement

title: Principal Software Engineer (C++, Dart)
description: Job Description Job Description Bring more to life. Are you ready to accelerate your potential and make a real difference within life sciences, diagnostics and biotechnology? At Leica Microsystems, one of Danaher’s 15+ operating companies, our work saves lives—and we’re all united by a shared commitment to innovate for tangible impact. You’ll thrive in a culture of belonging where you and your unique viewpoint matter. And by harnessing Danaher’s system of continuous improvement, you help turn ideas into impact – innovating at the speed of life. Shape the Future with Us! At Leica Microsystems, we have been shaping the future for over 175 years with groundbreaking optical and digital solutions.  With a culture rooted in customer focus, innovation, and teamwork, we lead the market in microscopy, imaging, and analysis, unveiling the invisible and empowering our customers to build a better, healthier world. Joining Leica Microsystems means contributing to scientific discoveries and supporting surgeons in making critical decisions. Our advanced microscopes and AI-based image analysis solutions enable users to gain profound insights into development and engineering challenges. Here, you will work on meaningful projects alongside passionate colleagues, driving progress and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn about the Danaher Business System which makes everything possible. The Principal Software Engineer is responsible to design and develop full-stack solutions for real-time medical device embedded systems. Lead implementation of user interfaces and backend services, architect scalable solutions, optimize performance across the stack, and ensure code quality while maintaining regulatory compliance and embedded system constraints. This position reports to the Manager, Software and is part of the Research & Development Medical located in Singapore and will be an on-site role. In this role, you will have the opportunity to: Design and implement full-stack solutions across user interfaces and backend services, ensuring compliance with regulatory and embedded system constraints. Own the software development lifecycle from requirements refinement through design, coding, testing, documentation, and release, maintaining high standards of quality and performance. Create and review technical documentation, providing guidance to team members and ensuring clarity and accuracy in all deliverables. Provide technical support to internal teams (Customer Service, RA&QA, Production) by reproducing and resolving issues and toolchain challenges. Collaborate with global, cross-functional teams to align architecture, processes, and deliverables, while driving continuous improvement using DBS and agile methodologies. The essential requirements of the job include Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Human-Computer Interaction, or a related field, coupled with strong communication and collaboration skills for distributed team environments. 8–10 years of professional software development experience, with strong programming skills in C++, Dart, or other modern object-oriented languages (e.g., C#). Proficiency in Flutter or similar cross-platform frameworks for building responsive, high-performance user interfaces, and experience with AI coding tools. Solid understanding of software development methodologies, including Scrum, Agile, and waterfall practices, as well as software testing principles. Deep UI/UX knowledge for embedded systems and familiarity with regulatory compliance requirements in medical device development. Leica Microsystems, a Danaher operating company, offers a broad array of comprehensive, competitive benefit programs that add value to our lives. Whether it’s a health care program or paid time off, our programs contribute to life beyond the job. Check out our benefits at Danaher Benefits Info .

title: Research Fellow (Asphalt Pavement Materials and Modelling)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description The National University of Singapore (NUS) Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering invites applications for one (1) Research Fellow position in the area of resilient asphalt pavement materials and modelling with focus on new materials, modelling and design. The candidate is expected to work in research projects related to the topic funded by the National Research Foundation of Singapore and Land Transport Authority of Singapore, and the Defence Science and Technology Agency of Singapore. The candidate will be working with Associate Professor Ong Ghim Ping Raymond from the Department of Civil and Environmental Engineering, College of Design and Engineering (CDE)( https://cde.nus.edu.sg/cee/staff/ong-ghim-ping-raymond/) in fundamental and translational research in the development of new asphalt pavement design guidelines. The selected individual is required to: •    Perform laboratory and field experiments on asphalt pavements functional and environmental performance as core focus. •    Work on mechanistic empirical pavement design and balanced mix design standard development. •    Model pavement behaviours using analytical and numerical methods with the end goal in modification of mechanistic empirical pavement design and balanced mix design to suit local context. •    Develop state of the art solutions for high performance pavement system and work closely with contractors and agencies on field construction implementation to ensure solution is commercially viable. •    Manage research project progress, grant administration, and finance. •    Work on other relevant tasks set out by the project PI. The candidate should also possess good communication and team working skills and has the ability to present research findings in publications and professional meetings (including with agencies and the industry). To apply, please furnish the following documents: •    Cover letter •    Curriculum vitae and transcripts, including a list of publications (provide softcopies of relevant papers) •    Four referee letters (of which one must be from the PhD supervisor) Only shortlisted candidates will be notified Job Requirements •    Possess a PhD in civil engineering or a related area. •    Applicants with relevant experience in both experimental and field asphalt pavement design, standard development and construction would be favourably considered. •    Candidates without experience are welcome to apply. •    Strong written and spoken communications. •    Open to fixed-term contract. •    Only shortlisted candidates will be notified

description: The AI/Robotics Engineer will lead the lifecycle management, from development to production deployment of advanced AI and autonomous applications to address key challenges in the maritime and offshore industries. CI/CD Implementation : Adopt a DevOps mindset, ensuring continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) practices are applied to machine learning and robotics workflows. Stakeholder Collaboration : Collaborate closely with Project Managers to translate business requirements into technical specifications and provide support to ensure smooth adoption of tools What You Will Need: Education and Experience Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Robotics, Engineering, or a related field. A Master’s or PhD is preferred. Minimum 2 years of experience in AI/ML development and deployment, preferably in a relevant field. Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Robotics & Vision: Strong grasp of sensor fusion (Camera + LiDAR), SLAM, and experience in crack or pattern detection algorithms. Cloud Ecosystem: Hands-on experience with the Azure ecosystem (e.g., Azure MachineLearning, Blob Storage, Azure DevOps, Azure OpenAI Service). Framework Proficiency: Proficiency in machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch. Scalable Deployment: Experience with distributed systems, cloud computing, and containerization tools (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker) for scalable AI deployment. API Integration: Proven experience in developing and fine-tuning AI models and integrating them via APIs. Soft Skills: Exceptional problem-solving skills, self-motivation, and strong communication skills to work effectively with non-technical stakeholders. title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About the Role We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project "Empowering Real-World 3D Scene Understanding: Navigating Noise, Distribution Shifts, and Incremental Learning". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Real-world 3D scene understanding problems. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of real-world 3D scene understanding. Design and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Analyze and interpret data sourced from inputs, including images or point clouds. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in real-world 3D scene understanding and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We're Looking For PhD in computer science (artificial intelligence, machine learning, computer vision, or related fields), with a solid theoretical foundation and extensive research experience. Ability to handle high-intensity research work and demonstrate independent working capabilities. Significant research achievements in computer vision and machine learning, with strong publication records in 3D computer vision and top international journals or conferences. Strong programming skills, proficient in Python, C++, and familiar with common deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch. Team player with strong academic communication skills and collaborates effectively with team members from diverse backgrounds. International academic exchange and project management experience. Proficiency in both written and spoken English, with the ability to write academic papers and reports and communicate fluently with project collaborators. Strong self-motivation and problem-solving abilities, with capability to drive project progress within a team. Passionate and curiosity for research, willing to actively undertake research projects, mentor students, and participate in academic presentations and seminars.

title: Construction Quality Technical Program Manager, Electrical/Mechanical - Singapore
description: Job description Our mission as Construction Quality and Commissioning (QCx) is to design, build, and operate a fact-based quality management system that provides predictable quality and a closed loop learning culture from design through operations for our global data centers. We strive to optimize construction quality and the commissioning process through data-driven decision making, in order to deliver data centers with the highest quality and reliability in the industry. As a team, we are committed to growing a culture of excellence and collaboration, and to continuously improving the processes and outcomes in order to exceed industry standards and the expectations of the stakeholders. As a Technical Program Manager, you will use your technical expertise to lead complex, multi-disciplinary projects from start to finish. You will work with stakeholders to plan requirements, identify risks, manage project schedules, and communicate clearly with cross-functional partners across the company. You will be responsible for developing the commissioning strategy and relevant documentation (i.e., specifications, test plans, checklists, guides) for mechanical or electrical equipment, supporting the APAC region. In this role, you will utilize an understanding of data center construction standards, quality control and assurance processes, and commissioning procedures to ensure the successful delivery of high-quality, reliable, and efficient data center facilities. You will also support a portfolio of construction projects to ensure the successful implementation of the general construction quality program and reduce cost of poor quality in the construction projects. Qualifications Job responsibilities Source commissioning agents and develop strategy and relevant documents (i.e., specifications, test plans, checklists, guides, trainings). Hold necessary cross-functional team reviews and onboard project stakeholders. Participate/facilitate vendor meetings to set expectations on commissioning requirements, verify feasibility of strategy and documenting agreements. Ensure document workflow approval in collaboration with the QCx librarian. Report the quality and commissioning status for the full lifecycle of the construction project. Monitor and oversee the implementation of all construction quality and commissioning processes by the project management consultants, general contractors, electrical/mechanical trades, and quality and commissioning agents. Identify risks to a project and develop mitigation strategies with the project team. Work and collaborate on regional/global initiatives to improve the construction quality program. Minimum qualifications Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or equivalent practical experience 5 years of experience with managing the end-to-end construction projects, or product lifecycle (e.g., process developments, implementation and improvement 5 years of infrastructure experience in the operation and construction of data center, semiconductor, pharmaceutical, power, nuclear, or other 24x7 mission critical facilities Preferred qualifications 10 years of experience with managing the end-to-end construction projects, or product lifecycle (e.g., process developments, and improvement 10 years of infrastructure experience in the operation and construction of data center, semiconductor, pharmaceutical, power, nuclear, or other 24x7 mission critical facilities. Experience in leading cross-functional process improvements and the development and implementation of construction quality and commissioning programs. Experience with the construction of hyperscale data centers. Understanding of SQL, Tableau, metrics dashboards, data visualization, and Google Workspace. Knowledge of data center structural, electrical, mechanical, and controls equipment.

MSI provides professional land transport consultancy, engineering services and solutions in Singapore and many other parts of the world. The Company owns a subsidiary in Shanghai and Bangalore. We are looking for highly motivated, results-oriented team players to join us as professional partners to strengthen and realise our vision and mission. Job Summary Working in a team, you will be responsible for the architecture, design and development of front-end devices that are part of an integrated e-payment fare collection system. You will be involved in the software development lifecycle from defining and translating complex requirements into comprehensive system architectures and software designs, to code implementation and testing. Responsibilities Translate complex software requirements into detailed system architectures and software designs to support integrated e-payment fare collection devices Develop and implement C++ software for embedded systems on QNX, Linux, or Windows platforms to deliver reliable front-end device functionality Apply object-oriented software design principles and UML modeling to create maintainable and scalable software solutions Utilize multi-threading, socket programming, inter-process communication, and serial communication techniques to enhance system performance and integration Incorporate cloud architecture concepts including cloud computing, cloud native services, cloud security, multi-tier architecture, and microservices to support system scalability and security Participate actively in the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) using Rational Unified Process (RUP), Agile, or Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment/Delivery (CI/CD) methodologies to ensure timely and quality software delivery Communicate design decisions clearly and effectively in written and verbal formats to align with team and stakeholder expectations Work independently with self-motivation while collaborating effectively as a team player to achieve project goals Education and Experience Recognised Degree in Electrical, Electronics, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or a relevant discipline Minimum of 3 years’ experience in C++ software development for embedded systems or Windows platforms At least 1 year of experience in object-oriented software design and UML modeling Preferred Competencies and Qualifications Knowledge of Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) methodologies including Rational Unified Process (RUP), Agile, or Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment/Delivery (CI/CD) In-depth knowledge of multi-threading, socket programming, inter-process communication, and serial communication Understanding of cloud architecture concepts including cloud computing, cloud native services, cloud security, multi-tier architecture, and microservices Strong skills and methodology in translating requirements into system architectures and software designs

title: Senior Electrochemical Plating Process Engineer (APM), IME
description: About the Role: We are seeking a highly experienced and driven Senior Electrochemical Plating Process Engineer to join our advanced packaging and semiconductor process development team. The ideal candidate will bring deep expertise in BEOL ECP processes, including Cu RDL, Cu pillar, TSV, and UBM plating, as well as strong capabilities in process control, equipment qualification, and yield improvement. Key Responsibilities: Lead development and optimization of ECP processes for advanced packaging applications (e.g., Cu/Ni/Au plating, SnAg bump/solder plating). Drive process control and SPC for BEOL PVD and ECP processes, including Ti/Cu seed layers and barrier films. Manage equipment procurement, qualification, and supplier sourcing for plating chemicals and consumables. Support integration and yield improvement for SVP builds such as WLP, FOWLP, and C2W. Conduct DOE and troubleshooting for process enhancement and defect reduction. Collaborate cross-functionally with QA, integration, and equipment teams to ensure robust process performance. Champion continuous improvement initiatives including cost reduction, productivity enhancement, and system optimization. Job Requirements: Master's Degree in Physics, Materials Science, Chemistry, Physics, Material science and equipment or related field. Minimum 10 years of experience in semiconductor manufacturing, with strong focus on ECP processes. Proven track record in process development, SPC, yield improvement, and equipment qualification. Strong analytical skills with tools like JMP, Excel, and defect analysis platforms. The above eligibility criteria are not exhaustive. A*STAR may include additional selection criteria based on its prevailing recruitment policies. These policies may be amended from time to time without notice. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Senior Industrial Engineer
description: About our group: The Drive & HSA Industrial Engineering (IE) Department is a key function within Seagate’s Worldwide Operations Planning organization, responsible for enabling efficient, cost‑effective, and scalable HDD manufacturing by optimizing critical factory resources such as space, equipment, manpower, material, machine utilization, and HPU (Hour Per Unit) requirements. The team partners closely with cross‑functional factory groups to ensure that current and future customer demand can be met through comprehensive capacity planning, long‑term volume forecasting, equipment and layout optimization, budget and technology roadmap alignment, and continuous operational improvement using Lean and IE methodologies to enhance OEE, yield, and cycle time. Additionally, the department drives digital transformation through Factory 4.0 initiatives, including automation, simulation modeling, and capacity visualization dashboards, serving as the central integrator that transforms complex manufacturing data into actionable strategies that keep Seagate’s factories agile, productive, and operationally excellent. About the role - you will: The incumbent will play a significant role in shaping Seagate’s factory capacity, layout efficiency, and digital transformation efforts. Your responsibilities include: Apply data analytics platforms and simulation tools (Python, FlexSim, Arena) to analyze data extracted from manufacturing systems. Develop data‑driven models to optimize capacity, layout, and resource allocation. Design optimized production layouts that improve process flow, minimize movement, and support future product and technology roadmaps. Calculate and standardize HPU (Hour Per Unit) requirements across product mixes and process variations. Assess the impact of new equipment and emerging technologies on existing production lines and long‑term factory plans. Provide standardized DOS, MCT, and process‑aligned layout requirements. Apply Lean and Industrial Engineering methodologies to improve Man, Machine, Material, and Space utilization. Drive improvements in factory yield, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness), and cycle time. Build and design automated workflow systems that support Factory 4.0 initiatives. Lead development of capacity dashboards to enhance visibility for key stakeholders. Lead overall factory capacity planning to meet customer volume demand. Collaborate with functional departments to expand capacity through process and equipment improvement. Support the IE Manager with budget planning and long‑term factory strategies. About you: Bachelor’s degree in Industrial Engineering or relevant disciplines. Strong communication, teamwork, interpersonal, presentation, and leadership skills. Ownership, adaptability, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑moving environment. Proficiency in data management, data processing, automation tools, Tableau, PowerBI, KNIME, Python, and optimization programming. A positive, self‑motivated, and flexible working approach. Willing to travel to Thailand for one (1) month for on-job-training. Your experience includes: Minimum 5 years in industrial engineering or manufacturing planning. Proficiency in data analysis and simulation using Python, FlexSim, and Arena. Strong knowledge of hard disk drive (HDD) manufacturing processes.

The successful candidate will conduct applied R&D to develop, implement, and validate robot learning algorithms integrating motion, force/torque, and multimodal perception for real-world industrial applications. This role involves designing experimental setups, collecting and analysing demonstration data, translating research outcomes into functional prototypes, and contributing to the strategic development of Embodied AI and humanoid robotics capabilities, while working closely with multidisciplinary teams and stakeholders to drive innovation, technical excellence, and impactful deployment in advanced manufacturing environments. Roles & Responsibilities •	Conduct applied R&D in Embodied AI for robotics, focusing on Learning from Demonstration (LfD) for contact-rich manipulation and skill acquisition. •	Design and execute experiments to collect, process, and analyse demonstration data for training intelligent robotic behaviours. •	Apply machine learning techniques, including deep-learning methods, to enhance robot control and decision-making processes, making our robots more adaptable and intelligent. •	Translate research outcomes into functional prototypes and deployable robotics solutions for industrial applications. •	Contribute to the strategic development of Embodied AI and humanoid robotics capabilities •	If applicable, leverage computer vision to improve robot perception and object recognition capabilities. •	Continuously research and implement state-of-the-art algorithms and methodologies to improve robot performance and efficiency. •	Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including hardware engineers, software developers, and researchers, to achieve project goals. •	Support stakeholder engagement and technical discussions with industry partners, providing expert input on system capabilities and limitations. Job Requirements •	PhD in Robotics, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field. •	Strong background in robot learning, Embodied AI, Learning from Demonstration, or related AI-driven robotics techniques. •	Knowledge of deep learning frameworks like TensorFlow and/or PyTorch. •	Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++ •	Hands-on experience with ROS2, simulation environments (e.g. Isaac Sim), and robotic hardware platforms. •	Familiarity with industrial automation or manufacturing environments is an advantage. •	Strong communication skills to collaborate effectively with colleagues and present technical findings. •	A passion for robotics and a desire to stay updated with the latest developments in the field. •	Strong commitment to solving technical challenges in the field of robotics, approaching obstacles with a positive and determined mindset

title: AI Engineer
description: JOB OVERVIEW: As an AI Engineer, you will leverage cutting-edge AI to solve complex, industry-specific problems, particularly within the maritime sector. You will be instrumental in a rapidly evolving, client-centric organization, making a significant impact by thinking critically, articulating a compelling vision, and guiding both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Your role requires a proven ability to architect complex AI/ML solutions from problem framing to production-ready design, a profound understanding of diverse business models, and strong consultative sales skills to identify strategic pain points solvable by AI. You will build functional AI/ML prototypes with clean, modular code for production, assess data readiness, identify data gaps, and recommend robust data governance frameworks. Additionally, you will identify emerging trends, synthesize complex information, and present strategic recommendations to senior leadership and external audiences. Success hinges on your ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value, build strong client relationships, and drive consensus across diverse teams. You must be highly motivated to drive initiatives independently in a dynamic startup environment, with a passion for continuous learning and innovation. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: •	Proactively identify and engage with high-value business opportunities and potential clients who can significantly benefit from advanced AI and machine learning solutions. Deeply understand client business challenges, pain points, and strategic goals, translating them into clear, solvable AI/ML problems. •	Design end-to-end AI solutions, articulating compelling value propositions and the strategic roadmap for adoption. •	Conduct in-depth assessments of client data infrastructure, data governance, data quality, and overall data maturity. •	Make expert judgment calls on data "AI-readiness" and provide actionable recommendations for data improvement, gap identification, and strategic data capitalization opportunities. •	Rapidly develop functional AI prototypes and Proof-of-Concepts (POCs) that are architecturally sound, adhere to best practices for maintainability, and are designed for production. •	Continuously research and evaluate the latest AI advancements, including Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), Deep Learning, and MLOps methodologies. Synthesize findings into actionable insights, influencing our company's strategic roadmap, service offering development, and contributing to industry thought leadership through presentations to leadership and external forums (moderate to high emphasis). •	Collaborate effectively with the internal AI Production team, ensuring prototypes and solution designs are well-documented, understood, and seamlessly integrated into the full development lifecycle. •	Clearly define the business case, expected return on investment (ROI), and measurable benefits of proposed AI/ML implementations, simplifying complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences. QUALIFICATIONS AND EXPERIENCES: •	Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field. Master's or Ph.D. preferred. •	At least 5+ years of progressive experience in AI/ML solution design, data science, or AI engineering, with a significant portion in a client-facing or consulting capacity. •	Proven experience in designing, developing, and deploying functional AI/ML prototypes that demonstrate business value and are engineered for production. •	Expert proficiency in Python and extensive experience with core AI/ML libraries (e.g., scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch). •	Demonstrated hands-on experience with Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs), including frameworks like LangChain, for designing and prototyping novel applications. •	Strong practical experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) services relevant to AI/ML (e.g., Vertex AI, BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Dataflow, Cloud Functions). •	Proficiency with Docker for containerization and a foundational understanding of container orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes concepts for GKE). •	Solid understanding of MLOps principles for reproducibility, experiment tracking (e.g., MLfl ow, Vertex AI ML Metadata/Model Registry), and model versioning. •	High-level expertise in data governance frameworks, data quality assessment (including tools/libraries like Great Expectations), metadata management (e.g., Google Cloud Data Catalog), and data architecture principles. •	Strong expertise in distributed computing and parallel processing techniques is vital for handling large-scale AI workloads. •	A proven track record of innovations and executions in deep learning, demonstrated through shipping products or first-author publications at leading AI conferences, is a strong differentiator. •	Experience in the container shipping industry or related logistics/supply chain domains is highly advantageous. •	Excellent presentation, documentation, and stakeholder management skills. Working hours: Mon to Fri, 8:30am to 5:30pm

title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Synthesis x Senior Data Scientist At Synthesis, we push the boundaries of what’s possible with Open Data. It’s a fast-paced, rapidly-changing environment at the intersection of data science, cultural analysis & brand consultancy. We call this Open Source Intelligence for brands. As a Senior Data Scientist at Synthesis, you will play a key role in shaping our data science practice and driving innovation across both client projects and internal products. You will lead end-to-end data initiatives, delivering project work from shaping datasets and building models to designing scalable data workflows that power the insights and decision frameworks we deliver. You will work with diverse open data sources, ranging from search, social, and e-commerce, to academic papers and patents, to understand audience behaviour and anticipate how needs will evolve over time. In this role, you will not only contribute hands-on but also guide junior data scientists, act as a thought partner to cultural strategists, and help define the roadmap for our data products and infrastructure. We are a small, collaborative team with diverse backgrounds, spanning computer science and engineering, anthropology, and law. We value curiosity, creativity, and a spirit of experimentation. If you are excited about combining data and culture to solve real-world problems, we would love to hear from you! What you’ll need. 5+ years of experience in data science, with a strong record of delivering impactful projects from data exploration through to production deployment. Deep proficiency in Python and SQL, with strong software engineering fundamentals (clean, modular, well-documented code, testing, and version control with Git). Proven experience designing and building scalable data workflows or data-driven products, including architecting pipelines, modular components, and reusable frameworks. Experience applying large language models or advanced NLP techniques (e.g. embeddings, semantic parsing, topic modelling, text classification); familiarity with processing Asian languages is a plus. Strong grasp of statistical methods and core machine learning algorithms, with practical experience using libraries such as scikit-learn, statsmodels, and the ability to select appropriate models based on data characteristics and problem context. Comfortable working with large, messy, and unstructured datasets, particularly text and image-based data. Ability to translate complex technical insights into clear, actionable recommendations for non-technical stakeholders, and collaborate closely with strategists to drive business outcomes. A collaborative mindset, with openness to feedback and diverse perspectives. Bonus points if you have the following. Experience leading or mentoring junior data scientists, including setting technical direction and supporting professional growth. Experience building and maintaining production-grade data pipelines (e.g. Airflow, Dagster) or integrating data from APIs, web scraping, or third-party providers. Familiarity with cloud environments such as Google Cloud or AWS, and deploying data science workflows at scale Why Synthesis? Synthesis is known for delivering exceptional data models and products that uncover the stories of the people they represent and inspire our partners to act with confidence. Partnering with the world’s most successful brands in fashion, beauty, content, and travel, we’re building specialised solutions to solve problems of planning for the future. Rooted in culture, tested in data science, we spot and anticipate changes and connections in culture to inspire action and help brands grow with a new wave of audiences. After three years of iterative development and refinement, we are preparing to launch a series of products over the next 5 years. We will be at the forefront of a shift towards leveraging open data to develop rich, honest, human insight. In an industry that has, for too long, relied on a ‘question-response’ approach to understanding changes in behaviour, we prioritise layering behavioural, performative, search, and sales data to highlight discrepancies between what people say they do and what they actually do. We see a $40 billion industry that has failed to innovate for too long, and we are just getting started. About Synthesis We do Human Centred Data Science. It’s a way of reimaging open data sources from a human perspective. It prioritises behaviour and context to unravel why people do what they do, at scale.  By blending data science with human and market intelligence, we help our clients spot early signals of change and predict implications for business. Diverse by design. Synthesis is a team of digital researchers, gaming designers, data forecasters, network scientists, ethnographers, and engineers.  Cultural and category experts train our models – ensuring they detect measures that matter most – whilst data scientists uncover patterns the eye cannot see. Building bespoke, comprehensive datasets where none exist. We work with open data - search, ratings, social, and sales.  By layering many signals, we build robust and relevant data-driven stories around brands, businesses, and consumer behaviour. Asia first, global applied. Our products must work in Asia first - the hardest test markets.  We continue to make significant investments in language and capabilities across Asian-specific platforms.  Now applied in Europe, LatAm, and the USA.

title: Physical AI Engineer
description:
We are looking for Physical AI Engineers focused on developing and
integrating Machine Learning (including Generative AI) models that enable real robots to perceive, reason, and act autonomously. This role bridges Robotics and Agentic AI — working on perception, decision-making, and multi-model integration for multi-robot and embodied systems. You will work on training and adapting Generative/Foundation models
(vision, language, planning, VLA models, swarm reasoning) and deploying them onto edge and robotic hardware. Responsibilities ·       Develop and integrate AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models for perception, mapping, task planning, and embodied decision-making. ·       Fine-tune or adapt Generative/Foundation models (Small Language Model/Small-Vision-Language/ Vision-Language-Action) models for edge deployment for real-world robotic applications. ·       Implement on-device model optimization (quantization, distillation, TensorRT, etc.). ·       Build data pipelines: simulation → real-world → feedback loop for continual model improvement. ·       Conduct domain adaptation and reinforcement/interactive learning for robot skills. ·       Integrating models into ROS2 and swarm autonomy stacks. ·       Evaluate model performance in both simulation and hardware deployment. ·       Optimize inference latency and reliability on edge compute platforms. ·       Formulate the conceptual and detailed technical solution for the development of applications to meet customer requirements. ·       Provide recommendations on relevant emerging technology in Physical AI/Robotics to senior management. ·       Identifying and leading strategic technical capability development for Physical AI/Robotics. ·       Collaborate on research and development projects to explore new capabilities and applications for Physical AI/Robotics technology. Minimum Requirements ·       Bachelor’s/Master’s in Computer Science, Machine Learning, AI, Robotics, or related field. ·       3 years of experience building robotics/Physical AI systems. Candidates without work experience but with relevant skills are also welcome to apply. ·       Strong foundation in ROS2 or robotics middleware integration. ·       Extensive experience building/fine-tuning of AI/ML and Generative/Foundation models (vision, transformer-based, or RL) for robotic systems. ·       Good knowledge of model compression/acceleration for embedded devices (Jetson, Pi,etc). ·       GPU/CUDA experience or on-device inference for embedded AI. ·       Experienced with deployment on embedded/edge Linux systems. ·       Good understanding of robotics model training pipelines (perception → planning →control). Preferred Experience ·       Vision-Language-Action (VLA) or Multi-Modal model experience. ·       Reinforcement learning / imitation learning / interactive training loops. ·       Synthetic data and simulation-based training (Isaac Sim, Habitat, etc.). ·       Knowledge of distributed training pipelines or MLOps for robotics. Additional Skills ·       Strong experimentation and iterative problem-solving mindset. ·       Comfortable bridging research prototypes into production-grade systems. ·       Able to collaborate tightly with AI and systems engineers. ·       Curious and self-driven — able to explore new Physical AI approaches and rapidly test them. What We Offer ·       Opportunity to work on state-of-the-art embodied AI models powering real robots. ·       Combination of research and deployment — not just writing models, but seeing them act in the physical world. ·       High-impact work on cutting-edge robotic autonomy and swarm behaviours.

title: Research Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities: Advanced Market Research & Quantitative Analysis Conduct deep quantitative analysis of global dry bulk, tanker, and container shipping markets, integrating physical freight fundamentals and derivatives (FFA) pricing dynamics. Assess geopolitical, regulatory, and macroeconomic developments and quantify their impact on freight demand, ton-mile evolution, and forward curves. 1. Model Development & Data Engineering: Conduct advanced quantitative research on Forward Freight Agreements (FFAs) across dry bulk and/or tanker markets. Design, build, and maintain end-to-end analytical pipelines using Python and R, including data ingestion, cleaning, feature engineering, and validation. Develop statistical and econometric models for freight forecasting (e.g. regression models, regime-switching models, Monte Carlo simulations, scenario analysis). Build and maintain forward-curve models, basis analysis, and spread structures across tenors. Automate recurring analyses and reporting workflows, replacing manual Excel processes with reproducible code-based solutions. Create detailed reports, presentations, and dashboards to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders. 2. Industry Monitoring: Track key industry metrics such as vessel utilization, shipping supply and demand, and pricing trends. Monitor competitor activity, trade agreements, and global economic indicators influencing trade flows. 3. Forecasting & Strategic Insights: Write production-quality Python code to support market analysis, dashboards, and internal tools. Create interactive dashboards to visualize freight curves, volatility, seasonality, and scenario outcomes. Integrate multiple data sources into coherent analytical frameworks. Apply version control and follow disciplined coding and documentation standards. Analyse freight market risk using volatility metrics, stress testing, and scenario analysis. Translate complex quantitative outputs into clear commercial insights for trading, chartering, and senior management teams. 4. Collaboration & Communication: Continuously monitor shipping indices, broker reports, and market intelligence sources, critically assessing data quality and bias. Work closely with commercial, trading, and risk teams to support real-time decision-making. Liaise with external stakeholders, such as analysts, consultants, and industry experts, to validate and enhance insights. Able to closely monitor market movements and work overnight when required. 5. Risk Assessment: Identify potential risks and opportunities in trade and shipping operations, including supply chain disruptions and regulatory impacts. Propose mitigation strategies and solutions based on research findings. Requirements: Education Bachelor’s degree or higher in Quantitative Finance, Applied Mathematics, Applied physic, Data Analytics, Applied Analytics, Maritime Studies. Shipping & Market Knowledge Demonstrated experience in shipping research, freight analytics, commodity markets, or freight derivatives (FFA). Strongunderstanding of both physical and paper freight markets, including: 1. Supply/demand mechanics 2. Freight rate formation 3. Forward curves, spreads, and seasonality Prior exposure to dry bulk and/or tanker markets is highly preferred. Experience with derivatives pricing, volatility modelling, or risk management is an advantage. Programming & Technical Skills Strong proficiency in data analysis tools Familiarity with version control tools and structured research or development workflows. Prior experience in financial services, trading, research, or related commercial industries is an advantage. Experience supporting or developing internal tools, reports, or dashboards for business or commercial use. Analytical & Quantitative Skills Strong grounding in statistics, time-series analysis, and forecasting techniques. Ability to independently design analytical frameworks and challenge assumptions. High attention to detail and data integrity. Communication Ability to clearly explain complex quantitative results to non-technical stakeholders. Strong written communication skills for producing structured research reports and presentations.

title: Technical Supervisor (Water Dispensers)
description: The Technical Supervisor will oversee the operations of the water dispenser servicing and maintenance department. This role involves setting up and managing the service workshop, supervising a team of indoor and field technicians, managing spare parts inventory, conducting training sessions, and drafting comprehensive servicing and maintenance Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). The supervisor ensures all service activities comply with quality and safety standards to maintain operational excellence and customer satisfaction. Setup & Manage Service Workshop - Establish and organize a fully functional service workshop, including layout planning, tools, equipment procurement, and ensuring a safe working environment. Team Supervision & Leadership - Lead, supervise, and coordinate a team of service technicians, assigning daily tasks, monitoring performance, and supporting skill development to maintain high productivity and quality standards. Spare Parts Inventory Management - Oversee the inventory of spare parts to ensure availability, proper storage, tracking, and timely replenishment to avoid service delays. Training & Development - Plan and conduct regular technical training and refresher courses for technicians to keep the team updated on the latest maintenance techniques, safety procedures, and equipment handling. Drafting SOPs - Develop, review, and continuously improve Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) related to servicing, preventive maintenance, troubleshooting, and repair for water dispensers. Quality & Safety Compliance - Ensure all activities comply with industry safety standards and internal quality policies; conduct periodic audits and implement corrective actions as needed. Reporting & Documentation - Maintain accurate records of service activities, inventory usage, training sessions, and performance metrics; prepare regular reports for management review. Customer Support Oversight - Work closely with the customer service team to address feedbacks, faulty dispensers, ensure timely resolution of technical issues, and maintain high levels of customer satisfaction. Work Environment - The role requires working in a service workshop environment with occasional visits to field service locations. Safety gear and adherence to health and safety protocols are mandatory. Requirements Minimum 2-3 years’ experience. Possess at least a diploma in Electrical or Mechanical Engineering, or related field. Proven experience in supervising a technical service team, preferably in water dispenser maintenance or similar appliance servicing. Hands-on experience in installation, servicing & maintenance of various types of drinking water dispensers, water coolers and water filtration systems. Including floor standing and table top POU (Point-of-Use) dispensers. Able to troubleshoot mechanical, electrical, refrigeration and common faults. Experience in workshop setup and inventory control. Ability to draft clear and concise SOPs and training materials. Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Proficient in using Microsoft office for record keeping and reporting. Safety-oriented with a good understanding of workplace safety standards (BizSafe). Ability to manage multiple tasks and work under pressure. 5.5 work days Class 3 Licence preferred

The successful candidate will contribute to value creation by delivering actionable insights and supporting strategic trading decisions. Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to: Monitor and evaluate market fundamentals, pricing structures, global trade flows, and competitor production profiles and other industry information to generate insights that support trading strategies. Lead and facilitate discussions on fundamentals with a broader team of traders and other analysts, providing timely key market data and relevant analysis to help the Trading team to formulate view of market directions and inform new trade ideas on physical and financial trading. Model deal economics/ analysis to identify feasible alternatives with trade-offs. Pilot and leverage advanced digital tools-including machine learning and predictive analytics-to enhance trading performance and uncover new market opportunities. Conduct performance lookbacks on trading outcomes and assess the accuracy of market calls and price forecasts. Build and maintain a network of industry contacts to enhance market intelligence, generate insights and conduct economic analysis for future opportunities. Champion process improvements by partnering with internal teams to optimize digital tools and pursue analytical process improvement opportunities to reduce manual effort. Required Qualifications: Bachelor's degree with a minimum of 3 years of experience in Oil & Gas Industry, trading, market data analytics, or related roles. Solid understanding of commodity markets, including crude, natural gas, renewables, and/or refined products. Experience analysing commodity market fundamentals, with a preference for that to have been in NGL and Petrochemical, LNG, gas or power markets would be advantageous. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills. Demonstrated ability to manipulate and analyse data sets and use economic models to test sensitivities and identify key value drivers all with minimal direction. Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Power BI, and PowerPoint; experience with Python for data analysis and visualization is preferred. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to present market insights clearly and concisely to influence and achieve optimum decisions. A collaborative team player who thrives in a fast-paced environment and ability to work effectively in a cross-functional team environment. A digital-first mindset with a passion for innovation, ability to utilize decision analysis tools and processes to evaluate multiple commercial options and drive continuous improvement.

title: Category Specialist, JV
description: Requisition ID: Job Location: Singapore – Duo Tower To submit application, please click below posting link or copy and paste to a new browser: https://chevron.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/jobs/job/Singapore-DUO-Tower-Singapore/Category-Specialist--JV_R000068320 The Category Specialist is a key member of the Asia Pacific and Middle East (APME) SCM team, supporting the Joint Venture Refineries Business. Reporting to the Regional Category Manager (JV Refineries), this role is responsible for developing and executing in-country category strategies, sourcing, negotiations, and commercial evaluations for the Singapore Joint Venture Refinery. The position requires regular interaction with SRC management and business stakeholders. The Category Specialist will lead cost management initiatives, oversee strategic sourcing, conduct negotiations, and implement supplier relationship management approaches to enhance commercial and operational performance. The role also supports benchmarking activities to drive best-in-class results. Close collaboration with Joint Venture Refinery management, internal stakeholders, local Procurement teams, and external vendors is essential to deliver sustainable growth and ensure the application of category management principles to maximise value for Chevron. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with strong interpersonal skills, able to work both collaboratively and independently. They should have a proven track record of managing multiple initiatives to completion, effectively communicating and influencing stakeholders and suppliers at all levels, and demonstrating professionalism, customer focus, and sound business judgement. Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to: Procurement Identify new opportunities, develop business cases, and drive change. Plan, coordinate, and develop category and contracting plans. Conduct market assessments, cost modelling, benchmarking, and total cost of ownership analyses. Provide strategic and business alignment direction to the Refinery Procurement team on category, sourcing, contracting strategies, negotiation, and supplier management. Partner with the business to develop procurement strategies that deliver sustainable cost savings, ensuring alignment with business objectives and consistent application of strategic sourcing principles. Develop mechanisms and metrics to hold suppliers accountable for performance and delivery. Collaborate with suppliers and technical stakeholders to introduce new ideas, processes, and technologies into the facilities. Understand the needs of Reliability, Operations, Maintenance, and Capital Projects stakeholders, translating them into supply base strategies that enhance cost effectiveness and resilience. Advisory Provide functional guidance on procurement processes and controls to the Refinery Procurement Manager, identifying opportunities for process improvement and efficiency. Support the rollout and implementation of procurement strategies, processes, standards, and guidelines. Act as a procurement advisor to the SRC Procurement Manager and team, providing category guidance and implementing global and regional initiatives at a local level. Engage regularly with SRC Refinery stakeholders to review project plans and drive service quality and supplier performance. Coordinate with internal stakeholders on legal, risk, and tax matters. Coordinates with Chevron Global Category Managers to seek advise and explore synergies for strategic contracts Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree, preferably in Engineering or Procurement/Supply Chain Management. 5–8 years’ experience in Procurement/Category Management within Oil & Gas, Pharmaceutical, Chemical, or related industries. Experience in a plant environment (e.g., terminal, refinery) is an advantage. In-depth understanding of Category Management, including category planning, strategic sourcing, contracting, supplier management, and strong negotiation skills. Experience with total cost ownership (TCO), Should Cost Modelling, and other category management techniques. Knowledge of refining/manufacturing commodities markets (e.g., maintenance, rotating equipment, catalysts & chemicals, projects). Experience providing project procurement support for capital projects. Ability to identify new opportunities, challenge the status quo, and drive change, including process improvements and simplification. Strong consensus-building, influencing, analytical, project management, change management, communication, and presentation skills. Proven team player with a strong drive for performance and results; consistent and reliable execution of work plans. Knowledge of refining process plants or similar manufacturing facilities is a plus. E-Procurement knowledge (SMART by GEP / SAP) is an advantage. Work Location The selected candidate will be based in Singapore, stationed at Chevron’s Singapore office in Duo Tower. This role will require frequent travel to the Singapore Joint Venture Refinery, with 50% to 80% of the time spent on-site, to provide essential support to the business.

Our System Engineers are key component of our operations by providing technical support, testing, installation, training, support and maintenance services for our programs in Singapore. In addition, the System Engineer shall support the Program / Project Manager on the project and system maintenance life cycle from pre-sales to maintenance. Candidates must show initiative, highly independent, hands-on, aspiration for increase role and responsibilities, and a demonstrated willingness to work in a team. Job Responsibilities: Reporting to the Program / Project Manager, the System Engineer will be using their expertise and experience to support both internal Glamco and external Customer activities for our Singapore commercial program. Specific responsibilities but not limited to the following includes: · Provide system engineering and networking support to our programs in Singapore including but not limited to hardware and software configuration and set up, troubleshooting networks and system defects, and fielding technical questions about hardware and software. · Manage and provide onsite and remote customer support including system and infrastructure installation, configuration, testing, training, maintenance, site-surveys etc. · Provide system engineering and networking expertise to our programs in Singapore during pre-sales and post-sales. · Develop, prepare and conduct test cases for customer’s program. · Develop and maintain technical and configuration documentation, training materials and user manuals. · Serve as a Technical Support expert and capable of handling escalations, incidents, outages and infrastructure challenges. · Evaluate and upgrade system hardware and/or firmware with consultation with program partners. Perform system patching and deployment. · Lead and manage a team of Technical Officers to provide technical and maintenance support to clients. · Support the Program / Project Manager to manage the project and system maintenance lifecycle from Proposal development to Project Kickoff to Initial Design to Development and Testing to Customer Acceptance Testing to System Maintenance Program. · Support the Business Development Team in determining project scope of work. · Ensure project and maintenance SLA (Service Level Agreement) and customer satisfaction levels are met. · Maintain standard operating procedures (SOP), processes and guidelines. · Maintain security, backup and redundancy strategies. · Research new technologies and solution in support of the programs. · Any other technical, operations or project related duties that may be required. Requirements · Diploma or Degree in Information Technology or Computer Engineering or related field. · Minimum of 3 years working experience in IT Support, Network, Systems, Cyber Security type function. · Knowledge or experience with network and system administration including firewall and network infrastructure topology, networking protocols, routing and switching, and firewall configuration. · Knowledge or experience with server and workstation administration. · Checkpoint Firewalls, PaloAlto Firewalls, Fortinet Firewall, Cisco Switches, HP Aruba Switches, VPN Technologies, DNS, NTP, 2 Factor Authentication technologies. · Experience in running projects is a plus points. · Strong interest and experience in the field of Radar and Radio Frequency (RF) technologies. · Hardware, software and technical aptitude. · Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. · Show initiative, self-discipline, excellent judgment, career aspirations for increased role and responsibilities, a demonstrated willingness to work as a team with the ability to work independently. · Willing to perform potential shift duties, weekends and overtime. · Willing to travel overseas to support overseas requirements. · Willing to work in an SME environment. · Must possess Class 3 Manual driving license. - Ownership of a car is an advance. About Glamco: GLAMCO Aviation Pte Ltd (GLAMCO) prides itself as a leading solutions provider for the Aerospace, Commercial, Air & Maritime Traffic Management and Defence & Security industries. Established in Singapore since 1985, GLAMCO provides component repair and overhaul, spares support, specialized products and other related consultancy services to Aircraft operators, Defence and Security agencies, Civil Airport and Seaport Operators. Representing Aircraft Manufacturers, Systems OEMs, Stocking Distributors and OEMs worldwide, we are able to support Programme Management, quick response and delivery. GLAMCO has also expanded its activities to include the supply of specialized equipment for the Navy, Army, as well as Civil Airport and Seaport. Since 2020s, GLAMCO has further expanded our services to provide logistics management (warehouse), system engineering and maintenance support to our customers. To learn more about us and our products, please visit our website at www.glamco.com.sg .

title: Project Manager
description: 1. Project Planning & Initiation Define project scope, objectives, and deliverables. title: Temporary Senior Pastry Instructor (Contract: 11.5 Months)
description: Contract Duration ·        11.5 Months (To start around mid Mar 2026) Working Location ·        Tampines Working Hours 5 Days (Mon to Thur: 8.30am to 6.00pm; Fri: 8.30am to 5.30pm) Lunch: 1 hour Offered Salary ·        S$3,500 to $4,500 per month Requirements Relevant qualification/certification in Baking & Pastry, Culinary Arts, or in a related discipline. Minimum 5 to 8 years of relevant industry experience working in hotel kitchens, catering companies, or large-scale F&B production environments.
