Job Description We are seeking an experienced and passionate Senior Pastry Instructor to join our teaching team at Bistro Lab, a unique Learning Enterprise where students receive hands-on training in hot kitchen production, baking and pastry, customer service, and food innovation. The instructor will also contribute to R&D initiatives, including recipe development using novel or alternative ingredients, and is encouraged to bring creativity and innovation into the learning environment. Candidates with experience in Culinary R&D, Culinary technology and exposure to digital and smart kitchen systems will be highly regarded. The responsibilities are as follows: ·        Conduct hands-on demonstrations in baking and pastry techniques to students, with emphasis on latest technology, ingredient functionality, baking science, product formulation, and recipe development. ·        Support Bistro Lab’s daily production and operational requirements in a teaching environment, ensuring kitchen safety and food hygiene. ·        Develop innovative products for the Bistro Lab and integrate them into the teaching curriculum. ·        Contribute to R&D projects, including recipe development using novel or alternative ingredients. ·        Where applicable, coach or prepare students for pastry competitions. title: Licensed Aircraft Engineer, Mechanical/Avionics
description: Why join us? Bombardier is a global leader, creating innovative and game-changing planes. Our products and services provide world-class transportation experiences that set new standards in passenger comfort, energy, efficiency, reliability and safety. We are a global organization focused on working together with a team spirit. Position Summary The Engineer is considered to be part of the operations group and is responsible for perform or supervise work on aircraft or aircraft components and to promote an open communication with all other related departments personnel. What are your contributions to the team? Perform maintenance on customer aircraft in complying with Bombardier Policies & Procedures and Aviation Authorities Rules and Regulations. Perform inspection, make adjustment and repair, replace components, and correct any malfunction or deficiency with and without power applied to the aircraft. Access and re-install aircraft components, which obstruct the performance of the assigned tasks. Perform basic mechanical and electrical removals and installation. Document work performed per quality procedure manual. Perform other tasks cleaning aircraft using approved materials, methods, and equipment, and loads and secures ballast and/or freight as required. Assist with fueling/ defueling aircraft as required. Assist Aircraft Technicians in troubleshooting and repairing of aircraft. Assist with repairs on components detached from the aircraft, for example, flaps, ailerons, elevators, rudders, doors, and leading edges. Work on aircraft as well as in component shops. Assist with complex repairs at on-site and off-site locations. Assist other employees in the performance of their duties within area(s) of qualification. How to thrive in this role? You have minimum Aircraft Maintenance Engineering License with at least 5 years signing experience, CAAS licensed preferred and experience in performing aircraft heavy/line maintenance. You have had experience working on Bombardier aircrafts. You must Hold College/ Diploma and / or equivalent academic qualification. You have interpersonal skills necessary to establish and maintain effective working relationships with internal and external customers. You have planning and organizational skills necessary to coordinate workload around multiple assignments. Now that you can see yourself in this role, apply and join the Bombardier family!

Review their inspection reports. • Monitoring the main Contractor/Vendor’s implementation of the approved Quality requirements and address any deficiencies. Also, Control of the Quality of main TPI/contractor/vendor's inspection personnel’s work activities on a daily basis. • Acting as focal point for the resolution of shop / site related Quality Control issues in consultation with Sr. Quality Engineer and QHSE Head. • Control and follow-up of Contractor/Vendor’s Technical deviations, material substitutions, changes to inspection and test requirements at shop/ sites/ installations. • Control and follow-up of Non-conformance Reports and ensure the implementation of the Corrective Action Requested and the preventive action proposed. • Monitoring and report Contractor’s quality statistics and KPI by monitoring welding repair rate, NDE performance, status of NCR’s, Audit findings, and site Surveillance findings. • Ensure that the Contractor / Vendor prepare, manages proper inspection & test records, and compiles test packs & dossiers/MRB for submission Qualification: • Master's or higher educational qualification in Mechanical or Metallurgical Engineering from recognized Engineering faculty. • A valid CSWIP 3.2.1 or AWS Sr. welding Inspection Certificate (SCWI) and at least five year post-certification experiences’ • B Gas Grade 2, or NACE level II Coating inspector qualified is preferable. • NDT level II in RT, UT, PT& MT/ NDT qualifications against ASNT or ISO requirements. • Certified QMS Lead Auditor in accordance with ISO 9001:2015 and having at least five years experience in auditing. • A good knowledge of international codes/standards EN, ISO, API, ASME and ASTM. • Good leadership skills and good communication skills. • Ability to work on projects in a complex and multicultural environment. • Be skilled in using computer programs, MS Office, database management, spreadsheets, and e-mail communications. Experience: • A minimum of fifteen (15) years experiences as QA/QC coordinator / QA/QC Lead, leading QC multidiscipline inspectors’ team, for inspection & testing activities for a major oil and gas projects/ offshore process platforms / jackets/subsea pipeline, including all phases of manufacturing, fabrication, construction, and offshore installation / laying. • A minimum 5-year offshore project experience. • Experience in an international oil company/operator company/EPIC contractor • Middle East project experience is an advantage. • Experience in managing contractor, Sub contractor, Vendor, TPI’s in major projects. Experience in multi discipline activities related to offshore projects such as fabrication/installation of structure and piping, NDT Material inspection, Mechanical equipment installation, Sour services, Cladded Piping and Corrosion Resistance Alloy “CRA” pipeline, welding, NDT, repair works, dimensional control, painting, coating, internal lining, Monel sheathing etc.

title: R&D Hardware Manager

Role Overview We are seeking an experienced full time on-site role for a R&D Hardware Manager to lead the design and development of Battery Management Systems (BMS) and related hardware for battery pack applications such as UPS, power tools, and energy storage systems . This role
combines hands-on technical leadership , team management , and customer-facing responsibilities to deliver reliable, high-quality products on schedule. Qualification & Experience: ·  Degree in Electrical / Electronics Engineering or equivalent. ·  Minimum 8 years of relevant experience in hardware design and development. ·  Proven experience delivering at least 5 successfully commercialized products in related fields. Technical Expertise · Strong electronic circuit design experience in: o    Battery Management System (BMS) design or o    Power supply and DC-DC charger design o    Battery technologies know-how including Lithium, Nickel Metal Hydride (NiMH), and supercapacitors ·  Proficient in schematic design using Altium Designer preferred, component selection, and circuit analysis. ·  Experience using simulation and design tools such as LTSpice is an advantage. ·  Experience in achieving product certification such as UL1973, UN38.3 etc is a plus. Leadership & Communication ·  Strong verbal and written communication skills , with the ability to act as a technical interface with customers. ·  Proven capability in managing local and remote teams , including cross-functional and overseas partners. ·  Customer-oriented mindset with a professional and solution-driven approach. Personal Attributes ·  Positive working attitude with a can-do mindset and a team player . ·  Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills. ·  Able to work under pressure and manage multiple priorities. Additional Advantages ·        Experience in patent writing is a plus. ·        Willingness to travel as required.

title: Class 4 Driver (Bedok/Bukit Merah)
description: Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition Cora Environment is a purpose-driven sustainable waste management company that rethinks waste. Envisioning a zero-waste future, our holistic approach empowers businesses to reduce their environmental footprint, through end-to-end solutions from waste profiling and infrastructure to resource recovery and energy-from-waste. We create value by driving industry partnerships to transform the waste landscape and shape the future for generations to come. Inspired by the words ‘collaboration’ and ‘heart’, the name “Cora” reflects our commitment to putting people, purpose and planet at the heart of everything we do. With over 45 years of experience, Cora Environment is a leading waste management company serving the City-Punggol and Clementi-Bukit Merah sectors. We manage public, industrial, and commercial waste, and operate a material recovery facility and an energy-from-waste plant. Responsibilities Collect waste materials and recyclables efficiently to maintain cleanliness and operational flow Supervise and restore work areas by managing spillages of refuse, recyclables, and oil to ensure safety and hygiene Dispose of collected waste and recyclables at authorized disposal facilities following company and regulatory standards Operate vehicles, equipment, and receptacles safely and correctly to support waste collection and disposal tasks Maintain vehicle cleanliness by washing vehicles daily to uphold company standards and operational readiness Required competencies and certifications Possess a valid Singapore Class 4 driving license to operate company vehicles safely and legally Other Information Working hours: 6.30am to 2.50pm or 7am to 3.20pm, with overtime as required Work schedule: 6 days per week with one rest day per week Contact for interview arrangement: Tel: 9017 5388 (Whatsapp)

title: Technician (Cleanroom/Contract)
description: Job Description We are looking for an Technician to support semiconductor manufacturing operations in a cleanroom environment. The role involves precision assembly, inspection, and handling of semiconductor components while strictly adhering to cleanroom, safety, and quality standards. Key Responsibilities Perform hands-on assembly of semiconductor components and sub-assemblies in a cleanroom environment. Follow work instructions, SOPs, and cleanroom protocols to ensure product quality and compliance. Handle and assemble small and delicate components with high accuracy and attention to detail. Conduct visual inspections and basic functional checks during and after assembly. Record production data and complete required documentation accurately. Maintain cleanliness and organization of workstations and equipment. Support continuous improvement, safety, and quality initiatives. Ability to perform repetitive tasks with high precision and consistency. Comfortable working in a cleanroom environment and wearing cleanroom attire. title: Packer
description: Company Overview SEW-EURODRIVE is a German MNC and has been established in Singapore since 1981 with close to 80 employees. ISO 9001 certified for the Production & Distribution of Geared Motors. The Company specialises in manufacturing gear units, motors, electric motor and inverter technology. Customer-specific and pre-defined modular solutions are also available. Today, the Company has more than 17,000 employees in 437 plants across 49 countries. Job Summary You will perform essential packaging and assembly support tasks, including preparing nameplates, measuring and weighing packages, creating handling units, and managing package transfers to ensure smooth dispatch and storage processes. Responsibilities Create and rivet nameplates on complete units to ensure proper identification and traceability. Measure and weigh packages accurately to comply with shipping and handling requirements. Create handling units for complete units and spare parts to facilitate safe transport and storage. Verify that packing specifications are met to maintain product quality and safety standards. Transfer packages directly to the dispatch area or designated storage locations to support efficient logistics. Create storage locations for packages when direct transfer to dispatch is not possible, ensuring organized inventory management. Scan completed assembly orders into the in-house software system to maintain accurate production records. Use basic software tools on the job to support packaging and inventory processes. Collaborate effectively with team members and demonstrate willingness to learn new tasks and procedures. Required competencies and certifications Basic English proficiency in spoken and written communication to understand instructions and document tasks. No prior experience required; training will be provided to develop necessary skills and knowledge. Benefits 14 days of Annual Leave. Additional plant closure days on selected public holiday eves and post-holiday dates (e.g. Christmas Eve, Deepavali Eve, Hari Raya Haji Eve, New Year’s Eve, and Chinese New Year Eve). Note: ½ day of annual leave will be deducted for each full day of plant closure. Annual Bonus Structure: - Guaranteed 1-month annual bonus (contractual, similar to AWS). - Performance-based variable bonus (non-guaranteed). Monthly meal allowance of SGD80. Annual health screening. Medical, dental, and hospitalisation coverage. Mandatory training hours provided for all employees to support continuous development.

Analyze device performance and reliability through bench testing and data collection. Work with cross-functional teams, including the modeling team, to satisfy customer needs and develop advanced device models. Job Requirement: Min Degree in Physics or Electrical Engineering. Min 3 year of expereince in TCAD (Technology Computer-Aided Design) tools for simulation and calibration. Experience with device layout, design rule checks, and test structure generation. Knowledge of BCD process development and device characterization techniques. title: Automation Engineer / Specialist (Python / Entry level / Banking Domain)
description: Executive Summary Smart IMS Inc provides Digital technology & Cloud transformation services, Application & Infrastructure Management Services, Unified Communications, and Insurance implementation services to customers across the Americas, Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific regions. As the trusted technology and business partner of leading MNCs, including Global Investment Banks, Smart IMS is also a Microsoft Gold Certified Partner, Oracle Platinum Partner and AWS MSP Partner. We are looking to hire a motivated Automation Engineer / Specialist in Singapore to support our client, a top global investment bank. You will be responsible for developing, testing, and maintaining automation solutions to enhance efficiency, reduce manual efforts, and improve system performance. Responsibilities: Develop and implement automation using programming or scripting (such as Python) or other relevant technologies. Collaborate with teams to identify automation opportunities in workflows, systems, and processes. Design, test, and maintain automation frameworks for software, infrastructure, or operational tasks. Troubleshoot and resolve automation-related issues to ensure seamless execution. Document automation processes, best practices, and technical solutions for future reference. Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field. Knowledge of any automation programming or scripting language (such as Python) Past relevant internship or work experience in programming or scripting will be highly advantageous Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a passion for automation. Ability to work independently and in a team-oriented environment. Eagerness to learn and apply new technologies in automation and system optimization.

title: Technician / Service Engineer
description:
Location: Tukang,
Singapore (West) Only candidates with the legal right to work in Singapore will be
considered. Please note that applicants without this eligibility will not be
considered. Why join
Rolls-Royce? At Rolls-Royce we are proud to be a business
that has truly helped to shape the modern world and are committed to always
being a force for progress; powering, protecting and connecting people
everywhere. By joining Rolls-Royce, you'll have the
opportunity to work on world-class solutions, supported by a culture that
believes individuality is our greatest strength, and all perspectives,
experiences and backgrounds help us innovate and enable our high-performance
culture. Position Summary The Electrical/Electronic Service Engineer will be responsible for providing hands-on technical service and support for
mtu electrical and electronic systems and associated equipment. This includes
commissioning, installation, troubleshooting, maintenance, and repair works at
both customer sites and in-house facilities. The role requires a high level of
technical competence, problem-solving skills, and the ability to work
independently in dynamic environments. What you will be doing: ·        
Independently undertake assignments including: Commissioning, installation,
      maintenance, troubleshooting, repair and overhaul of mtu
      electrical/electronic products and associated systems either at customer
      sites or in the company’s workshop. Ensuring all work is performed in
      compliance with manufacturer manuals, technical specifications, and
      specific work instructions. ·        
Resolve technical issues efficiently and effectively, ensuring services
are completed as per work orders and accepted by customers. ·        
Perform functional testing and carry out necessary repairs or
adjustments on electronic equipment and control systems. ·        
Conduct acceptance tests together with customers or their
representatives; provide expert recommendations on repair work if required. ·        
Prepare and submit detailed service or investigation reports, travel
reports, and job completion documentation in a timely manner. ·        
Ensure compliance with all safety and health regulations under WSHA and
company WSH guidelines during on-site and workshop activities. ·        
Support continuous improvement of service processes and contribute to a
culture of operational excellence. ·        
Carry out any other ad hoc tasks assigned by the Manager or Department
Head. Position
requirements: ·        
Degree or Diploma in Electrical or Electronic Engineering. ·        
Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience in a service or
maintenance engineering environment, preferably within power systems or
industrial equipment sectors. Preferred
qualifications: ·        
Prior experience with mtu engines or power generation systems . ·        
Knowledge in hybrid, electrified, or digitalized power solutions. ·        
Team leadership or mentoring experience. ·        
WSQ/NEBOSH safety certifications or equivalent. About mtu Power Systems Power
Systems is the Rolls-Royce business which provides world-class power solutions
and complete life-cycle support under our product and solution brand mtu .
Through digitalization and electrification, we strive to develop drive and
power generation solutions that provide answers to the challenges posed by the
rapidly growing societal demands for energy and mobility. We
deliver and service comprehensive, powerful and reliable systems, based on both
gas and diesel engines, as well as electrified hybrid systems. These
technologically advanced solutions serve our customers in the marine and
infrastructure sectors worldwide. Our vision
is to ensure that the excellence and ingenuity that shaped our history
continues into our future. Our multi-year transformation programme aims to turn
Rolls-Royce into a high-performing, competitive, resilient and growing company.
Join us, and it can be your future vision too. Rolls-Royce
is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive
consideration for employment without regard to any protected characteristics. Relocation assistance
is not available for this position.

title: BIM Modeller
description: We are seeking a detail-oriented M&E Drafter to prepare and manage technical drawings for mechanical and electrical (M&E) systems. The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of M&E systems, proficiency in drafting software, and a commitment to accuracy and efficiency. Duty & Responsibilities Drafting and Documentation: · Prepare detailed technical drawings for mechanical and electrical systems based on project specifications. · Ensure drawings are accurate, updated, and adhere to company and regulatory standards. · Coordinate with the project team to ensure drawings reflect the design intent. Collaboration and Coordination: · Work closely with engineers, project managers, and contractors to gather necessary inputs for drafting. · Review and revise drawings based on feedback from stakeholders. · Ensure timely submission of shop drawings, as-built drawings, and other required documentation. Technical Support: · Assist in resolving technical issues related to M&E drawings during project execution. · Provide technical advice and recommendations to ensure design feasibility. Compliance and Quality Control: · Ensure all drawings comply with relevant codes, standards, and project requirements. Conduct quality checks to verify the accuracy and completeness of drawings. Requirements · Qualifications: Diploma or Certificate in Mechanical/Electrical Engineering or a related field. · Experience: Minimum of 2 years of experience in M&E drafting. · Skills: o Proficiency in AutoCAD and other drafting software. o Strong understanding of M&E systems and construction processes. o Attention to detail and ability to interpret technical specifications. o Good communication and coordination skills. description: Nova Singapore is looking for a Field Service Engineer to support our business and drive its growth. As a Field Service Engineer, you will play a pivotal role, contributing to the overall success of Nova. This position offers an exciting opportunity for individuals who are passionate about Semiconductor and are eager to make a meaningful impact through the role. Roles & Responsibilities 1. Implement the Customer Support Policy for the OEM End User. Supervise, maintain and support company systems in the field. 2. Support OEM partners, and End Users internal service as defined under warranty and service support agreements. 3. Maintain, support, and guide proper training to all users, including OEM and End User personnel. (Training will be provided, in most cases, by an engineer dedicated to training). 4. Maintain proper reporting methods for all activities of customer support as defined by the Territory Service Manager/Customer Support Manager. 5. Maintain company’s parts inventory under the position’s control. Maintain proper level of stock in accordance with company spare parts tock policy, as set from time to time. 6. Assist in new equipment integration, “add-ons,” and upgrades. 7. Provide feedback to the headquarters based on experience gained with customers. 8. Support the customer beyond the defined working schedule as required. 9. Other tasks deemed necessary by the Management. Requirements 1. Bachelor’s degree related to Semiconductor / Engineering industry. 2. Prior experience in semiconductor industry would be an advantage. 3. Good communication skills, team player, service orientate. 4. Able to accept work shift and oversea support if need. 5. Proficient in English. 6. Fresh graduates are welcome Life at Nova: Enjoy paid annual vacation and sick days, as well as allowance for transportation and spot bonus. Get recognition of your special occasions and enjoy diverse holiday gifts and fun swag. Employee Insurance (Outpatient Medical Plan, Hospitalization & Surgical Plan, Critical Illness Plan, Accidental Plan, Life Insurance Plan) Take advantage of training and learning programs to further develop as a professional. Get to know us better Nova (NASDAQ: NVMI) is a global company and a leading provider of innovative metrology and process control solutions for semiconductor manufacturing. With approximately 1,400 employees, three R&D centers and 31 sites worldwide, we provide valuable insights into the world’s most advanced industry. We leverage a variety of technological fields, including physics, optics, chemistry, and algorithms, to develop hardware and software solutions that measure nearly every advanced computer chip built in the world. Nova dives deep into the atomic level, extracting unique insights using dimensional, materials and chemical metrology, providing our customers with crucial decision-making data, and addressing critical challenges by offering differentiated solutions. Our outstanding, people-focused, and innovative culture means that every individual at Nova has the opportunity to make a significant impact and redefine the boundaries of what can be achieved through technology. Sentronics Metrology GmbH (a Nova Company) , acquired by Nova, is a leading metrology company specializing in high-precision measurement solutions for semiconductor manufacturing. Based in Mannheim, Sentronics brings over 25 years of expertise in developing cutting-edge metrology systems. The company’s integration into Nova is already underway, and it is now an integral part of the Nova family, sharing the same values of innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction. The combination of Nova's global reach and Sentronics' advanced technology and specialized knowledge creates a unique platform for growth and innovation. As part of Nova, Sentronics will fully benefit from the exciting opportunities in the rapidly evolving semiconductor industry. Join us if you are A dreamer and brilliant high aimer who sees the impossible as the starting point and loves working in a multidisciplinary global team to create innovative breakthroughs.

title: AI Finance / Accounting Trainer (Freelance/Part Time)
description: Position Overview: WLP Academy is looking for a skilled and experienced Accounting/Finance Trainer to join our team. The Trainer will play a key role in providing top-notch training, ensuring adult learners achieve effective learning outcomes and assessments. This role demands a strong commitment to upholding assessment integrity while contributing to a positive and engaging learning environment. The ideal candidate will be passionate about teaching and dedicated to promoting a culture of excellence. Training Delivery: •	Participate in curriculum improvements and deliver course content in alignment with WLP’s curriculum and learning standards. •	Design and develop AI training content, including both core/beginner and advanced levels. •	Prompt Engineering for Finance: Teach learners how to construct precise prompts to generate financial summaries, draft overdue payment emails, and interpret P&L variances using LLMs (Large Language Models). •	Excel & AI Integration: Guide learners on using AI features within spreadsheets (e.g., Copilot for Excel) to clean data and generate formulas automatically. •	Facilitate manual and digital assessments to evaluate and track learner performance and progress. •	Ensure learners meet the specified course learning objectives through engaging and effective teaching methods. •	Conduct engaging, practical training sessions (in-person)making complex AI tools accessible and relevant to learners. •	Adapt delivery style to suit different audience types (e.g., professionals, entrepreneurs, junior staff). •	Encourage participant interaction, learning, and real-world application. •	Provide practical exercises and simulations in financial statements analysis Assessment Integrity: •	Uphold the highest standards of assessment integrity, ensuring that assessments follow WSQ guidelines and internal policies. •	Ensure that all assessment questions are marked, and learners' answers are accurately evaluated. •	Verify that all questions are answered and attempted by learners. Incomplete responses should not be marked as “pass.” •	Conduct and properly document oral recovery sessions, ensuring transparency and consistency. •	Prevent instances of “flashing” answers and address any behaviors that compromise assessment integrity. •	Ensure incorrect answers are not marked as "pass"; only correct answers aligned with the assessment criteria should be considered. Administrative Responsibilities: •	Complete and submit Assessment Summary Records (ASRs) and other required documentation in a timely manner. •	Maintain accurate records of attendance, assessments, and learner-related data. •	Ensure that all training materials remain up-to-date and relevant in accordance with industry standards. Learner Support and Engagement: •	Provide constructive feedback on assessments and offer guidance for learners to improve. •	Address learner concerns professionally, promoting a supportive and positive learning environment. •	Foster inclusivity and respect in the classroom, ensuring equal learning opportunities for all students. Requirements •	ACLP (Advanced Certificate in Learning and Performance) or ACTA (Advanced Certificate in Training and Assessment) certification is required. •	Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. •	Prior experience teaching (preferably 3 years) or training adult learners in AI / digital technology. •	Professional certification (e.g., CA, ACCA, CPA) is a plus. •	At least 3–5 years of practical accounting experience. •	Strong presentation, facilitation, and interpersonal skills. •	Prior training or teaching experience is highly desirable. •	Ability to simplify complex financial concepts for diverse learners.

title: Safety & Decarbonisation Engineer / Coordinator
description: Role Purpose The Safety & Decarbonisation Engineer / Coordinator is responsible for ensuring workplace safety compliance while driving the organisation’s decarbonisation and sustainability initiatives. This role supports regulatory compliance, risk reduction, carbon emissions management, and capability building across facilities and operations. Key Responsibilities 1. Safety Management Implement and maintain Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) policies, procedures, and safe work practices. Conduct risk assessments, hazard identification, and job safety analyses for operational and project activities. Lead accident/incident investigations, root cause analysis, and corrective/preventive actions. Coordinate safety inspections, audits, and toolbox meetings. Ensure compliance with local regulations (e.g. WSH Act, subsidiary legislation, MOM requirements). Liaise with contractors, vendors, and internal teams to enforce safety standards on-site. Maintain safety documentation, permits, and records. 2. Decarbonisation & Sustainability Support implementation of decarbonisation initiatives aligned with company sustainability goals. Collect, manage, and analyse data for Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions (and Scope 3 where applicable). Support carbon baseline establishment and monitoring of emissions performance. Identify energy efficiency and carbon reduction opportunities (e.g. equipment, processes, utilities). Coordinate participation in sustainability programmes (e.g. NTUC SCDP, government-funded initiatives). Work with consultants and internal stakeholders to set science-aligned targets and track progress. Prepare sustainability-related reports, dashboards, and management updates. 3. Training & Capability Building Coordinate safety and sustainability training for employees and contractors. Use online and external programmes to upskill facilities engineers and operational staff. Promote safety culture and sustainability awareness across the organisation. 4. Coordination & Reporting Act as a point of contact for safety and decarbonisation matters. Coordinate with Facilities, Operations, Engineering, and Management teams. Support audits, inspections, and regulatory submissions. Maintain accurate records, KPIs, and compliance documentation. Qualifications & Experience Diploma / Degree in Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Sustainability, Safety, or related field. At least 2–5 years of relevant experience in safety, facilities, engineering, or sustainability roles (depending on seniority). Familiarity with WSH regulations and safety management systems. Basic understanding of carbon accounting, energy management, or ESG principles is preferred. Experience in industrial, warehouse, cold room, or manufacturing environments is an advantage. Skills & Competencies Strong coordination and communication skills. Analytical mindset with ability to work with data and reports. Detail-oriented and systematic in documentation and follow-up. Able to engage both technical teams and management. Proactive, organized, and able to manage multiple initiatives. Preferred Certifications (Optional) WSQ Advanced Certificate in WSH / Safety Officer certification Energy Efficiency / Sustainability-related certificates Carbon accounting or ESG-related training

description: Pickering Global Campus is the online learning platform, part of the SagePaths Group. We deliver accessible, high-quality academic and professional programmes designed for learners across Asia and beyond. As we continue expanding our digital education offerings, we are building a dedicated team to drive online growth, brand visibility, and student acquisition. This role is ideal for an individual who thrives in a start-up-style environment—resourceful, hands-on, adaptable—yet appreciates the structure and support of a well-established organisation. If you want to play a pivotal role in shaping a new digital learning initiative, this is your opportunity. Role Brief We are seeking a dynamic and ambitious Online Education Marketing Specialist to lead Pickering Global Campus’ digital presence, growth initiatives, and student recruitment strategy. You will design and execute marketing campaigns from scratch, experiment with new tactics, analyse performance, and directly influence PGC’s overall marketing roadmap. This role requires creativity, data-driven thinking, and ownership of the full digital funnel. Key Responsibilities Digital Strategy & Campaign Execution Develop and execute digital marketing strategies to grow brand awareness, leads, and online enrolments. Create multi-channel content and campaigns (email, social media, paid ads, landing pages) tailored to target audiences. Maximise use of CRM and automation tools (e.g., HubSpot/Brevo) for email marketing and behavioural journeys. Growth & Performance Marketing Run growth experiments such as A/B testing, landing page optimisation, and lead magnets to achieve rapid, measurable results. Monitor and analyse campaign performance using Google Analytics, HubSpot, and internal dashboards. Adapt strategies based on data insights to continually improve ROI and conversion rates. Content, Brand & Web Manage website content and ensure alignment with PGC’s brand direction. Collaborate with internal teams and freelancers (design, content, sales) to ensure cohesive and impactful messaging. Guide social media content planning alongside Brands & Communications for consistent digital presence. Market Research & Strategy Development Conduct market and competitor research to identify new opportunities and emerging trends. Contribute to shaping PGC’s marketing roadmap and prioritise high-impact initiatives. Support the development of new programmes by providing audience insights and demand analysis. Agile Collaboration Work closely with Product, Tech, and Sales teams to ensure marketing initiatives support the learner journey and platform features. Adjust campaigns quickly in response to performance data and operational needs. Take initiative and operate independently in a fast-paced, start-up-like environment. Requirements Minimum Bachelor degree with 2+ years of experience in digital marketing, growth marketing, or performance marketing. Strong understanding of digital fundamentals: SEO, PPC, social media, content marketing, email marketing, and funnel optimisation. Comfortable with analytics tools (Google Analytics, HubSpot, Meta Ads Manager, etc.). Creative problem-solver with a willingness to test new ideas and iterate rapidly. Excellent written and verbal communication skills; attention to detail is essential. Ability to work independently and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial environment. Advantageous: Experience building/launching new online products, start-up experience, EdTech or education industry exposure.

title: M&E Engineer
description: World Health SafetyTec (WHST) is a Singapore-based company providing EHS Consultation, Biological Indicator (BI) sterility testing, bizSAFE 3 certification support, and outsourcing of Safety & Medical Personnel. We partner with clients and businesses to strengthen workplace safety, regulatory compliance, and operational excellence. For this position, selected candidate will be stationed onsite (Tuas) with a client partner as part of our outsourced services. This key role to provide technical support and perform preventive and corrective maintenance for all mechanical, electrical and building systems to ensure the facility operates safely, efficiently, and in compliance with applicable standards. Key Responsibilities: 1. Maintenance & Repairs i. Perform routine preventive maintenance on HVAC, plumbing, lighting, fire protection and electrical systems. ii. Troubleshoot and repair electrical circuits, panels, motors, pumps and other building equipment. iii. iv. Carry out minor plumbing, air-conditioning and carpentry repairs. Assist with lift, generator and UPS system inspections and servicing. 2. Monitoring & Operations i. Monitor Building Management System (BMS) systems for alerts and anomalies. ii. iii. Respond promptly to alarms, power failures or mechanical system issues. Operate equipment safely and ensure all readings and conditions are recorded accurately. 3. Compliance & Documentation i. Ensure compliance with safety regulations and internal SOPs. ii. Maintain logs, checklists and maintenance records. iii. Support audits and inspections by authorities or internal stakeholders. 4. Coordination i. Coordinate with vendors or contractors for specialized repair or contract services work. ii. Support facilities team during shutdowns, renovations and new installations. iii. Liaise with tenants or building occupants to minimize disruption during maintenance work. 5. Emergency Support i. Provide emergency response for M&E failures or breakdowns. ii. Participate in 24/7 duty roster or standby support if required. Qualification & Experience Requirements: 1 Education & Experience: ITE, Higher ITE, or Diploma in Mechanical / Electrical / Building Services Engineering or related field. 2 Key Skills & Attributes: i. At least 2 years of relevant experience in building maintenance, facilities management, or a similar role. ii. Hands-on experience with HVAC, electrical systems, or plumbing is highly preferred 3 Skills & Competencies i. Basic knowledge of M&E drawings, schematics, and technical manuals. ii. Familiarity with CMMS (Computerized Maintenance Management System) is an advantage. iii. iv. Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail. Physically fit and able to work at heights or in confined spaces, when required. 4 Other Requirements i. Willingness to work shifts, weekends or after-hours as needed. ii. Valid electrical license (e.g Licensed Electrical Worker - LEW) is a plus. Please include your current and expected salary in your resume. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your understanding.

Required Experience and Skills:- • Bachelor’s degree in engineering, Computer Science, Project Management, or a related discipline. • Proven experience as a Project Engineer or Project Coordinator in technology or system migration projects. • Relevant project management or project experience in the area of Automatic Fare Collection system in Singapore transit environment will be a plus. • Proficient in Microsoft Office for Windows. • Strong organisational and time management skills with ability to work independently. • Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills. • Organizational, leadership, analytical, problem-solving and decision-making skills. • Ability to work under pressure, manage competing priorities, and adapt to changing requirements.

Key Responsibilities • Support the development of next-generation electrical systems and infrastructure. • Collaborate with engineering teams to integrate new technologies into product development. • Produce system-level concepts, schematic designs, and detailed electrical documentation. • Lead cross-functional stakeholders (design, construction, facilities, and EHS) on programme-level deliverables and commissioning activities. • Manage external vendors providing design and construction services across the product lifecycle. • Prepare and maintain technical documentation, including statements of work, engineering reports, design documents, cost analyses, budgets, schedules, and commissioning plans. • Coordinate and consolidate design documentation across all disciplines for large-scale data centre builds. • Review design drawings and Autodesk Revit models, ensuring accuracy, milestone compliance, and resolution of critical coordination issues. • Develop proprietary electrical design standards and data centre products. • Implement automation tools and workflows to streamline production and reduce design cycle times. • Identify and escalate technical and programme issues, develop solutions, and drive approved actions to completion. description: We are seeking a proactive and experienced Safety Officer cum Environmental Control Officer (ECO) to ensure a safe and environmentally compliant work environment. The ideal candidate will be responsible for overseeing safety protocols, monitoring environmental regulations, and ensuring compliance with both workplace safety standards and environmental practices. Educational Qualifications: A degree or diploma in Occupational Health and Safety, Environmental Science, or a related field. Additional certifications in safety or environmental management (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISO 14001, OSHA) are preferred. Experience: Minimum of 2-3 years of experience in a safety officer or environmental control officer role, ideally within an industrial, manufacturing, or construction setting. Experience in conducting safety audits, inspections, and environmental assessments. Skills and Competencies: Strong knowledge of health, safety, and environmental regulations and best practices. Proficiency in risk assessment and the implementation of safety management systems. Strong organizational and leadership skills with the ability to enforce safety protocols and maintain a culture of safety. Good communication skills, both verbal and written, for training, reporting, and liaising with various stakeholders. Ability to work under pressure and in potentially hazardous environments. Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). Personal Attributes: Detail-oriented and capable of identifying safety and environmental hazards quickly. Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to suggest and implement corrective actions. A team player with a proactive attitude and commitment to fostering a safe and sustainable workplace. Ability to work independently and demonstrate initiative in handling safety and environmental challenges. Additional Requirements: Certification in First Aid/CPR would be an advantage. Knowledge of waste management systems, emergency response planning, and environmental impact assessments. Educational Qualifications:A degree or diploma in Occupational Health and Safety, Environmental Science, or a related field. Additional certifications in safety or environmental management (e.g., NEBOSH, IOSH, ISO 14001, OSHA) are preferred.

title: Senior Product Development Engineer (ATE)
description: THE ROLE: We are a growing Product and Test Engineering team who manages critical and strategic New Product Introduction (NPI) and sustaining to support the Embedded Business Unit. We do exciting product development based on leading-edge technology that will power the next generation processors used for automotive, high-end networking and storage, industrial, aerospace and defense. If you are looking for a fulfilling and challenging career as an engineer in NPI ATE Product Development, you are in the right place! As a Senior ATE Product Development Engineer, you will be responsible for developing and driving ATE test solutions to meet NPI business milestones and KPI metrics (test time, yield, cost, quality). The expectation is to perform technical leadership through interaction with DFT and CT IP teams, Product & Test Engineering teams, Quality & Reliability teams, software & platform teams to come up with the best solution that balances quality and cost. You will experience working with and learning from AMD’s talented engineering teams around the world. THE PERSON: We are looking for someone with strong technical, analytical and problem-solving skills, as well as demonstrating the characteristics of an independent and resourceful individual. The applicant must have a proficient understanding of DFT(Design-For-Test) architecture for SOC (System-On-Chip) product, a strong test development, Silicon characterization/debug working experience with various industry standard ATE test platforms, as well as data analysis. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES: Accountable for driving test solution/coverage development, optimization to meet business milestones: schedule, test time, yield, cost and quality. Pre-Silicon engagement with Design, test solution/methodology scoping, planning & validation Post Silicon Bring up of test patterns and calibration enablement leading to optimization for mass production enablement Optimization of test coverage and flows for increased quality, yield, cost improvement, and test time reduction Conduct engineering evaluations and analysis to drive closure of technical issues during NPI and sustaining phases Solves complex, novel, and non-recurring problems; initiate significant changes to existing processes/methods and leads development and implementation Influences technical decisions that have a significant impact on final product Involves collaboration on or assuming the consultative or leadership responsibilities for a specific project or for product development initiatives Provide technical supervision or mentoring junior engineers Familiar with RMA debug is a plus. PREFERRED EXPERIENCE: Experience in ATE Test patterns, calibration test flows/method development, debug, test and characterization experience working in an NPI Product Engineering team. Hands-on experience in bringing up ATPG SCAN, MBIST, HSIO, Functional or Thermal content in ATE tests, preferably for products using latest silicon designs and advanced process technology nodes. Hands-on experience in ATE Test handling/ execution and development experience - Advantest V93K experience preferred. (Advantest SMT7 or SMT8 experience is a plus) Good understanding of RMA test program development, debug analysis and solution enablement related to test escapes. Software programming and scripting proficiency (C++, Java, Groovy, Perl, Ruby, Python) Experience with HVM (High Volume Manufacturing) is a strong plus Strong and effective presentation, written and verbal communication skills, and the ability to work with geographically distributed product engineering teams Excellent interpersonal, organizational, and analytical skills ACADEMIC CREDENTIALS: Degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, or comparable disciplines LOCATION: Singapore

title: Research Fellow (Aerospace Engineering/Transport/Mechanical Engineering/Computer Science)
description: Air Traffic Management Research Institute (ATMRI) is a research institute at Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore, supported by the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS). The key objective of ATMRI is to support research and innovation in air traffic management. ATMRI focuses on developing practical and data-driven solutions for complex aviation systems, in close collaboration with industry and international partners. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/atmri . We are looking for a Research Fellow to conduct research on air traffic management (ATM) algorithms and data-driven methods. The role will focus on developing intelligent methods and operation-support tools for ATM applications, contributing to the advancement of research and innovation at Nanyang Technological University. Key Responsibilities: Conduct research on methods and approaches to support air traffic management studies and applications. Develop, test, and evaluate analytical models, algorithms, and tools using operational and simulated data. Support research activities related to airspace and air traffic operations under current and future ATM concepts. Work collaboratively with researchers and engineers to develop prototype solutions and decision-support tools. Analyse results and assess the performance and applicability of developed methods in realistic operational scenarios. Prepare technical reports, research papers, and project deliverables, and participate in project meetings and reviews. Contribute to research proposals, technical documentation, and other project-related activities as required. Job Requirements: PhD qualification degree in Aerospace Engineering, Transport, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or other related fields. At least three (3) years of relevant research experience in air traffic management or related technical research roles. Familiarity with research related to air traffic management systems, airspace operations, or air traffic operations. Good written and oral communication skills with the ability to prepare technical reports and academic publications. Proficiency in job-specific technical skills, including programming for research and data analysis purposes (e.g. Python or similar languages), as well as strong numerical and analytical skills. Competent in general research-related skills and professional attributes, including problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work independently and collaboratively, develop solutions under defined timelines, and demonstrate good organisational and time management skills. The Role Responsibilities Lifecycle Strategy and Execution Develop and own the overall user lifecycle roadmap- from testing and experimentation to scaling automated lifecycle programmes Lead the programmatic transformation of lifecycle management in CMB: Brainstorm and suggest new ideas to motivate behaviour based on user psychology (eg. how to get guys to take better photos?) Triggers: actions requiring follow-up (e.g., abandoned onboarding, inactive users, incomplete purchases) ...via sales campaigns, in-app pushes, push notifications, and email experiences Data-Driven Optimisation Write and execute SQL queries identify key engagement and conversion drivers Set up experimentation frameworks (A/B tests, holdout groups) to measure impact Analyse results and translate insights into actionable improvements across CRM touchpoints Report and recommend on the improvement of lifecycle health according to engagement, retention, and revenue metrics Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with Marketing, Design, and Engineering to ensure seamless CRM execution and integration with product experiences Influence messaging logic, in-app surfaces, and technical infrastructure needed for lifecycle management scalability Our Ideal Candidate 0-3 years of managing a consumer-facing product as a Product Manager in a Technology company (open to fresh grads) Has worked in a day-to-day capacity with a scrum team building actual products Strong user empathy and analytical skills Can confidently write and execute their own SQL with no data team support (or, with other programming language knowledge, can prove to us that  they can learn) Is detail-oriented, willing and conscientious to execute on and QA operational lifecycle campaigns by him/ herself Has experience working with other functions (marketing, operations) to launch a product from end to end Is comfortable in a fast-paced environment interacting with global teams Ideally possess practical communication skills across English and Mandarin, both written and verbal to work with mandarin stakeholders. title: Electro-Technical Officer
description:
Responsible for the efficient maintenance, condition and upkeep of all electrical equipment, appliances, and fittings onboard under his charge. Keep day-to-day record of all work carried out and in connection with the electrical equipment, machinery, and
lighting onboard. Plan preventive maintenance of all items of work, submit the same to the Chief Engineer for approval and thereafter carry out repairs and maintenance. title: Cocktail Lab Assistant
description: Jigger & Pony Group is a leading hospitality company that owns and operates restaurants and bars in Singapore. A pioneer in Singapore’s cocktail bar scene, the Group’s portfolio comprises of six cocktail bars and two restaurants. Cocktail Bars: Jigger & Pony (No. 9 The World’s 50 Best Bars 2025, No. 3 Asia’s 50 Best Bars 2025) Gibson – Timeless cocktail bar balancing balances ingredient‑driven creativity with classics Live Twice – Cocktail bar inspired by mid-century modern Japan Pop City - Japanese-lifestyle cocktail bar in Singapore’s Central Business District Cosmo Pony - Convivial bar with a cosmopolitan soul in Jakarta BOP - Korean cocktail dining bar in Tanjong Pagar Restaurants: Humpback – oyster bar and seafood bistro with a focused wine & cocktail list Caffe Fernet – Modern Italian restaurant bar with a view of the Marina Bay waterfront Jigger & Pony Cocktail Lab Serves as the group’s central production hub, supporting our outlets through the preparation of pre-batched cocktails and large-volume accompaniments. The Lab ensures consistency, efficiency, and quality across all venues by handling advance preparations, allowing our bar teams to focus on service, execution, and guest experience. It plays a key role in maintaining standards and supporting smooth daily operations across the group. Role Overview: The Lab Assistant is responsible for daily preparation and production of high-quality ingredients and pre-mixed cocktails used across our beverage programs. This role supports day-to-day tasks, ensuring that mise en place and cocktail ingredients are prepared efficiently and according to standards. This position is ideal for individuals with a keen interest in mixology, food science, or culinary arts, and is an opportunity to be part of an innovative and collaborative environment that bridges bar craft with kitchen precision. Key Responsibilities: Assist in the preparation of ingredients, following recipes and procedures. Operate basic lab equipment under supervision (e.g., blending, infusion, carbonation). Maintain cleanliness and organization within the lab. Label and store products correctly. Ensure all lab equipment is properly maintained, functioning safely, and operated according to protocols. Conduct regular quality checks on products to ensure consistency in flavor and presentation. Collaborate with other departments (e.g., guest shifts, R&D) for special projects and product innovation. Requirements: Prior experience in a culinary or beverage production setting is advantageous Attention to detail Willingness to learn and take direction Comfortable working in a fast-paced, production-oriented environment with changing priorities. Ability to follow through and meet its deadlines and achieve its goals Pastry trainees may apply
